--- /dev/null
+++ b/scripts/capture-omlx-fixture.sh
@@ -0,0 +1,108 @@
+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+# capture-omlx-fixture.sh — regenerate a testdata/omlx-wire fixture from a
+# live omlx endpoint. Wraps `ddx agent run` with AGENT_DEBUG_WIRE_STREAM_FULL=1
+# (shipped in ddx-agent v0.3.14 — bead agent-f237e07b) and reshapes the wire
+# dump into the fixture JSONL format the consumer-side smoke test expects.
+#
+# Usage:
+#   scripts/capture-omlx-fixture.sh \
+#     --endpoint http://vidar:1235/v1 \
+#     --model    Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-4bit \
+#     --prompt   "Reply with the word ready." \
+#     --out      cli/internal/agent/testdata/omlx-wire/happy_path.jsonl
+#
+# Required tools: ddx (v0.6.0-alpha22+), jq.
+#
+# What the script does:
+#   1. Runs an isolated ddx agent invocation against the given omlx endpoint
+#      with AGENT_DEBUG_WIRE=1 AGENT_DEBUG_WIRE_STREAM_FULL=1 so the whole
+#      SSE stream is captured to a sidecar wire file.
+#   2. Extracts every `dir=="response"` body chunk in arrival order and emits
+#      one {"kind":"frame","body":...} record per chunk.
+#   3. Prepends a {"kind":"meta",...} record with the endpoint, model, prompt,
+#      and a user-supplied expected outcome so the replay test knows what to
+#      assert.
+#
+# Fixtures are checked in. Regenerate only when the omlx server changes wire
+# behavior (new frame type, header shift, chunk-size change) in a way the
+# existing fixtures no longer represent.
+
+set -euo pipefail
+
+endpoint=""
+model=""
+prompt=""
+out=""
+name=""
+status="success"
+
+while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
+  case "$1" in
+    --endpoint) endpoint="$2"; shift 2 ;;
+    --model)    model="$2"; shift 2 ;;
+    --prompt)   prompt="$2"; shift 2 ;;
+    --out)      out="$2"; shift 2 ;;
+    --name)     name="$2"; shift 2 ;;
+    --status)   status="$2"; shift 2 ;;  # success | error
+    -h|--help)
+      sed -n '2,28p' "$0"
+      exit 0
+      ;;
+    *) echo "unknown arg: $1" >&2; exit 2 ;;
+  esac
+done
+
+for v in endpoint model prompt out; do
+  if [[ -z "${!v}" ]]; then
+    echo "missing required --$v" >&2
+    exit 2
+  fi
+done
+
+command -v jq >/dev/null || { echo "jq is required" >&2; exit 2; }
+command -v ddx >/dev/null || { echo "ddx is required" >&2; exit 2; }
+
+wire_file="$(mktemp -t omlx-wire.XXXXXX.jsonl)"
+trap 'rm -f "$wire_file"' EXIT
+
+echo "capturing wire dump from $endpoint → $wire_file" >&2
+
+AGENT_DEBUG_WIRE=1 \
+AGENT_DEBUG_WIRE_STREAM_FULL=1 \
+AGENT_DEBUG_WIRE_FILE="$wire_file" \
+ddx agent run \
+  --harness agent \
+  --provider vidar-omlx \
+  --model "$model" \
+  --prompt-text "$prompt" \
+  --timeout 120s \
+  >/dev/null || {
+    echo "ddx agent run failed — fixture capture incomplete" >&2
+    echo "wire dump (may still be useful): $wire_file" >&2
+    exit 1
+  }
+
+[[ -z "$name" ]] && name="$(basename "$out" .jsonl)"
+
+tmp_out="$(mktemp -t omlx-fixture.XXXXXX.jsonl)"
+trap 'rm -f "$wire_file" "$tmp_out"' EXIT
+
+# Meta record — expected outcome is a hint for the replay test. Authors may
+# need to hand-edit content_contains / error_contains after inspecting the
+# captured body.
+jq -cn \
+  --arg name "$name" \
+  --arg desc "Captured $(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ) from $endpoint model=$model prompt=$prompt" \
+  --arg status "$status" \
+  '{kind:"meta", name:$name, description:$desc,
+    expected:{status:$status, error_must_not_contain:"unexpected end of JSON input"}}' \
+  >> "$tmp_out"
+
+# Frame records — every response chunk in capture order. AGENT_DEBUG_WIRE
+# with STREAM_FULL=1 emits one {"dir":"response","body":"…"} record per read.
+jq -c 'select(.dir=="response" and (.body|type=="string") and (.body|length>0))
+       | {kind:"frame", body:.body}' \
+  "$wire_file" >> "$tmp_out"
+
+mv "$tmp_out" "$out"
+echo "wrote $(wc -l < "$out") records to $out" >&2","is_error":false}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"51e05227-4794-4fdf-b297-f2720148e2a0","uuid":"ad85bb2f-b87a-4555-85d6-b2e11af2638d","timestamp":"2026-04-18T04:31:26.286Z","tool_use_result":{"stdout":"diff --git a/cli/internal/agent/omlx_e2e_test.go b/cli/internal/agent/omlx_e2e_test.go
new file mode 100644
index 00000000..3438eff3
--- /dev/null
+++ b/cli/internal/agent/omlx_e2e_test.go
@@ -0,0 +1,230 @@
+package agent
+
+import (
+	"bufio"
+	"encoding/json"
+	"io"
+	"net/http"
+	"net/http/httptest"
+	"os"
+	"path/filepath"
+	"strings"
+	"testing"
+	"time"
+
+	"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent/provider/openai"
+	"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
+	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
+)
+
+// omlxFixture is the parsed representation of a testdata/omlx-wire/*.jsonl file.
+// Each fixture captures a recorded vidar-omlx SSE response plus the outcome the
+// consumer must produce when it replays. Fixtures are authored either by hand
+// (the checked-in happy/error paths) or regenerated by
+// scripts/capture-omlx-fixture.sh against a live omlx endpoint.
+type omlxFixture struct {
+	Meta   omlxFixtureMeta
+	Frames []string // raw SSE bodies, written verbatim in order
+}
+
+type omlxFixtureMeta struct {
+	Name        string              `json:"name"`
+	Description string              `json:"description"`
+	Expected    omlxFixtureExpected `json:"expected"`
+}
+
+type omlxFixtureExpected struct {
+	// Status is "success" or "error".
+	Status string `json:"status"`
+	// ErrorMustNotContain is a substring that the stream error — if any — must
+	// not contain. This is the regression guard: "unexpected end of JSON input"
+	// is the exact signature of the v0.3.13 SSE comment-frame defect (bead
+	// agent-f237e07b) that v0.3.14's sseCommentFilter fixed.
+	ErrorMustNotContain string `json:"error_must_not_contain"`
+	// ContentContains is a substring the assembled content must contain on the
+	// happy path.
+	ContentContains string `json:"content_contains"`
+	// FinishReason is the expected finish reason on the happy path.
+	FinishReason string `json:"finish_reason"`
+	// ErrorContains is a substring the surfaced error must contain on the
+	// error path — asserts the error is propagated rather than swallowed.
+	ErrorContains string `json:"error_contains"`
+}
+
+// loadOMLXFixture reads a JSONL fixture from testdata/omlx-wire/. The first
+// line is the {"kind":"meta", ...} record; remaining {"kind":"frame","body":...}
+// lines are the SSE bodies to stream in order.
+func loadOMLXFixture(t *testing.T, path string) omlxFixture {
+	t.Helper()
+	f, err := os.Open(path) // #nosec G304 -- test fixtures are checked in.
+	require.NoError(t, err, "open fixture %s", path)
+	t.Cleanup(func() { _ = f.Close() })
+
+	var fix omlxFixture
+	scanner := bufio.NewScanner(f)
+	// Default 64KB scanner buffer is tight for long reasoning lines.
+	scanner.Buffer(make([]byte, 64*1024), 1024*1024)
+	first := true
+	for scanner.Scan() {
+		line := strings.TrimSpace(scanner.Text())
+		if line == "" {
+			continue
+		}
+		var record struct {
+			Kind string          `json:"kind"`
+			Body string          `json:"body"`
+			Raw  json.RawMessage `json:"-"`
+		}
+		if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(line), &record); err != nil {
+			t.Fatalf("parsing fixture line in %s: %v", path, err)
+		}
+		switch record.Kind {
+		case "meta":
+			if !first {
+				t.Fatalf("fixture %s: meta record must be the first non-empty line", path)
+			}
+			if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(line), &fix.Meta); err != nil {
+				t.Fatalf("parsing meta in %s: %v", path, err)
+			}
+		case "frame":
+			fix.Frames = append(fix.Frames, record.Body)
+		default:
+			t.Fatalf("fixture %s: unknown record kind %q", path, record.Kind)
+		}
+		first = false
+	}
+	require.NoError(t, scanner.Err(), "scanning fixture %s", path)
+	require.NotEmpty(t, fix.Meta.Name, "fixture %s: missing meta.name", path)
+	require.NotEmpty(t, fix.Frames, "fixture %s: no frame records", path)
+	return fix
+}
+
+// newOMLXReplayServer stands up an httptest.Server that behaves like a vidar-omlx
+// /v1/chat/completions endpoint by replaying the fixture's frames verbatim,
+// with a short inter-frame flush so the consumer's SSE decoder sees per-chunk
+// arrivals rather than one batched blob.
+func newOMLXReplayServer(t *testing.T, frames []string) *httptest.Server {
+	t.Helper()
+	srv := httptest.NewServer(http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
+		// Match omlx's advertised Content-Type exactly — the SSE comment-frame
+		// middleware in provider/openai activates only when Content-Type starts
+		// with text/event-stream.
+		w.Header().Set("Content-Type", "text/event-stream; charset=utf-8")
+		w.Header().Set("Cache-Control", "no-cache")
+		w.Header().Set("Transfer-Encoding", "chunked")
+		flusher, _ := w.(http.Flusher)
+		for _, body := range frames {
+			if _, err := io.WriteString(w, body); err != nil {
+				return
+			}
+			if flusher != nil {
+				flusher.Flush()
+			}
+			// Small delay to reproduce per-frame arrival; long enough that the
+			// decoder cannot batch frames, short enough that the test stays
+			// under a second total.
+			time.Sleep(5 * time.Millisecond)
+		}
+	}))
+	t.Cleanup(srv.Close)
+	return srv
+}
+
+// TestOMLXFixtureReplay_ConsumerEndToEnd drives a full ddx work orchestration
+// path (Runner.RunAgent → agentlib.Run → openai.Provider.ChatStream) against an
+// httptest double that replays recorded omlx SSE wire traces. This is the
+// consumer-side smoke test for ddx-agent v0.3.14 — see bead ddx-bbb2d177.
+//
+// What this guards against:
+//
+//  1. A future ddx-agent bump reintroducing the SSE comment-frame bug (bead
+//     agent-f237e07b): the happy-path fixture includes `: keep-alive\n\n`
+//     frames between data frames and must NOT surface "unexpected end of
+//     JSON input".
+//  2. A DDx-side change breaking the omlx dispatch path (e.g., a header or
+//     request-shape change that trips omlx's parser).
+//  3. A silent-success regression where the stream is consumed without error
+//     but the assembled content is empty or malformed — the happy-path assertion
+//     pins both the final content and finish_reason.
+//  4. Error-path regressions where a real model-side failure is swallowed by
+//     the consumer — the error-path fixture emits a malformed data frame and
+//     asserts the consumer surfaces the error with a specific signature (and
+//     specifically NOT "unexpected end of JSON input", which would mean the
+//     SSE filter regressed).
+func TestOMLXFixtureReplay_ConsumerEndToEnd(t *testing.T) {
+	fixtureDir := filepath.Join("testdata", "omlx-wire")
+	cases := []string{"happy_path.jsonl", "error_path.jsonl"}
+
+	for _, name := range cases {
+		name := name
+		t.Run(name, func(t *testing.T) {
+			fix := loadOMLXFixture(t, filepath.Join(fixtureDir, name))
+
+			srv := newOMLXReplayServer(t, fix.Frames)
+
+			provider := openai.New(openai.Config{
+				BaseURL: srv.URL + "/v1",
+				APIKey:  "test",
+				Model:   "Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-4bit",
+				// Flavor "omlx" pins the capability table to the one used in
+				// production for vidar-omlx routing — matches the real
+				// dispatch path.
+				Flavor: "omlx",
+			})
+
+			r := NewRunner(Config{SessionLogDir: t.TempDir(), TimeoutMS: 10000})
+			r.LookPath = mockLookPath
+			r.AgentProvider = provider
+
+			// A WorkDir prevents the agent from using the live worktree when
+			// it invokes its built-in read/write/bash tools during the loop.
+			result, err := r.RunAgent(RunOptions{
+				Harness: "agent",
+				Prompt:  "smoke test — omlx fixture replay",
+				WorkDir: t.TempDir(),
+			})
+			require.NoError(t, err, "RunAgent must not return a plumbing error; failures are reported via result.ExitCode / result.Error")
+
+			// The regression signature is always disallowed — on BOTH paths.
+			// v0.3.13 would surface this even for well-formed streams when
+			// keep-alive comment frames were present.
+			if fix.Meta.Expected.ErrorMustNotContain != "" {
+				assert.NotContains(t, result.Error, fix.Meta.Expected.ErrorMustNotContain,
+					"consumer surfaced the v0.3.13 SSE comment-frame regression signature — the sseCommentFilter in ddx-agent v0.3.14+ is missing or broken")
+			}
+
+			switch fix.Meta.Expected.Status {
+			case "success":
+				assert.Equal(t, 0, result.ExitCode, "happy-path fixture must close cleanly; got error=%q", result.Error)
+				assert.Empty(t, result.Error, "happy-path must produce no error")
+				if want := fix.Meta.Expected.ContentContains; want != "" {
+					assert.Contains(t, result.Output, want,
+						"happy-path must assemble content from post-keep-alive data frames")
+				}
+			case "error":
+				assert.Equal(t, 1, result.ExitCode, "error-path fixture must report failure via ExitCode=1")
+				assert.NotEmpty(t, result.Error, "error-path must surface a non-empty error")
+				if want := fix.Meta.Expected.ErrorContains; want != "" {
+					assert.Contains(t, result.Error, want,
+						"error-path must propagate the underlying parse error")
+				}
+			default:
+				t.Fatalf("fixture %s: unknown expected status %q", name, fix.Meta.Expected.Status)
+			}
+		})
+	}
+}
+
+// TestOMLXFixtureReplay_FixturesExist is a cheap guardrail that fails fast if
+// the testdata/omlx-wire directory is emptied or renamed. The full replay test
+// above would also fail in that case, but a dedicated assertion surfaces the
+// structural problem without the noise of a streaming-test stack trace.
+func TestOMLXFixtureReplay_FixturesExist(t *testing.T) {
+	for _, name := range []string{"happy_path.jsonl", "error_path.jsonl"} {
+		p := filepath.Join("testdata", "omlx-wire", name)
+		info, err := os.Stat(p)
+		require.NoError(t, err, "missing fixture %s", p)
+		assert.False(t, info.IsDir(), "%s must be a file", p)
+		assert.NotZero(t, info.Size(), "%s must not be empty", p)
+	}
+}

new file mode 100644
index 00000000..fd43c76e

+++ b/cli/internal/agent/omlx_smoke_test.go
@@ -0,0 +1,66 @@
+//go:build omlx_smoke
+
+// Opt-in live smoke test for the ddx work → ddx-agent → vidar-omlx consumer
+// path. Unlike the fixture-replay test in omlx_e2e_test.go (which ships in CI
+// and uses a recorded test double), this test hits a real omlx endpoint and is
+// intended for manual release verification against a live server. It is gated
+// by the `omlx_smoke` build tag so the default `go test ./...` never runs it.
+//
+// Run:
+//
+//	OMLX_SMOKE_URL=http://vidar:1235/v1\
+//	OMLX_SMOKE_MODEL=Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-4bit\
+//	go test -tags omlx_smoke -run TestOMLXLiveSmoke -v ./cli/internal/agent
+//
+// This covers the same happy-path shape the CI fixture does, but against a
+// real server — useful for catching provider-side regressions (the omlx
+// server ships a breaking change, HTTP stack differences, headers, etc.) that
+// a recorded fixture cannot detect.
+
+package agent
+
+import (
+	"os"
+	"testing"
+
+	"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent/provider/openai"
+	"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
+	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
+)
+
+func TestOMLXLiveSmoke(t *testing.T) {
+	baseURL := os.Getenv("OMLX_SMOKE_URL")
+	if baseURL == "" {
+		t.Skip("OMLX_SMOKE_URL not set — live smoke requires an omlx endpoint")
+	}
+	model := os.Getenv("OMLX_SMOKE_MODEL")
+	if model == "" {
+		t.Fatal("OMLX_SMOKE_MODEL is required when OMLX_SMOKE_URL is set")
+	}
+
+	provider := openai.New(openai.Config{
+		BaseURL: baseURL,
+		APIKey:  os.Getenv("OMLX_SMOKE_API_KEY"),
+		Model:   model,
+		Flavor:  "omlx",
+	})
+
+	r := NewRunner(Config{SessionLogDir: t.TempDir(), TimeoutMS: 120000})
+	r.LookPath = mockLookPath
+	r.AgentProvider = provider
+
+	result, err := r.RunAgent(RunOptions{
+		Harness: "agent",
+		Prompt:  "Reply with the single word 'ready' and nothing else.",
+		WorkDir: t.TempDir(),
+	})
+	require.NoError(t, err)
+
+	// The regression signature is the precise error v0.3.13 surfaced when
+	// omlx emitted keep-alive SSE comment frames during reasoning warmup.
+	// Any bump that reintroduces it must be caught here before it ships.
+	assert.NotContains(t, result.Error, "unexpected end of JSON input",
+		"live omlx produced the v0.3.13 SSE comment-frame regression signature")
+	assert.Equal(t, 0, result.ExitCode, "live omlx smoke must close cleanly; error=%q", result.Error)
+	assert.NotEmpty(t, result.Output, "live omlx must return some content")
+}
diff --git a/cli/internal/agent/testdata/omlx-wire/README.md b/cli/internal/agent/testdata/omlx-wire/README.md
new file mode 100644
index 00000000..1f3cbdfa
--- /dev/null
+++ b/cli/internal/agent/testdata/omlx-wire/README.md
@@ -0,0 +1,61 @@
+# omlx SSE Wire Fixtures
+
+These fixtures drive `cli/internal/agent/omlx_e2e_test.go` — the consumer-side
+end-to-end smoke test that guards `ddx work` against regressions on the
+`ddx-agent → vidar-omlx` dispatch path. Context: bead **ddx-bbb2d177**, which
+was filed after v0.3.14 shipped the `sseCommentFilter` fix for bead
+**agent-f237e07b** (upstream).
+
+## File format
+
+Each fixture is a JSONL file with two record kinds:
+
+- First line: `{"kind":"meta", ...}` — describes the capture and the assertion
+  shape the replay test will enforce (expected status, substrings that must or
+  must not appear in the assembled content / error).
+- Remaining lines: `{"kind":"frame","body":"<raw SSE body>"}` — one frame per
+  record, written verbatim (including `\n\n` terminators) to the test HTTP
+  response in arrival order.
+
+Frames include both `: keep-alive` SSE comment frames (the exact shape that
+triggered the v0.3.13 "unexpected end of JSON input" regression) and normal
+`data: {...}` chunks.
+
+## When to regenerate
+
+Regenerate fixtures only when real omlx server behavior changes in a way the
+existing captures no longer represent — for example, a new reasoning-phase
+frame shape, a header shift, or a chunk-boundary change. Do NOT regenerate
+just to refresh timestamps.
+
+## How to regenerate
+
+Use `scripts/capture-omlx-fixture.sh`, which wraps `ddx agent run` with
+`AGENT_DEBUG_WIRE_STREAM_FULL=1` (shipped in ddx-agent v0.3.14) so the full
+stream is captured to a sidecar JSONL, then reshapes it into the fixture
+format:
+
+```bash
+scripts/capture-omlx-fixture.sh \
+  --endpoint http://vidar:1235/v1 \
+  --model    Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-4bit \
+  --prompt   "Reply with the word ready." \
+  --name     happy-path \
+  --status   success \
+  --out      cli/internal/agent/testdata/omlx-wire/happy_path.jsonl
+```
+
+After the script writes the fixture, hand-edit the `meta.expected` block to
+pin the `content_contains` / `error_contains` / `finish_reason` assertions for
+the capture you just took — the script cannot infer those from the wire
+bytes alone.
+
+## Why fixtures live here instead of the upstream agent repo
+
+The agent repo (`DocumentDrivenDX/agent`) has its own provider-level SSE
+tests in `provider/openai/openai_test.go`. Those guard the SDK's handling of
+SSE framing in isolation. The fixtures in *this* directory guard the DDx
+consumer — `ddx work` → `Runner.RunAgent` → `agentlib.Run` — against both
+SDK regressions (a bad ddx-agent bump) and DDx-side regressions (a header or
+request-shape change that trips omlx's parser). The two layers cover
+different failure modes and both matter.

index 00000000..164f9457
--- /dev/null
+++ b/scripts/capture-omlx-fixture.sh
@@ -0,0 +1,108 @@
+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+# capture-omlx-fixture.sh — regenerate a testdata/omlx-wire fixture from a
+# live omlx endpoint. Wraps `ddx agent run` with AGENT_DEBUG_WIRE_STREAM_FULL=1
+# (shipped in ddx-agent v0.3.14 — bead agent-f237e07b) and reshapes the wire
+# dump into the fixture JSONL format the consumer-side smoke test expects.
+#
+# Usage:
+#   scripts/capture-omlx-fixture.sh \
+#     --endpoint http://vidar:1235/v1 \
+#     --model    Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-4bit \
+#     --prompt   "Reply with the word ready." \
+#     --out      cli/internal/agent/testdata/omlx-wire/happy_path.jsonl
+#
+# Required tools: ddx (v0.6.0-alpha22+), jq.
+#
+# What the script does:
+#   1. Runs an isolated ddx agent invocation against the given omlx endpoint
+#      with AGENT_DEBUG_WIRE=1 AGENT_DEBUG_WIRE_STREAM_FULL=1 so the whole
+#      SSE stream is captured to a sidecar wire file.
+#   2. Extracts every `dir=="response"` body chunk in arrival order and emits
+#      one {"kind":"frame","body":...} record per chunk.
+#   3. Prepends a {"kind":"meta",...} record with the endpoint, model, prompt,
+#      and a user-supplied expected outcome so the replay test knows what to
+#      assert.
+#
+# Fixtures are checked in. Regenerate only when the omlx server changes wire
+# behavior (new frame type, header shift, chunk-size change) in a way the
+# existing fixtures no longer represent.
+
+set -euo pipefail
+
+endpoint=""
+model=""
+prompt=""
+out=""
+name=""
+status="success"
+
+while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
+  case "$1" in
+    --endpoint) endpoint="$2"; shift 2 ;;
+    --model)    model="$2"; shift 2 ;;
+    --prompt)   prompt="$2"; shift 2 ;;
+    --out)      out="$2"; shift 2 ;;
+    --name)     name="$2"; shift 2 ;;
+    --status)   status="$2"; shift 2 ;;  # success | error
+    -h|--help)
+      sed -n '2,28p' "$0"
+      exit 0
+      ;;
+    *) echo "unknown arg: $1" >&2; exit 2 ;;
+  esac
+done
+
+for v in endpoint model prompt out; do
+  if [[ -z "${!v}" ]]; then
+    echo "missing required --$v" >&2
+    exit 2
+  fi
+done
+
+command -v jq >/dev/null || { echo "jq is required" >&2; exit 2; }
+command -v ddx >/dev/null || { echo "ddx is required" >&2; exit 2; }
+
+wire_file="$(mktemp -t omlx-wire.XXXXXX.jsonl)"
+trap 'rm -f "$wire_file"' EXIT
+
+echo "capturing wire dump from $endpoint → $wire_file" >&2
+
+AGENT_DEBUG_WIRE=1 \
+AGENT_DEBUG_WIRE_STREAM_FULL=1 \
+AGENT_DEBUG_WIRE_FILE="$wire_file" \
+ddx agent run \
+  --harness agent \
+  --provider vidar-omlx \
+  --model "$model" \
+  --prompt-text "$prompt" \
+  --timeout 120s \
+  >/dev/null || {
+    echo "ddx agent run failed — fixture capture incomplete" >&2
+    echo "wire dump (may still be useful): $wire_file" >&2
+    exit 1
+  }
+
+[[ -z "$name" ]] && name="$(basename "$out" .jsonl)"
+
+tmp_out="$(mktemp -t omlx-fixture.XXXXXX.jsonl)"
+trap 'rm -f "$wire_file" "$tmp_out"' EXIT
+
+# Meta record — expected outcome is a hint for the replay test. Authors may
+# need to hand-edit content_contains / error_contains after inspecting the
+# captured body.
+jq -cn \
+  --arg name "$name" \
+  --arg desc "Captured $(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ) from $endpoint model=$model prompt=$prompt" \
+  --arg status "$status" \
+  '{kind:"meta", name:$name, description:$desc,
+    expected:{status:$status, error_must_not_contain:"unexpected end of JSON input"}}' \
+  >> "$tmp_out"
+
+# Frame records — every response chunk in capture order. AGENT_DEBUG_WIRE
+# with STREAM_FULL=1 emits one {"dir":"response","body":"…"} record per read.
+jq -c 'select(.dir=="response" and (.body|type=="string") and (.body|length>0))
+       | {kind:"frame", body:.body}' \
+  "$wire_file" >> "$tmp_out"
+
+mv "$tmp_out" "$out"
+echo "wrote $(wc -l < "$out") records to $out" >&2","stderr":"","interrupted":false,"isImage":false,"noOutputExpected":false}}

acceptance:
1. .ddx/config.yaml schema supports profile_ladders (per-profile ordered tier list) AND the legacy profile_priority is still readable with a deprecation warning.

2. Four shipped profile ladders: default=[cheap,standard,smart], cheap=[cheap], fast=[fast,smart], smart=[smart]. Documented in FEAT-006.

3. Tier escalation in routing.go / execute_bead_orchestrator.go iterates profile_ladders[P] on failure, respecting --provider affinity (ddx-3c5ba7cc) and capability gating (ddx-4817edfd). Escalation triggers: execution_failed, land_conflict, post_run_check_failed, structural_validation_failed. no_changes uses the existing cooldown path.

4. Bead routing event records: requested_profile, requested_tier, resolved_tier, escalation_count, final_tier. A run that escalates cheap -> standard -> success at standard has an event with escalation_count=1, resolved_tier=standard, final_tier=standard.

5. 'ddx work --profile default' drains the queue by trying local first and escalating only when needed. Measured outcome over a test run of 10 representative beads shows at least 50% closing at the cheap tier (target: most work lands locally; escalations are the exception).

6. Regression tests cover each ladder path: success-at-cheap, escalate-cheap-to-standard, escalate-all-tiers-then-exhaustion, --max-tier caps the ladder, --profile cheap never escalates.

7. FEAT-006 Profile Routing section updated with the ladder semantics and the intent table (default/cheap/fast/smart).
description:
## Target UX

The user-facing profile vocabulary (cheap / default / fast / smart) should map to concrete routing policies that take full advantage of local models without blocking on their limits. Specifically:

### default profile (the common case)
Try local models first, escalate to cloud if:
- local model's context window is too small for the bead prompt
- local model lacks a capability the bead requires (tools, structured output, etc.)
- local dispatch fails (provider unreachable, timeout, model-side error)
- bead reopens due to post-run check failure (review-malfunction, land-conflict, etc.)

Escalation target: next tier up in the ladder (standard cloud — e.g. codex gpt-5.4 within subscription). If THAT fails, escalate once more to smart cloud. Then stop and unclaim.

### cheap profile
Local only. Never escalate. Intended for high-volume exploratory work where cost dominates and adequate-quality is sufficient. If the cheap tier can't serve the bead, the bead unclaims and waits.

### fast profile
Cloud-first, skip local entirely. Intended for latency-critical or interactive work where local inference's 9s warmup is unacceptable. Starts at a fast cloud model (e.g. gemini-flash or haiku) and escalates to smart on failure.

### smart profile
Cloud-high-quality from the start. Intended for bead-review, adversarial checks, or beads whose AC demands high-quality reasoning. No escalation — if smart fails, the work fails meaningfully.

## Why this matters (dogfooding signal)

As of 2026-04-18, DDx's default is cheap = qwen/qwen3.6 local (vidar-omlx). With v0.3.14 shipping the SSE-comment-frame filter, the local path actually works end-to-end. The queue can drain mostly on local hardware for pennies per bead, escalating only when the bead genuinely needs cloud quality. That's the throughput-per-dollar goal (goal:throughput-per-dollar label).

## Prereqs (already filed under ddx-2d974641 or sibling beads)

This epic is blocked on the autorouting epic delivering its exit-gate UX — 'declare provider + use canonical ref + autorouting works'. Specifically:

- ddx-8610020e (RouteRequest.Provider field) — foundation
- ddx-0486e601 (fuzzy normalization + per-provider) — lets cheap-tier model refs resolve to provider-native IDs
- ddx-2f5a2284 (catalog/Profile discovery integration) — lets Profile tier overrides retarget to provider-native IDs
- ddx-3c5ba7cc (tier escalation --provider affinity) — the lower half of this epic's 'escalate on failure' semantics
- ddx-4817edfd (capability gating) — prevents routing to a model that can't serve the bead

With those in place, the profile-level policy here is mostly CONFIGURATION + a small amount of scoring-layer logic.

## Scope

### Config schema additions

.ddx/config.yaml agent.routing extends with an explicit per-profile ladder:

    agent:
      routing:
        profile_ladders:
          default: [cheap, standard, smart]      # local first, escalate
          cheap:   [cheap]                        # local only
          fast:    [fast, smart]                  # cloud fast, escalate to smart
          smart:   [smart]                        # cloud high-quality, no escalation
        model_overrides:
          cheap:    qwen/qwen3.6
          fast:     kimi/k2.5
          standard: codex/gpt-5.4                # new: subscription cloud, mid-quality
          smart:    minimax/minimax-m2.7

Current profile_priority field (a flat list) becomes a legacy alias for profile_ladders.default with a deprecation warning.

### Tier-escalation policy (routing.go)

For a given profile P, iterate profile_ladders[P]:
- Evaluate model_overrides[tier] resolves on a viable candidate (with --provider affinity from ddx-3c5ba7cc).
- If yes, try it; on failure (execution_failed / land_conflict / post_run_check_failed), move to next tier.
- Record tier decision + escalation chain in routing event on the bead so telemetry reflects where work actually landed.

### User-facing CLI

- ddx work --profile default — the new primary invocation. Most users run this.
- ddx work --profile cheap / fast / smart — opt-in deviations.
- --max-tier continues to cap the ladder (explicit user override beats config).

### Telemetry + measurement

Every execution event records: requested_profile, requested_tier, resolved_tier, escalation_count, final_tier. Aggregated metrics (FEAT-016 / ddx-4b59b816) report per-profile throughput, per-tier cost, per-bead escalation rate.

## Exit gate

'ddx work --profile default --once --local' against a ready bead on a host with a healthy local provider AND healthy cloud fallback:
- Claims the bead.
- Tries cheap tier (local). If it succeeds, closes bead with tier=cheap recorded.
- If cheap fails for any escalation-triggering reason, automatically retries at standard tier without operator intervention.
- If standard fails, retries at smart.
- Final outcome recorded faithfully (success at whatever tier landed, or exhaustion if all tiers failed).

## Out of scope

- Dynamic profile selection ('auto-pick profile based on bead complexity'). Too much magic; explicit profile choice is fine.
- Cost budgets / circuit breakers. FEAT-014 (token awareness) tracks this separately.
- Per-bead profile overrides (bead-level 'requires-profile: smart'). Could be a future follow-up but out of scope here.

## Metadata

Governing spec: FEAT-006 (agent service) + the autorouting epic ddx-2d974641. This bead is the PROFILE-layer policy that sits on top of the routing fixes from ddx-2d974641. Do not start until ddx-2d974641's Phase-1 children are closed.

events.body:
2026-04-18 dogfooding evidence: 2 omlx attempts at cheap tier, 2 no_changes.

Run 1 — ddx-91bc770a (Checkpoint dirty parent trees before managed bead execution):
- harness: agent / provider: vidar-omlx / model: Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-4bit
- duration: 1300s (21.7 min)
- tokens: 254,413
- outcome: task_no_changes — no rationale written
- bead-class: substantial git-layer refactor

Run 2 — ddx-e3493010 (Resolution Path developer guide):
- harness: agent / provider: vidar-omlx / model: Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-4bit
- duration: 4150s (69.2 min)
- tokens: 2,086,838 (2.09M)
- outcome: task_no_changes — no rationale written
- bead-class: documentation-only markdown authoring with code-walk citations

Findings:

1. v0.3.14 SSE filter is solid. 2M+ tokens streamed on run 2 with zero parse errors. Cheap-tier dispatch path no longer the blocker.

2. Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-4bit is not strong enough to reliably close real DDx beads. Both attempts burned significant wall-clock and tokens without committing. "Free" local compute has real opportunity cost when model thrashes for an hour without output.

3. Without ladder escalation, operator has to manually fall back. This IS the motivating case for ddx-bbb65768: default profile should detect no_changes on first tier and escalate to standard (codex subscription) for a second try.

4. AC #5 of this bead sets 50% close-at-cheap target over 10 representative beads. Current observed rate: 0% close-at-cheap over 2 representative beads. Epic delivery must LIFT this rate — either through better capability gating OR by making escalation cheap enough that failed cheap attempts don't dominate total cost.

5. Qwen3-Coder-Next-MLX-4bit might do better on code-heavy beads; gemma-4-31B-it on docs. Worth follow-up experiment pinning different local models per bead-class after autorouting foundation lands.

Evidence bundles: .ddx/executions/20260418T023901-ba3d8d59/ and 20260418T030326-*/.
events.summary: Qwen3.6 cheap-tier: 2 no_changes over 2 beads (21m + 69m, 2.34M tokens, 0 commits)

description:
Surfaced by ddx-81f71d44. DDx still calls Runner observability methods (r.ProbeHarnessState, r.LoadRoutingSignalSnapshot, r.RefreshClaudeQuotaViaTmux, r.RefreshCodexQuotaViaTmux, r.metricsStoreFromRunner) from agent_usage.go, agent_cmd.go doctor/list/log paths, agent_route_status.go, server/providers.go.

Service surface today does not expose these. Two paths:
A) Extend CONTRACT-003 with explicit observability methods (ProbeHarness, RefreshHarnessQuota, RoutingSignalSnapshot)
B) Subsume into existing methods (HealthCheck for probe; RouteStatus already returns routing snapshot; tmux refresh becomes implicit on HealthCheck)

Path B is cleaner — no contract growth, fewer methods. ListHarnesses + HealthCheck + RouteStatus together cover all observability needs the DDx callers have.

Process: audit DDx callers, map each to existing service methods (or expand HealthCheck/RouteStatus return shapes), update DDx callers, delete the Runner observability methods.

description:
Surface ddx-agent's provider/model/routing visibility through the DDx CLI, fix the execute-loop flag passthrough so named-provider routing works correctly, and capture routing decisions in bead evidence.

Background: DDx imports github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent as a Go library for in-process execution. ddx-agent already owns provider config, /v1/models discovery, health-aware routing, model catalog, and has working CLI subcommands (`ddx-agent providers`, `models`, `check`, `route-status`) — but DDx users cannot access any of that through the DDx CLI. This epic surfaces those capabilities via thin DDx wrappers that call the same public Go packages (agentconfig, modelcatalog, provider/openai, etc.), fixes the `--model` orphan passthrough bug that silently fell back to OpenRouter, and records resolved provider/model/route reason in bead evidence so we can drive cost-tiered escalation analytics later.

Boundary: ddx-agent owns providers/models/routing/health; DDx owns beads/worktrees/orchestration/UI and surfaces ddx-agent state through its own commands.

If any ddx-agent porcelain function is not exposed as a public Go API, file an upstream bead on the github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent repository rather than duplicating logic or parsing stdout.

acceptance:
- [ ] New function gitFetchAndVerifyAncestry(projectRoot, defaultBranch) in cli/internal/agent/ called from execute_bead_loop.go worker body before Executor.Execute
- [ ] Default branch is read from .ddx/config.yaml (fallback: 'master') so non-master trunks work
- [ ] Fetch failure is logged as a structured event but does not fail the worker (air-gap friendly)
- [ ] Divergence detection uses `git merge-base --is-ancestor HEAD origin/<branch>` and is a HARD stop: the bead is unclaimed, the worker loop exits, a 'divergence_detected' event is emitted with both SHAs and the commit count
- [ ] Fast-forward merge from upstream happens automatically when HEAD is behind and clean
- [ ] Fast-forward is skipped with a warning if the working tree is dirty (don't silently stash)
- [ ] Unit tests: fresh clone happy path, ahead-of-origin happy path, behind-origin fast-forward, diverged hard stop, dirty worktree skip, network failure soft continue
- [ ] Integration test simulates two workers with the second starting after the first has landed and verifies the second fetches and fast-forwards before running
- [ ] Docs updated in cli/internal/agent/README.md or equivalent explaining the fetch invariant
description:
First child of ddx-b7ee0d6f. Execute-loop workers currently start each bead from their local HEAD without verifying that HEAD is an ancestor of origin/master. When it isn't (because another machine pushed work), the worker produces commits that can never be fast-forward pushed, and the local branch silently diverges.

## Evidence

cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go around line 320 contains the worker-loop body:

    nextPhase("queueing", false)
    ...
    nextPhase("running", false)
    report, err := w.Executor.Execute(ctx, candidate.ID)

There is no `git fetch origin` and no ancestry check between these phases and the executor call. The worker enters Execute with whatever HEAD happens to be checked out.

Grep confirms: `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_commit.go` has zero references to push/fetch/origin. `execute_bead_orchestrator.go` has git merge/commit calls but never git fetch.

## Required behavior

Before claiming a bead (or at minimum before invoking the executor on a claimed bead), the worker must:

1. Run `git fetch origin <default-branch>` with a timeout. On network failure, log a structured warning and continue (so air-gapped runs still work), but record the staleness in the attempt metadata.
2. Run `git merge-base --is-ancestor HEAD origin/<default-branch>`. If HEAD is NOT an ancestor of origin, the local branch has diverged. This is a hard error:
   - Mark the bead as land_conflict and unclaim it
   - Emit a structured event 'divergence_detected' with both commits and the count
   - Stop the worker loop (do not pick another bead) until a human reconciles
3. If HEAD IS an ancestor but there are upstream commits (origin is ahead), fast-forward the local branch to origin before starting work. This rebases cleanly onto the latest server state.

## Test cases

- Fresh clone, no divergence: worker claims and runs normally
- Upstream is ahead by N commits, local has no uncommitted work: worker fast-forwards then runs
- Local is ahead of upstream (diverged): worker refuses to claim, emits divergence_detected, stops the loop
- `git fetch` times out / network unreachable: worker continues with a staleness warning
- Default branch is configurable (not always 'master') — config read from .ddx/config.yaml

## Related

Complementary to the push-on-success bead (sibling). Both are required for the loop to stay synchronized. Fetch alone is useless without push; push alone is useless without fetch.
title: execute-loop: fetch origin + verify ancestry before claiming a bead

description:
Create skills/ddx/SKILL.md — the single entrypoint for every skills-compatible harness. Body is an overview + vocabulary + intent-router directive that grounds Claude/Codex/etc. in the portable DDx model.

Frontmatter (portable minimum — only name + description):
---
name: ddx
description: Operates the DDx toolkit for document-driven development. Covers beads (work items), the queue, executions, agents, harnesses, personas, reviews, spec-id. Use when the user says "do work", "drain the queue", "run the next bead", "execute a bead", "review this", "check against spec", "what's on the queue", "what's ready", "create a bead", "file this as work", "run an agent", "dispatch", "use a persona", "how am I doing", "ddx doctor", or mentions any ddx CLI command.
---

No argument-hint, when_to_use, context: fork, allowed-tools, disable-model-invocation, user-invocable, paths, model, effort, agent, or hooks — those are Claude-Code extensions that break on Codex.

Body structure (under 500 lines):
1. One-paragraph summary of DDx and what the skill covers.
2. Vocabulary (inline glossary, single source of truth): bead, queue, ready, blocked, claim, close, work, execute-bead, execute-loop, execution, agent, harness, persona, role, binding, profile, plugin, skill, update vs upgrade, review (bead-review vs quorum review), governing artifact, spec-id. One line per term with the CLI command it maps to.
3. Intent router — preceded by the explicit directive: 'Before responding to any DDx-related request, read the matching reference file below. The router is not optional — your answer must be grounded in the reference file's guidance, not this overview alone.' Then a table mapping phrases to reference/*.md files.
4. Top-level policy reminders (one-liners): never edit .ddx/beads.jsonl directly; tracker changes are commit-worthy; plain merge-only for branches with execute-bead commits.
5. Links out: ddx --help, docs/helix/01-frame/features/.

In-scope files:
- skills/ddx/SKILL.md (new)

Out-of-scope (file separately):
- skills/ddx/reference/*.md — child beads
- skills/ddx/evals/ — child bead
- Init changes, embed.go, Makefile — child beads

acceptance: (1) reviewer ProjectRoot, BeadStore, and Runner all use the local 'projectRoot' variable, not m.projectRoot. (2) Test: TestExecuteLoopProjectRootOverride is extended to assert the reviewer is constructed against the spec.ProjectRoot, not m.projectRoot. (3) go test ./internal/server/... -count=1 green.
description:
Discovered 2026-04-15 while resolving land_conflict between ddx-0b9c980a (--project flag) and ddx-a317208c (post-merge reviewer on-by-default).

In cli/internal/server/workers.go runWorker, the reviewer construction uses m.projectRoot:

  reviewer = &agent.DefaultBeadReviewer{
      ProjectRoot: m.projectRoot,
      BeadStore:   bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(m.projectRoot, '.ddx')),
      Runner:      m.buildAgentRunner(m.projectRoot),
      ...
  }

But the rest of runWorker uses the local 'projectRoot' parameter, which respects spec.ProjectRoot from the --project flag. When a worker is dispatched against project B via --project, the implementation runs against B but the reviewer runs against A (m.projectRoot). The reviewer reads B's commits from A's bead store, pollutes A's bead events with reviews of B's beads, and uses A's agent runner config for B's review.

Fix: replace m.projectRoot with projectRoot (the runWorker local variable) in all 4 lines of the reviewer construction.

This was introduced when ddx-a317208c landed in parallel with ddx-0b9c980a. The pattern matches: both touch ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec and both extend runWorker. The reviewer was built against m.projectRoot because at the time --project flag did not exist.
title: bug(server): post-merge reviewer is hard-coded to m.projectRoot, ignores --project flag

acceptance: File exists at skills/ddx/reference/agents.md. Documents --profile (cheap|fast|smart) as default routing, with --harness/--model/--effort as overrides. Lists the 5-persona target roster (code-reviewer, test-engineer, implementer, architect, specification-enforcer). Explicitly states the cross-harness persona consumption contract (DDx injects, not the harness). Cross-references reference/review.md for quorum. Contains a one-paragraph 'Getting more capabilities' note covering ddx install. Linked from skills/ddx/SKILL.md intent-router.
description:
Create skills/ddx/reference/agents.md — the reference file a harness reads when the user says 'run an agent', 'dispatch', 'use a persona', or asks about harnesses/profiles/models.

Content required:
- **Profile-first routing**: ddx agent run --profile cheap|fast|smart. Default is smart. Explain each profile tersely.
- **Explicit overrides**: --harness / --model / --effort only when overriding profile routing for a controlled test, provider-specific bug, or pinning. Do NOT stack --profile with --model or --effort unintentionally.
- **Personas**: bindable via ddx persona bind <role> <persona>. The default plugin ships a curated roster: code-reviewer, test-engineer, implementer, architect, specification-enforcer. See library/personas/README.md for the quality bar. Projects can install additional personas from plugins.
- **Cross-harness consumption contract**: personas are consumed by DDx itself (not by the harness); DDx loads the persona body and injects it as a system-prompt addendum to ddx agent run. Result: same persona file works identically across claude, codex, gemini, etc.
- **Quorum cross-reference** to reference/review.md.
- **CLI reference**: ddx agent run/list/capabilities/doctor/log, ddx persona list/show/bind/bindings.
- **Getting more capabilities** (one paragraph, not a top-level section): ddx install <plugin> adds personas, prompts, and skills. ddx install helix for the HELIX workflow plugin.

Source material: existing /skills/ddx-agent/SKILL.md and /skills/ddx-install/SKILL.md (merged). FEAT-006-agent-service.md for the persona consumption contract (now documented in the Personas section).

In-scope files:
- skills/ddx/reference/agents.md (new)

Out-of-scope:
- skills/ddx/SKILL.md (separate bead)
- Other reference/*.md files (separate beads)
- library/personas/* (Phase 3)
- Skill for ddx install was dropped; the install guidance is folded in here as one paragraph.
title: Create skills/ddx/reference/agents.md — harness/profile/persona dispatch

acceptance: (1) After `ddx agent execute-bead <id> --provider vidar-coder` closes a bead, `ddx bead evidence list <id>` shows a new entry with kind=routing containing resolved_provider=vidar-coder, resolved_model=qwen/qwen3-coder-next (or whatever vidar resolves to), route_reason, base_url=http://vidar:1234/v1. (2) Evidence body is structured JSON that a script can parse. (3) Test: run execute-bead against the script harness (no real agent), verify the routing evidence entry is added with the expected fields. (4) `go build ./...` passes. (5) `go test ./cli/internal/agent/... -count=1` passes.
description:
Every execute-bead run must record a kind:routing evidence entry on the bead after agent resolution but before landing. Fields: resolved_provider (name), resolved_model (concrete model ID), route_reason (catalog-match / pattern-match / first-available / health-fallback / direct-override), fallback_chain (list of providers tried before success, if any), base_url (for local providers — audit trail for "where did the tokens go").

Implementation: add hook in cli/internal/agent/runner.go's RunAgent path that inspects the resolved AgentRunConfig + agentlib Provider after resolution, and calls bead.Store.AppendEvent via a new evidence-append helper. The evidence entry is structured JSON in the body field.

Purpose: feeds the bigger cost-tiered routing epic's analytics. Answers the "where did my tokens go" question. Makes per-bead retry escalation auditable without having to scrape session logs.
title: Visibility 8: capture routing decisions in bead evidence (kind:routing)

description:
Surfaced by ddx-dac03f0a (commit 72d60f2b). Four DDx test files still test Runner internals (LookPath injection, resolveHarness, AgentProvider wiring, context cancellation through Runner) — not Runner usage. They block deleting Runner entirely.

Coverage analysis: the agent module has comprehensive tests for the same behaviors (provider construction, route resolution, OS-cancel cleanup, etc.) — see /Users/erik/Projects/agent/service_*.go and internal/harnesses/claude/runner_test.go. The DDx Runner-internals tests are largely redundant with the upstream coverage of the same logic via the service.

This bead does:
1. DELETE the four Runner-internals test files: agent_runner_test.go, agent_runner_service_test.go, omlx_e2e_test.go, runner_context_test.go
2. DELETE Runner struct + remaining methods + observability_workdir wrappers internal Runner build
3. Execute the 5 file-delete beads as a single atomic sweep:
   - ddx-f0cab7b3: state.go + registry.go
   - ddx-ea7f041c: routing.go (+ routing_test.go, routing_discovery_test.go, etc.)
   - ddx-70c48576: routing_signal_*.go (KEEP routing_metrics.go — DDx evidence, not agent state)

   - ddx-2912e7cd: discovery.go + provider_deadline.go + providerstatus/probe.go
4. Update types.go to drop dead types (RouteRequest, CandidatePlan, RoutingSignalSnapshot if no longer used)
5. Run full test suite; verify only known-unrelated failures remain

After this lands, DDx imports from agent are minimal: just root agentlib + types referenced in the contract.

Re-scope note: ddx-70c48576 originally said delete routing_metrics.go, but RoutingMetricsStore writes .ddx/agent-logs/routing-outcomes.jsonl which is DDx-managed evidence, not agent state. Keep routing_metrics.go in DDx.
notes:
<close-rescoped>
2026-04-21: Closed as PARTIALLY satisfied + re-scoped. Progress against the 6 AC items:

(1) Runner struct deleted — NOT SATISFIED, by design. Per service_run.go lines 29-35 (codex commit 7cc1eb58): DDx's virtual and script harnesses are different products from upstream's same-named stubs — virtual is a content-addressed record/replay dictionary keyed by PromptHash; script reads a filesystem directive file; neither maps to upstream v0.8.0's in-request metadata model (agent-81830379). DDx's HTTP-provider dispatch (lmstudio, openrouter) also lives in runner.go because upstream requires these to route through the embedded agent runtime. Runner stays as the DDx-owned fixture/HTTP dispatcher.

(2) Four Runner-internals test files deleted — SATISFIED. routing_test.go, catalog_test.go's BuildCandidatePlans matrix, and state_test.go all gone (commit 00481155 + 7cc1eb58).

(3) 5 file-delete beads cascade and close — 3/5 done: routing.go (ddx-ea7f041c ✓), state.go + registry.go (ddx-f0cab7b3 ✓; registry.go renamed to harness_config.go with DDx-owned narrower scope). routing_metrics.go was already re-scoped per its own re-scope note (kept as DDx evidence store). routing_signals*.go already deleted prior.

(4) types.go has no dead types — SATISFIED (codex pruned in 7cc1eb58; -75 LOC).

(5) grep 'agent.Registry|agent.Runner|BuildCandidatePlans|RankCandidates|HarnessState' in cli/cmd + cli/internal/server shows exactly one hit: cmd/exec.go:93 which is a COMMENT ('constructed a *agent.Runner only to call its Run method'), not a call. Zero production call sites.

(6) Build + test green — SATISFIED.

The Runner-struct-deleted goal belongs to a different design. If DDx ever wants to delete Runner, the follow-up would remove the record/replay dictionary for virtual, the script-directive harness, and push HTTP-provider dispatch into the agent library's provider registry — none of which are tracked today. Closing so the tracker reflects reality; any future push to delete Runner files a fresh bead with the revised design.
</close-rescoped>
title: Phase 5g: delete Runner internals tests + execute cascade file-deletes (final DDx cleanup)

stalled: no write activity after 30 consecutive read-only tool calls
result_rev=ee9b397f84dfab2865c21b213e540746c6393236
base_rev=ee9b397f84dfab2865c21b213e540746c6393236
retry_after=2026-04-15T04:55:59Z

This is a distinct failure mode from the long-context hang seen on Stage 2.2. Here the model is actively producing tool calls (reading files) but never commits to a write/edit, so the stall detector after 30 reads cancels the run. Symptom of context-window exhaustion: the model is re-reading the same files or exploring too broadly without ever reaching a decision. Not a 500. Not a hang. Not a loop abort.

Escalated to claude sonnet per user directive. Local model is not viable for code-heavy beads with the current prompt shape (plan doc + 1908-line schema + bead context). Future cost-tiered routing epic should capture: prompt-context pre-filtering for local models, AND a check for "N consecutive reads without a write" that kicks in earlier than the 30-read cap.

description:
<ddx agent execute-loop injects z-ai/glm-5.1 model override that fails, while ddx agent run --harness agent works fine with qwen3.5-27b via vidar LMStudio host.>

ddx agent execute-loop on eitri consistently fails because the execute-loop → execute-bead path injects --model z-ai/glm-5.1, overriding both the ~/.config/agent/config.yaml default_provider (vidar → qwen3.5-27b) AND the models.yaml catalog. The same agent harness works perfectly with direct ddx agent run.

PROOF:

1. dd agent run --text "Hello" --harness agent --timeout 60s succeeds:
   - model: qwen3.5-27b
   - exit_code: 0
   - duration_ms: ~44s

2. ddx agent execute-loop --once --json --harness agent fails:
   - ps shows spawned process with --model z-ai/glm-5.1
   - result: execution_failed, 502 Bad Gateway

3. ddx agent execute-loop --once --json --harness agent --model qwen3.5-27b also fails:
   - Still gets a 404 from OpenRouter or 502 from Alibaba
   - execute-loop's --model flag doesn't propagate to the spawned execute-bead subprocess the way expected

ROOT CAUSE: execute-bead resolves the model from ddx metadata/routing (profiles, targets, or cached model catalog) rather than from ~/.config/agent/config.yaml or from execute-loop's --model flag. The z-ai/glm-5.1 model appears to come from a stale or incorrect routing data source.

EVIDENCE:
- models.yaml defines code-high profile with agent.openai: gpt-5.4
- ~/.config/agent/config.yaml defines vidar with qwen3.5-27b
- Neither source mentions z-ai/glm-5.1
- The model appears to come from a separate routing layer in execute-bead or execute-loop (possibly a cached catalog entry or the server's worker routing config)

FILES:
- cli/cmd/agent_execute_loop.go (execute-loop model resolution)
- cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead.go (execute-bead model resolution)  
- cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go (resolveModel chain)

acceptance: CLI help, operator docs, and ddx-bead skill guidance all teach the same model: execute-bead is the primitive, execute-loop is the normal queue-driven surface, planning/document-only beads are valid execution targets, merge is default unless explicit required execution docs block landing or --no-merge is set, and the documented JSON/result semantics tell operators and automation how close/preserve outcomes behave
description:
<context-digest>
Review area: consumer-facing execute-bead / execute-loop guidance. Evidence covers the shipped CLI help surfaces, the open docs gaps tracked by ddx-a4195a8e and ddx-7a01ba6c, and the need to teach humans and agents one coherent model: execute-bead is the primitive, execute-loop is the normal queue-driven surface.
</context-digest>
Implement the first complete consumer-facing guidance surface for DDx agent execution.

## Required changes
1. Update CLI help text and any nearby command docs so `ddx agent execute-bead` is described as the single-bead primitive and `ddx agent execute-loop` is described as the queue/supervisor command built on top of it.
2. Add or update user-facing operator docs and CLI reference surfaces so users discover `execute-loop` first for normal queue-driven work, with concrete examples for:
   - `ddx agent execute-loop`
   - `ddx agent execute-loop --once`
   - `ddx agent execute-loop --poll-interval <duration>`
   - `ddx agent execute-bead <bead-id>` for bounded/manual runs
3. Update the ddx-bead skill and any relevant internal skill/operator guidance so agents are told explicitly:
   - planning/document-only beads are valid execution targets
   - execute-loop is the default queue-driven entrypoint
   - execute-bead is the lower-level primitive for targeted/manual execution
4. Document the result/close semantics operators need in practice:
   - which outcomes/statuses close a bead
   - which outcomes preserve and leave the bead open
   - which JSON fields automation should read (`status`, `outcome`, `preserve_ref`, `result_rev`, `session_id`)
5. Make the operator guidance consistent with the current merge model:
   - merge is the default successful outcome
   - explicit required execution documents are the automatic merge gates
   - `--no-merge` is the manual preserve override

## Boundaries
- Do not invent a new bead shape or merge-gate field
- Use the existing execute-bead / execute-loop / execution-document contract
- Keep the user-facing guidance aligned with the actual shipped command behavior and documented result surface
title: Implement execute-loop-first help, skill, and operator guidance

acceptance: The bead tracker has fuzz/property/chaos coverage that hammers CRUD, deps, claims, closes, evidence appends, malformed JSONL, and duplicate-row repair paths; randomized runs preserve core invariants (parseable file, one live row per bead ID, valid statuses/deps, deterministic ready/blocked behavior); and the new coverage is easy to rerun locally when tracker bugs are suspected
description:
<context-digest>
Review area: bead tracker durability and corruption resistance. Evidence covers the existing concurrency/chaos tests in cli/internal/bead/chaos_test.go, the recent duplicate-row/latest-state bugs, and the need to aggressively validate JSONL tracker safety under randomized updates, crashes, and malformed/interleaved state.
</context-digest>
Add deeper destructive test coverage for the bead tracker so we can trust it under queue-worker churn.

## Scope
- Add property/state-machine style tests for bead CRUD, deps, claim/unclaim, close/reopen, and evidence append invariants
- Add fuzz coverage for JSONL parsing, duplicate-row folding, and tracker round-trip safety
- Expand chaos tests to mix concurrent update/claim/close/create/evidence operations against the same store
- Verify the tracker never returns corrupt JSONL, duplicate live IDs, or impossible state transitions after randomized operation streams
- Add any minimal repair/canonicalization assertions needed to prove the live view remains one current state per bead ID

## Boundaries
- Focus on the tracker/store and queue derivation surfaces, not broader agent execution behavior
- Prefer deterministic seeds and reproducible shrinkable failures where possible
title: Harden bead tracker with fuzz, property, and chaos coverage

description:
Migrate cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead_test.go TestExecuteBeadHarnessNoiseNotSynthesized() from fakeExecuteBeadGit + fakeAgentRunner to real git + script harness.

Pattern:
- Use cli/internal/agent/integration_helper_test.go newScriptHarnessRepo(t, beadCount) to build an isolated repo with real git.
- Use runGitInteg (from cli/internal/git/git_basic_test.go) for any supplemental git invocations, so GIT_* env is scrubbed.

STACK.md recommended Option A (downgrade vite) or Option B (drop Houdini for graphql-request). Stage 3.4 shipped with neither — works for now because the single NodeInfo query is trivial.

acceptance: (1) `ddx agent check` reports runtime liveness for every configured provider, not config sanity. (2) `ddx agent check --provider vidar` probes only vidar and reports result. (3) `ddx agent doctor` still exists and is about config/registry health, NOT provider liveness (semantic separation preserved). (4) Exit code 0 when at least one provider is reachable and has at least one model; 1 otherwise. (5) `go build ./...` passes. (6) Manual: stop lmstudio on vidar temporarily, run `ddx agent check --provider vidar`, see explicit unreachable status; restart, run again, see connected + model count.
description:
Add a new DDx CLI subcommand `ddx agent check [--provider X]`. Semantic distinction from existing `ddx agent doctor`: doctor answers "is my config failed?" (config validation, malformed YAML, missing API keys); check answers "what agents can I use right now?" (runtime liveness, which providers respond, which models are currently available). Both commands coexist.

Implementation lives in cli/cmd/agent_check.go. Mirrors ddx-agent's `cmdCheck` — probes each configured provider's /v1/models endpoint, reports reachability and current model inventory, exits 0 if at least one provider is reachable and has at least one usable model, exits 1 otherwise. Uses the Go package, not shell-out.
title: Visibility 5: ddx agent check [--provider X] — runtime availability

acceptance: After a `no_changes` execute-loop result where `base_rev == result_rev`, the bead persists `execute-loop-retry-after`, `execute-loop-last-status`, and `execute-loop-last-detail`; `ddx bead ready --json --execution` suppresses the bead until the retry-after time; and a deterministic test covers the tracker-backed path rather than only in-memory worker logic.
description:
<context-digest>
Follow-up observation from the HELIX alpha run that launched `helix-fef22846` via `ddx agent execute-loop --once --json` and returned `status=no_changes`, `detail=agent made no commits`, `base_rev=result_rev=de67981a79a27ab8449bf6c475dda677fb25acfc`.
</context-digest>

`ddx agent execute-loop` reported a no-progress result but did not persist the retry-suppression metadata that `ReadyExecution()` expects, so the same bead remained at the front of the execution-ready queue immediately after the attempt.

## Reproduction
1. In `/Users/erik/Projects/helix`, run `ddx agent execute-loop --once --json` and get a `no_changes` result where `base_rev == result_rev`.
2. Inspect bead `helix-fef22846` in `.ddx/beads.jsonl` or via `ddx bead show --json`.
3. Observe the new `execute-bead` event is present, but `execute-loop-retry-after`, `execute-loop-last-status`, and `execute-loop-last-detail` are absent.
4. Run `ddx bead ready --json --execution` again.
5. Observe `helix-fef22846` is still the first execution-ready candidate instead of being suppressed for the cooldown window.

## Observed gap
`ExecuteBeadWorker.Run()` calls `SetExecutionCooldown()` for no-progress outcomes, but the tracker-backed HELIX run did not retain any of the cooldown fields that `ReadyExecution()` checks.

## Why this matters
Without durable cooldown metadata, `execute-loop` can repeatedly burn agent time and tokens on the same no-op bead in back-to-back scans.
title: Persist execute-loop cooldown metadata after no-progress attempts

description:

Steps:
1. grep -rE '/ddx-(bead|run|agent|review|status|doctor|install)' docs/ and catalog matches.
2. For each match, decide:

- docs/helix/01-frame/ (features, prd, vision, concerns)

- library/ content — separate concern (Phase 3 for personas).
- Plugin content (.ddx/plugins/) — not part of ship-with.

description:
server/providers.go uses agent.RoutingSignalSnapshot extensively (8+ signature touch points) plus agent.NewRegistry (4x) and agent.LoadRoutingSignalSnapshotForWorkDir (4x).

Two paths depending on the routing_signals re-scope decision:
A. If RoutingSignalSnapshot stays in DDx as a DDx-evidence type: providers.go imports it as-is (no migration needed beyond the Registry references)
B. If it moves to agent contract: convert to service.ListProviders + per-provider RouteCandidateStatus

Recommended: A. The snapshot is DDx-side evidence aggregation; the data sources (codex/claude/openrouter native CLI logs) are DDx-managed paths.

Pre-condition: routing_signals re-scope bead.

Either way: drop agent.NewRegistry usage (replace with service.ListHarnesses).
notes: Codex flagged this may be high-risk because server/providers.go has many helpers typed directly on DDx agent.Harness, HarnessStatus, RoutingSignalSnapshot, RoutingOutcome. If first-pass migration uncovers >2 hours of refactoring, split into "source data via service" (drop NewRegistry) and "preserve provider read-model helper semantics" (RoutingSignalSnapshot/RoutingOutcome handling).
title: Migrate server/providers.go RoutingSignalSnapshot consumers (last big production caller)

acceptance: FEAT-006, FEAT-012, FEAT-014, FEAT-015, and API-001 describe the same contract: execute-bead compiles a deterministic prompt from bead data plus resolved references; each attempt produces tracked machine-readable execution artifacts; ignored runtime scratch is clearly separated from tracked evidence; and commit-message metadata is projected from tracked files rather than ad hoc runtime state
description:
<context-digest>
Review area: governing contract for execute-bead prompts and execution evidence. Evidence covers the current FEAT-006/API-001 workflow, the prompt fallback bug, the desire to keep beads authored and relatively stable, and the new requirement that execution evidence be tracked in git so autoresearch can replay, compare, and analyze runs.
</context-digest>
Align the governing docs around one execute-bead evidence contract.

## Goals
- Define the execute-bead prompt template as a DDx-owned rationalizer over bead fields plus resolved governing references, not a bead mutation and not a speculative summary of whole specs
- Define the stable tracked artifact set for each execution attempt: prompt, manifest, result, checks, normalized log, and usage/provider pointers
- Define the split between tracked execution evidence and ignored local scratch/runtime state
- Define the default commit policy for successful and preserved attempts
- Define the rule that all programmatically-added commit message metadata must be rendered from tracked machine-readable files

acceptance: FEAT-006 and the execution-evidence design docs define embedded-agent runtime paths for managed execute-bead runs; the embedded harness no longer writes default root-level agent state inside execution worktrees; and tests prove session/telemetry output is redirected into DDx-owned evidence or runtime directories
description:
<context-digest>
Review area: embedded ddx-agent runtime ownership inside execute-bead worktrees. Evidence covers the tracked execution-evidence lane, the current rule that DDx should own execution artifacts for managed runs, and the need to prevent embedded-agent session/telemetry files from appearing as ad hoc root-level state inside execution worktrees.
</context-digest>
Define and implement the path contract for embedded ddx-agent logs and telemetry when DDx runs the embedded harness inside execute-bead and execute-loop.

## Goals
- Prevent embedded ddx-agent session logs, traces, or telemetry from being written as default root-level runtime state in managed execution worktrees
- Redirect embedded-agent runtime output into DDx-owned execution-evidence or runtime paths
- Keep the split explicit between tracked execution evidence and ignored local runtime scratch
- Update the governing specs so embedded-agent log ownership is documented rather than implied by implementation

## Required spec work
- Update FEAT-006 and the execution-evidence design lane to define where embedded-agent logs/telemetry live during execute-bead runs
- Clarify how embedded-agent-owned logs differ from external provider-native logs

## Required implementation work
- Configure the embedded agent harness so managed execute-bead worktrees do not accumulate root-level agent state
- Add tests proving embedded-agent runtime output is redirected into DDx-owned paths

acceptance: The governing docs and queue contain a coherent lane for single-ticket-first queue policy, epic-scoped workers, epic branch/worktree naming, child-bead sequencing, and regular merge-commit landing with epic merge gates
description:
<context-digest>
Review area: epic execution semantics after the execute-loop dogfood pass. Evidence covers the need to prioritize single tickets ahead of epics, keep open epics out of the ordinary execute-loop launch set, execute one epic sequentially in one persistent worktree, preserve child-ticket history on the epic branch, and land completed epics with a regular merge commit after epic-level merge gates pass.
</context-digest>
Define epic-scoped execution and merge behavior across DDx queueing, git, server, and UI surfaces.

## Goals
- Define single-ticket-first queue ordering with open epics excluded from the ordinary execute-loop worker
- Define one persistent branch/worktree per active epic and sequential child execution inside that worktree
- Define how child tickets close individually while their commits accumulate on the epic branch
- Define epic-level merge gates and regular merge-commit landing semantics
- Define the server/UI worker and queue surfaces needed to supervise epic workers alongside ordinary workers
title: Design epic-scoped execution and merge workflow

acceptance:
1. This bead lists (and keeps current) the status of every upstream agent bead DDx filed or consumes: filed date, upstream status, DDx bump commit, any DDx-side follow-up bead(s).

2. For each landed upstream fix, a DDx-side consumer or smoke test exists that exercises the new behavior (or a tracking bead says 'no DDx consumer needed' with reasoning).

3. When agent-a365bcf2 (preset rename) lands, a migration bead is filed and this bead closes.
description:
## Context

Tracker bead for propagation of upstream ddx-agent fixes filed 2026-04-17/18.
Both fresh-eyes reviews (codex + opus, 2026-04-18) flagged the need for a
single consolidated tracker on the DDx side — this is it.

## Status table (refreshed 2026-04-19; DDx is now on agent v0.5.0)

| Upstream bead | Title                                 | Upstream status | DDx bump     | DDx commit  | DDx consumer / smoke                                                                                                       |
|---------------|---------------------------------------|-----------------|--------------|-------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| agent-92f0f324| DetectedFlavor                        | landed v0.3.12  | v0.3.11→12   | c9368312    | Covered by ddx-4817edfd (capability-gating epic; decomposed into 4 sub-beads, all closed).                                 |
| agent-767549c7| capability flags (SupportsTools)      | landed v0.3.12  | v0.3.11→12   | c9368312    | ddx-4817edfd sub-beads: context-window gating, tool-calling gating, effort/permissions gating, structured-output gating.   |
| agent-941e7e42| AGENT_DEBUG_WIRE                      | landed v0.3.12  | v0.3.11→12   | c9368312    | Used ad-hoc via scripts/capture-omlx-fixture.sh (regenerates fixtures); no CI-level regression test drives it.             |
| agent-04639431| strip thinking field on non-Anthropic | landed v0.3.13  | v0.3.12→13   | 9d057f83    | NO DDx CONSUMER NEEDED — the filter is entirely internal to agent's provider layer; DDx just sees the clean content stream.|
| agent-f237e07b| SSE comment-frame filter              | landed v0.3.14  | v0.3.13→14   | b59ebacf    | ddx-bbb2d177 smoke test landed in 1cd8e3a3 and was deleted in d5ebab2b during the v0.5.0 cutover (live-smoke equivalent now lives in the ddx-agent comparison/benchmark suite). Fixture files remain orphaned at cli/internal/agent/testdata/omlx-wire/ and should be cleaned up in a follow-up. |
| agent-a365bcf2| preset rename (cheap/default/smart)   | in_progress     | —            | —           | Blocks this bead's closure. When it lands, file a DDx migration bead for any --preset references in .ddx/config.yaml (currently at least one harness uses '--preset codex').|

## v0.5.0 cutover context (2026-04-19)

Commit d5ebab2b cut DDx over to agent v0.5.0 and dropped the local replace
directive. That post-dates the v0.3.x-era fixes this tracker was originally
scoped around. v0.5.0 contracted the public surface (provider/openai now
internal/), which is why the omlx smoke test was removed rather than
migrated. Any residual v0.3.x consumer test would be orphaned by the surface
contraction anyway — the DDx side of that consumer coverage now lives in the
upstream agent repo's benchmark suite, and DDx's own execute-bead path is
covered by the broader test set in cli/internal/agent/.

## Remaining work before this bead closes

1. Upstream agent-a365bcf2 (preset rename) lands.
2. File a DDx-side migration bead: sweep .ddx/config.yaml, library templates,
   and any hardcoded preset strings for claude/codex/cursor/agent aliases;
   swap to smart/cheap/default/minimal; optionally keep a compatibility shim
   for one release.
3. Optional (P4): delete the orphaned cli/internal/agent/testdata/omlx-wire/
   fixtures (no test consumes them post-v0.5.0) OR relocate them to the
   upstream benchmark suite.
4. Close this tracker bead.

## Non-goals

- Re-implementing the upstream fixes. They've landed.
- Tracking ddx-agent's own backlog (agent-b1c2d3e4 manifest v4 etc.). That's upstream's concern.
- Retrofitting v0.3.x-era consumer tests after the v0.5.0 surface contraction.

acceptance: The queue contains a concrete contract-and-build lane for execute-loop no-change disposition, satisfaction-based auto-close, and retry suppression so queue burning does not stall on one unresolved no-op bead
description:
<context-digest>
Review area: execute-loop queue-drain reliability after the no-change misclassification fix. Evidence covers repeated installed-binary `execute-loop --once` runs selecting the same execution-ready bead, the new `no_changes` status, the need to auto-close already-satisfied beads without reintroducing false-success closure, and the requirement to avoid infinite retry loops on unresolved no-progress work.
</context-digest>
Improve execute-loop so repeated no-progress attempts are classified and handled in a way that lets the queue make forward progress without sacrificing correctness.

## Goals
- Distinguish already-satisfied no-change beads from unresolved no-progress attempts
- Prevent immediate re-execution loops on beads that repeatedly return `no_changes`
- Keep closure semantics machine-owned and evidence-backed rather than inferred from freeform agent output
- Preserve queue ordering semantics while making the queue drain behavior operationally useful
title: Improve execute-loop no-progress handling and queue-drain behavior

</context-digest>
Align DDx server and UI surfaces with the new invocation-activity model.

## Scope
- Update API/detail semantics for agent activity
- Surface native session and trace references where available
- Keep embedded ddx-agent detail richer when DDx owns or can reference it
- Avoid promising full external provider transcript browsing
title: Align server and UI agent-activity surfaces with native provider ownership

acceptance: 1) TestExecuteBeadHiddenRefUniqueness no longer references fakeExecuteBeadGit or fakeAgentRunner. 2) Test dispatches through RealLandingGitOps + newScriptHarnessRepo (or equivalent real-git harness). 3) cd cli && go test ./cmd -run '^TestExecuteBeadHiddenRefUniqueness$' -count=1 green. 4) No other test in agent_execute_bead_test.go is modified.
description:
Migrate cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead_test.go TestExecuteBeadHiddenRefUniqueness() from fakeExecuteBeadGit + fakeAgentRunner to real git + script harness.

Pattern:
- Use cli/internal/agent/integration_helper_test.go newScriptHarnessRepo(t, beadCount) to build an isolated repo with real git.
- Use runGitInteg (from cli/internal/git/git_basic_test.go) for any supplemental git invocations, so GIT_* env is scrubbed.
- Replace fakeExecuteBeadGit with the RealLandingGitOps flow used in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land_test.go.
- Replace fakeAgentRunner with the script harness (cli/internal/agent/script.go) driven by a per-attempt directive file. When the test needs to simulate 'agent emits no commits' or 'agent emits a dirty worktree', encode that as script directives rather than a canned Result struct.
- Preserve every assertion — the point is that the test now exercises real git + real merge/preserve semantics, not that the surface shrinks.

Scope limit: ONLY this test. Do not migrate any other test in the same file; touching another test expands blast radius outside the bead.

Verification: cd cli && go test ./cmd -run '^TestExecuteBeadHiddenRefUniqueness$' -count=1 passes. No struct ending in -GitOps or -Runner that fakes the thing under test is referenced from this test after the migration.

Governing concern: concerns.md §testing 'no mocks, period' + 'never mock the thing you are testing'. Parent: ddx-d9df348d.

Clean up:

acceptance: DDx updates its ddx-agent dependency/reference to upstream v0.1.1; any required integration or config changes for retry ownership, telemetry, usage/session continuity, or tool path behavior are implemented; affected tests pass; and any noteworthy compatibility or rollout notes are recorded in the bead or linked docs
description:
Upstream `DocumentDrivenDX/agent` now has point release `v0.1.1`. Update DDx to consume that release and reconcile any integration assumptions that changed since `v0.1.0`.

Key upstream changes since `v0.1.0`:
- OTel telemetry landed for session, chat-attempt, and tool spans/metrics, with a documented telemetry contract and runtime-specific cost attribution.
- Provider retries moved into the runtime so providers execute one attempt per call and expose richer attempt metadata.
- Session continuity/history support landed in `Run`.
- `ddx-agent usage` reporting landed.
- Tool path handling now resolves symlink chains.
- Compaction behavior was hardened: prefix-aware accounting, balanced compaction events, and fail-closed handling when compaction cannot fit within budget.
- Self-update cache writing was fixed for first-run homes without an existing cache directory.
- Mixed-provenance known-cost session rollups now preserve the root known cost instead of downgrading to unknown.

DDx should review whether any wrapper logic, telemetry ingestion, usage/reporting integration, or runtime assumptions need adjustment when moving from `v0.1.0` to `v0.1.1`.
notes: Bumped github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent to v0.1.1 in cli/go.mod; no DDx wrapper/source changes were required because the v0.1.1 API remained compatible. Verified with 'go test ./internal/agent ./cmd -count=1' in the cli module.
title: Update DDx to ddx-agent v0.1.1

description:
## Scope
Add a context budget option that shrinks the execute-bead prompt for cheap-tier attempts by omitting large governing documents and including only the bead's own description, acceptance criteria, and the most relevant file content.

## Context
Local models (qwen3-coder-next, 32k context) frequently hit compaction limits on beads with large prompts. Shrinking the prompt for cheap-tier attempts lets them attempt more beads before escalating.

## Files to touch
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go — add ContextBudget to ExecuteBeadOptions, gate prompt assembly
- cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead.go — add --context-budget flag
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_test.go — test minimal budget produces shorter prompt
title: Prompt-context pre-filter for cheap-tier attempts
