acceptance:
- [ ] execute-loop with zero flags escalates across cheap → standard → smart on failure
- [ ] Review agent auto-selects a tier >= impl tier, defaulting to smart
- [ ] Provider health probe runs at attempt time; unhealthy providers skip cleanly with cooldown
- [ ] Each attempt records tier + provider + probe_result + outcome on the bead
- [ ] Integration test: cheap provider 502 + standard succeeds → bead closes with escalation trail visible
- [ ] --min-tier / --max-tier overrides work and are documented
- [ ] --harness / --model debug overrides disable escalation
- [ ] Zero-flag 'ddx agent execute-loop' is the documented happy path
description:
The CLI should not require the user to enumerate harness/model pairs. The tool already knows which tiers exist (model-catalog.yaml: cheap/standard/smart) and which harnesses are configured. Given a live probe of each provider's health at attempt time, the loop can auto-escalate on its own.

## Principle

Discovery over configuration. The user shouldn't write a fallback chain — the tool should figure it out.

## Default policy (no flags)

- Start at **cheap** tier, execute the bead
- If merge succeeds AND post-review (gap #1) APPROVES → close
- If anything fails (execution_failed, no_changes after N retries, post_run_check_failed, REQUEST_CHANGES from review) → escalate one tier and retry
- Chain: cheap → standard → smart → exhausted (preserve, flag for human)
- Review agent runs at max(impl_tier+1, smart). Never review cheap with cheap.

## Resource discovery at attempt time

- Before picking a concrete harness/model for a tier, probe each candidate provider (local inference host, API key validity, network reachability)
- If provider returns 502 / 500 / timeout / auth-rejected within a short budget, mark it unhealthy with a cooldown and try the next provider in the tier
- If every provider in a tier is unhealthy, skip the tier and escalate
- Per-provider health state is process-local and visible via a new 'ddx agent providers' sub-command (ddx-09ea1884 Visibility 3)

## Rare overrides

- --min-tier standard (skip cheap — useful for hard beads known to need it)
- --max-tier standard (never reach smart — cost control)
- --harness <name> / --model <name> (debug override; disables escalation)

## Surface

Default invocation becomes:

  ddx agent execute-loop --once

Everything else is implicit. Review, escalation, and resource discovery are on-by-default.

## Events

Each attempt appends an execute-bead event: { harness, model, tier, provider, probe_result, attempt_status, review_status }. The event stream shows exactly why a tier/provider was picked or skipped.

## Context

Observed during axon ADR-018 loop:
- bragi was down (502) — I manually probed with curl and switched to vidar-coder. Tool should do this.
- qwen3-coder-next merged but opus review rejected — I manually re-invoked with sonnet. Tool should escalate automatically.
- Resulting CLI invocation in my notes was too complex (—-fallback-chain a/b,c/d,e/f --post-review g/h). User feedback: too many knobs. Reach for a default that just works.

## Acceptance criteria replaced with discovery-driven ones (see accept field)

events.body: This bead is one of the review-loop primitives the meta-epic depends on. See ddx-4b59b816 for the full scope.
events.summary: Part of cost-tiered execution + measurement meta-epic ddx-4b59b816 — maximize closed-bead throughput per dollar.

notes:
<operator-feedback 2026-04-15>
Additional observation: --local is too prominent as a flag. The default IS server-worker mode, but I (an experienced DDx operator) still reached for --local reflexively because I wanted a synchronous handle to the subprocess. The root cause: I didn't know how to tail a server-side worker from the CLI.

Possible fixes (minor, could be part of this bead's scope):
- 'ddx agent execute-loop --once --follow' that submits to server and tails the worker log to stdout until completion, then returns. Gives the synchronous-feel without forking a subprocess.
- 'ddx server workers wait <worker-id>' that blocks until the named worker is in a terminal state.
- Document in execute-loop --help that --local is for debugging only; the primary surface is server-worker.

Not worth a separate bead — just ergonomics for the already-correct default.
</operator-feedback>
title: exec: tier-based auto-escalation driven by resource discovery

description:
Migrate cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead_gates_test.go TestExecuteBead_RequiredGatePass_Merges() from fakeExecuteBeadGit + fakeAgentRunner to real git + script harness.

Pattern:
- Use cli/internal/agent/integration_helper_test.go newScriptHarnessRepo(t, beadCount) to build an isolated repo with real git.

Scope limit: ONLY this test. Do not migrate any other test in the same file; touching another test expands blast radius outside the bead.

Verification: cd cli && go test ./cmd -run '^TestExecuteBead_RequiredGatePass_Merges$' -count=1 passes. No struct ending in -GitOps or -Runner that fakes the thing under test is referenced from this test after the migration.

acceptance: File exists at skills/ddx/reference/review.md. Explicitly distinguishes 'bead review' (ddx bead review <id>) from 'quorum review' (ddx agent run --quorum). Documents required evidence format (verdict + per-criterion grade + artifact). Lists at least 3 anti-patterns (hallucinated approval, verdict without evidence, reviewing prompt not diff). No mention of 'context: fork' or .claude/agents/ (those are harness-specific). Linked from skills/ddx/SKILL.md intent-router.
description:
Create skills/ddx/reference/review.md — disambiguates the two 'review' concepts in DDx and guides the harness on which to use.

Content required:
- **Bead review** (ddx bead review <id>): structured grading of a completed bead's implementation against its declared acceptance criteria. Output contract: APPROVE / REQUEST_CHANGES / BLOCK per AC item, with evidence (file:line references, test output, diff hunks).
- **Quorum review** (ddx agent run --quorum=<policy> --harnesses=<list>): dispatch the review prompt to multiple harnesses and aggregate verdicts. Use for high-stakes changes, controversial refactors, or adversarial checks.
- **Required evidence format** for both: verdict + per-criterion grade + the specific artifact (diff, test output, file path) supporting it. No verdicts without evidence.
- **Anti-patterns**: hallucinated approvals, verdict without evidence, reviewing the prompt instead of the diff.
- **No harness-specific subagent instructions in the skill body.** Quorum review is simply ddx agent run --quorum=<policy> --harnesses=<list> — each harness decides for itself whether to run that in a forked context (Claude: context: fork / .claude/agents/) or inline. Portability over optimization.

Source material: existing /skills/ddx-review/SKILL.md + cli/cmd/bead_review.go for the bead-review output contract. Link to the CLI commands, don't reimplement the grading logic here.

In-scope files:
- skills/ddx/reference/review.md (new)

Out-of-scope:
- skills/ddx/SKILL.md (separate bead)
- Other reference/*.md files (separate beads)
- library/personas/code-reviewer.md (Phase 3)
title: Create skills/ddx/reference/review.md — bead review and quorum review

acceptance: execute-bead synthesizes a deterministic prompt file from bead data when `--prompt` is omitted; the synthesized prompt includes title, description, acceptance, and available governing metadata instead of only the bead id; execute-loop inherits that contextual prompt automatically; `--prompt` still overrides synthesis; and automated tests fail if the default prompt regresses to the bare `Work on bead <id>.` fallback or if the runner stops receiving the synthesized context.
description:
<context-digest>
Review area: execute-bead default prompt construction. Evidence covers cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead.go falling back to the literal prompt `Work on bead <id>.`, FEAT-006 requiring execute-bead to resolve the bead and governing artifacts before launch, and a real no-change Codex run in /Users/erik/Projects/agent where the harness received only the bead id and wandered the repo.
</context-digest>
Fix execute-bead so omitted `--prompt` no longer produces an under-specified agent task.

## Required implementation
1. Replace the bare string fallback in `cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead.go` with deterministic prompt synthesis driven by the bead record.
2. Build the default prompt from durable bead context:
   - bead id and title
   - description
   - acceptance criteria
   - relevant metadata such as `spec-id`, parent, labels, and any declared governing references when available
3. Materialize that synthesized prompt as a prompt file in the managed execution worktree instead of relying on shell-fragile inline quoting.
4. Keep `--prompt` as an explicit override; only synthesize the default prompt when the caller did not supply one.
5. Make `execute-loop` inherit the same behavior automatically by continuing to call `execute-bead` without needing its own prompt-building fork.
6. Add regression coverage proving the default path no longer sends only `Work on bead <id>.` and that the runner receives the synthesized bead context.

## Boundaries
- Do not invent a new bead schema.
- Do not move prompt assembly into HELIX-specific logic.
- Use DDx-owned bead/store/docgraph context only.
notes: Implemented. buildPrompt() synthesizes from bead title/description/acceptance/governing refs when --prompt is omitted. Regression test at cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead_test.go:520 asserts the fallback 'Work on bead <id>.' prompt is never used.
title: Give execute-bead a deterministic bead-context prompt

acceptance: (1) Integration tests pass. (2) MANUAL for each: subscribe in GraphiQL, trigger the underlying event (execute a bead / run execute-loop), see events arrive. Record in completion note.
description: Implement subscription resolvers for executionEvidence(runID) and coordinatorMetrics(projectRoot). New file cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_sub_exec.go. Wire to land-coordinator event hook and execution run event stream. See docs/plan-svelte-migration.md Stage 2 bead #25.

events.body:
Opus review of 6910c2e.

EXECUTIONEVIDENCE: resolver_sub_exec.go:39-90 polls r.ExecLogs.GetExecLog(runID) every 500ms. Adapter at graphql_adapters.go:13-16 delegates to ddxexec.NewStore(workingDir).Log(runID) reading real run bundle. When available, splits by newlines, emits each as ExecutionEvent with seq-based EventID, then closes channel. ONE-SHOT dump (waits for run completion, then streams all lines at once) — not live tail. Acceptable given schema name is "executionEvidence" (final artifact), not "executionTail". Limitation worth documenting upstream.

COORDINATORMETRICS: resolver_sub_exec.go:96-150 polls CoordMetrics.GetCoordinatorMetrics(projectRoot) every MetricsPollInterval (default 1s). Adapter at graphql_adapters.go:24-37 calls reg.MetricsFor(projectRoot) on real workers.LandCoordinators registry. Diff with prev via snap != last, emits only on change. 1s interval appropriate for land events (seconds-to-minutes timescale).

BOTH TESTS REAL: httptest.NewServer + full graphql-transport-ws handshake. Coordinator test uses atomic.Pointer[CoordinatorMetricsSnap] flipped mid-test to simulate registry updates — verifies diff emission across snap1 -> snap2.

GRAPHQL_ADAPTERS.GO: proper import-cycle workaround. graphql package defines ExecLogProvider/CoordinatorMetricsProvider interfaces + CoordinatorMetricsSnap DTO; outer server package provides concrete implementations (execLogAdapter, coordMetricsAdapter). Standard pattern for nested resolver wiring.

MINOR (non-blocking):
1. executionEvidence polls forever until ctx cancels if run never completes — correct but worth docs
2. 3 separate test helpers (newGraphQLHandlerWithBeadBus/ExecLogs/CoordMetrics) duplicate ~90 lines of setup — cosmetic dedupe opportunity (note in refactor bead)

VERDICT: ACCEPT.
events.summary: ACCEPT: both subscriptions poll real data sources with diff-based emission. executionEvidence streams real ddxexec.Store log once after run completes (500ms poll for availability); coordinatorMetrics polls real workers.LandCoordinators registry every 1s with snapshot diff. Both tests use full graphql-transport-ws WebSocket handshakes. graphql_adapters.go is proper import-cycle workaround.

title: Stage 2.12: Subscription resolvers — executionEvidence + coordinatorMetrics

acceptance: In a scratch dir that ran ddx init under an older DDx version (simulated by creating .claude/skills/ddx-run/ and .claude/skills/ddx-doctor/ with old content, plus an AGENTS.md with stale 'symlinks' wording), running ddx update: (a) replaces .claude/skills/ddx/ contents with current embedded content; (b) removes .claude/skills/ddx-run/ and .claude/skills/ddx-doctor/; (c) updates the AGENTS.md DDx block to current wording without clobbering user content outside markers. cd cli && go test ./cmd -run TestUpdate passes.
description:
When a project ran ddx init under an older DDx version and the user now runs ddx update (or ddx upgrade followed by ddx update), the shipped skill should refresh in-place without re-running init.

Changes required:
1. In cli/cmd/update.go, after pulling plugin updates, re-run the skill-copy step that init uses (see the sibling 'Update ddx init' bead for the copy + stale-cleanup logic). Refactor the shared copy code into a helper so init and update both call it; do not duplicate.
2. Remove stale ddx-prefixed skill dirs per the same allowlist used by init (ddx-bead, ddx-run, ddx-agent, ddx-review, ddx-status, ddx-doctor, ddx-install, ddx-release across .claude/skills/, .agents/skills/, .ddx/skills/).
3. Log which files changed in the skill tree so the user can see the refresh happened (e.g., 'Refreshed .claude/skills/ddx/SKILL.md', 'Removed stale .claude/skills/ddx-run/').
4. AGENTS.md merge: the marker-based injector handles re-injection naturally. ddx update should also re-run the AGENTS.md merge so any updates to the DDx block (new wording, new policy) are picked up.

In-scope files:
- cli/cmd/update.go
- cli/cmd/update_test.go (new or updated)
- Shared helper extracted from cli/cmd/init.go (placement TBD — likely cli/internal/skills/install.go or similar)

Out-of-scope:
- ddx upgrade (binary update) — separate concern, already handled by cli/cmd/upgrade.go
- Plugin update logic — already in update.go

Prerequisite: the 'Update ddx init' bead lands first and extracts the shared helper.
title: Update ddx update to refresh .claude/skills/ddx/ and remove stale dirs

acceptance: (1) MCP tools ddx_list_projects and ddx_show_project are registered and functional. (2) Existing MCP tools that read project-local data (ddx_list_documents, ddx_read_document, ddx_search, ddx_list_beads, ddx_show_bead, ddx_doc_graph, etc.) accept an optional project parameter for multi-project disambiguation. (3) When exactly one project is registered, tools work without project parameter (singleton compat). (4) cd cli && go test ./internal/server -run 'TestMCP' -count=1 passes with at least one test covering project-aware MCP tool selection.
description:
## Scope
Add project-awareness to the MCP tool layer per SD-019 requirements.

## Context
FEAT-002/SD-019 requires ddx_list_projects, ddx_show_project, and project-aware selection semantics for all existing project-local MCP tools. Currently, all 18 MCP tools operate against the single working directory without project scoping.

## Implementation approach
1. Add ddx_list_projects tool — returns project registry entries
2. Add ddx_show_project tool — returns single project detail by ID
3. Add optional "project" parameter to all project-local tools
4. When project param is provided, resolve via GetProjectByID and use that project's path
5. When omitted and exactly one project registered, auto-resolve (singleton compat)
6. When omitted and >1 project registered, return error requesting disambiguation
7. Add MCP-specific tests

## Depends on
ddx-36d2775f (route middleware — shares projectResolver logic)

## Files to touch
- cli/internal/server/mcp.go (or mcp_handler.go) — tool registration + project param
- cli/internal/server/server_test.go or mcp_test.go — MCP project tests
title: Add project-scoped MCP tools: ddx_list_projects, ddx_show_project, and project-aware tool selection

acceptance: A framing design exists for the future service-backed DDx topology, defining central storage/UI vs headless node-gateway responsibilities, git-authoritative vs service-backed data boundaries, degraded local operation when the service is unavailable, and migration from the one-machine multi-project server stage
description:
<context-digest>
Review area: future ddx server control-plane topology. Evidence covers FEAT-002, FEAT-013, architecture.md, and the requirement for a central UI/storage layer with multiple headless servers managing many projects and workers.
</context-digest>
Frame the post-local topology for DDx after the one-machine multi-project server lands.

## Goals
1. Define the boundary between git-authoritative project state and any future service-backed storage/read model
2. Describe a topology with one central storage/control service, one central web UI, and multiple headless `ddx server` node gateways
3. Define what a node gateway owns: project discovery/registration, worker supervision, local execution, and synchronization with the central service
4. Define failure modes, degraded operation, and migration path so local multi-project servers continue to work when the central service is unavailable
5. Keep this as framing only until the one-machine multi-project module is designed and accepted
title: Frame future service-backed multi-node ddx control-plane topology

acceptance:
(1) ClosureGate in cli/internal/bead enforces close invariants ONLY on the automated execution path (execute-loop + Store.CloseWithEvidence): terminal verdict event present (kind=review with APPROVE + non-empty rationale, or kind=review-skipped with reason), execution-evidence event present (base_rev + result_rev). Gate applies to CloseWithEvidence; it does not wrap Store.Close (manual tracker administration remains a separate concern).
(2) Empty-bodied APPROVE verdicts rejected by the gate with a clear error. This is the axon-c5cc071a failure mode.
(3) Table-driven unit test covers at minimum: no execution event, no verdict, malformed verdict, success without result_rev, APPROVE with empty rationale.
(4) Replay of axon-c5cc071a's closure scenario through CloseWithEvidence rejects the close.
description:
Sibling to ddx-738edf47. Codex review of the sequencing plan flagged that the false-closure bug has a broader fix than just its pinpoint: the execute-loop has no invariant check enforcing that every `status: closed` transition carries execution evidence plus a parseable terminal verdict (review APPROVE, review SKIPPED_BY_FLAG, or manual close).

Reporter context: axon bead `axon-c5cc071a` closed in 4m33s with `events: []`, no bundle, no commit — the closure path was walked without any gate.

## Work

1. Identify every call site in cli/internal/agent and cli/internal/server that transitions a bead to `status: closed`. At minimum: execute-loop happy path, reviewer APPROVE path, `--no-review` skip path, manual `ddx bead close`.
2. Centralize closure through a single `ClosureGate` (or similar) that refuses to close unless:
   - execute-bead has a success event referencing a base_rev/result_rev pair, AND
   - a terminal verdict event exists (kind=review with summary APPROVE, or kind=review-skipped with an explicit reason, or kind=manual-close with actor set).
3. Add a table-driven invariant test that constructs synthetic bead states (missing execution evidence, missing verdict, malformed verdict, manual close) and asserts the gate's verdict.
4. Explicit escape hatch for `ddx bead close --force` (manual operator close) — logged with actor + reason, bypasses the gate.

## Relationship to ddx-738edf47

738edf47 is the specific false-closure symptom report + suspected root cause. This bead is the structural invariant test that catches similar regressions regardless of which upstream component (reviewer, execute-loop, worker) walks the close path incorrectly. Both can land together; this one owns the test harness, 738edf47 owns the repro fix.
title: exec: invariant test — no bead close without execution evidence + valid review/skip verdict

acceptance:
1. docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-015-resolution-path-trace.md exists and documents all 10 layers above with file:line citations current as of the document's creation date.

2. The document contains a sequence diagram showing CLI -> NormalizeRouteRequest -> Discovery -> BuildCandidatePlans -> SelectBestCandidate -> Run -> RunAgent -> agent library -> HTTP.

3. The document contains a 'Current Gaps' table with at least the 4 confirmed gaps from epic ddx-2d974641 and links to the tracking beads.

4. FEAT-006 and skills/ddx/reference/agents.md each have a one-line pointer to the new doc.

5. **CI grep verifies citations resolve** (per opus fresh-eyes review). A CI step (or lefthook hook) parses every file:line citation in SD-015-resolution-path-trace.md and verifies the named symbol/line pattern exists in the current source tree. Prevents the doc from rotting silently as routing.go / discovery.go / types.go evolve. Failing citations block the PR.
description:
## Problem

No single document traces the provider/model/harness resolution path from CLI flags to the final HTTP request. SD-015 and SD-023 are SPECS — they describe what should happen in 5 routing modes. The code lives across routing.go, discovery.go, agent_runner.go, runner.go, and agent_cmd.go. A newcomer debugging a routing bug must infer the flow by reading all of them.

The audit for epic ddx-2d974641 turned up a RouteRequest struct missing its Provider field, and a parallel RunOptions.Provider path that bypasses routing — neither of which is documented. This is the kind of hidden structural defect a Resolution Path document would have flagged on first read.

## Fix

Write docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-015-resolution-path-trace.md (or similar location; coordinate with SD-015's author). The document traces one concrete invocation end-to-end:

    ddx work --once --local --provider vidar-omlx --model-ref qwen/qwen3.6

Walk through every layer, with file:line citations:

1. **CLI parse** — cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go flag extraction (line references).

2. **RouteFlags -> RouteRequest normalization** — routing.go:NormalizeRouteRequest. What each CLI flag maps to; which flags are discarded when they don't match an expected shape; the current gap around --provider.

3. **Discovery probe** — discovery.go:DiscoverProviderModels. When it fires; what it caches; the 30s TTL; how failures are handled.

4. **Candidate planning** — routing.go:BuildCandidatePlans / evaluateCandidate. For each registered harness, the state map lookup and the viability checks.

5. **Fuzzy matching** — discovery.go:FuzzyMatchModel. Pool selection (global vs per-provider), normalization (current gaps), prefix + tiebreak algorithm.

6. **Scoring** — routing.go:scoreCandidate. Per-profile scoring, cost class, historical success rate, provider-affinity bonus (when implemented).

7. **Candidate ranking** — routing.go:RankCandidates / SelectBestCandidate. Non-viable rejection, stable tiebreak.

8. **Dispatch** — runner.go:Run. Harness-specific branches (virtual, agent, script, HTTP-provider-embedded, default exec). For embedded: agent_runner.go:RunAgent -> resolveEmbeddedAgentProvider -> agent library call.

9. **Wire-level request** — ddx-agent builds the HTTP body (system_prompt + messages + tools), sends to the provider. What the server receives.

10. **Response parsing** — streaming or non-streaming decode; where 'unexpected end of JSON input' (ddx-6a5dfe35) surfaces.

Include a SEQUENCE DIAGRAM (mermaid or ASCII) showing the layers.

Include a GAP TABLE at the top listing every currently-missing piece (as of whatever the SD-015 implementation state is) with links to the specific beads tracking each gap.

Keep the document MAINTAINED alongside the code: update it when RouteRequest gains a field, when a new routing mode lands, when fuzzy match gains a normalization step.

## Files likely touched

- docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-015-resolution-path-trace.md (new)
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-006-agent-service.md — add a single-line pointer at the top of the 'Overview' section
- skills/ddx/reference/agents.md — add a one-line pointer in 'Harness / profile / persona dispatch' section so agents consulting the skill can follow up to the full trace when needed

## Out of scope

- Rewriting SD-015 or SD-023 — this doc complements them; they describe intent, this describes current reality.
- Documenting future routing modes that haven't landed.

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01B6AbPTsfUEXdAmLmB1gcKk","type":"tool_result","content":"--- ddx: id: SD-015-trace depends_on: - SD-015 - FEAT-006 --- # Resolution Path Trace: CLI Flag → HTTP Request **Companion doc to** [SD-015 Agent Routing and Catalog Resolution](SD-015-agent-routing-and-catalog-resolution.md) and [SD-023 Agent Routing Visibility](SD-023-agent-routing-visibility.md). SD-015 and SD-023 describe *intent* — what the routing layer should do across five routing modes. This document describes *reality* — how one concrete CLI invocation flows through every layer of the code today, with file:line citations. It exists so a newcomer debugging a routing bug can read one document instead of reverse-engineering the flow from `routing.go`, `discovery.go`, `agent_runner.go`, `runner.go`, and `agent_cmd.go`. **Author's invocation used throughout this trace:** ```bash ddx work --once --local --provider vidar-omlx --model-ref qwen/qwen3.6 ``` **Citations are anchored to base revision `654b4cdcd9200fa8db6c38d6af9dad2acfbaa779` (2026-04-18).** Line numbers drift as the code evolves — see the *CI citation check* section at the end for how this document is kept in sync. ## Current Gaps (as of 2026-04-18) Each gap links to its tracking bead. Update this table when a bead closes or a new structural defect is discovered. | # | Gap | Visible at | Tracking bead | |---|-----|------------|---------------| | 1 | `RouteRequest` has no `Provider` field; `--provider` is silently dropped during normalization unless it names a registered harness. | `cli/internal/agent/types.go:337` (struct def); `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:305` (only consumed as harness alias) | [ddx-8610020e](#) — routing: add Provider field to RouteRequest | | 2 | `RunOptions.Provider` is a parallel path consumed by `resolveHarness()` and `resolveNativeAgentProvider()` — bypasses the routing layer entirely for the embedded-agent harness. | `cli/internal/agent/runner.go:382`; `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:519` | [ddx-2f5a2284](#) — ModelRef/Profile paths must consult discovery for `--provider` + catalog override | | 3 | `FuzzyMatchModel` does no case-folding and no canonical-prefix normalization. `qwen/qwen3.6` will not match `Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-4bit` because the slash, vendor prefix, and case are treated literally. | `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:76` | [ddx-0486e601](#) — fuzzy matcher lacks case + canonical-prefix normalization | | 4 | `scoreCandidate` has no provider-affinity bonus: when two harnesses can serve the same discovered model, the scorer does not prefer the harness whose provider actually advertised the model. | `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:477` | [ddx-3c5ba7cc](#) — tier escalation must respect `--provider` affinity | | 5 | Fuzzy-match tiebreak picks shortest-suffix, not highest-version. `qwen3` against `[qwen3.5-27b, qwen3.6-35b]` picks `qwen3.5`. | `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:100` | [ddx-0216b966](#) — discovery fuzzy match tiebreak | | 6 | No pre-dispatch capability check — context window, tool support, effort, permissions. | `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:396` (`rejectIfNotViable` covers availability only) | [ddx-4817edfd](#) — pre-dispatch capability check | ## Sequence Diagram ```mermaid sequenceDiagram actor User participant CLI as cli/cmd/work.go + agent_cmd.go participant Norm as NormalizeRouteRequest
(routing.go:289) participant Disc as DiscoverProviderModels
(discovery.go:124) participant Plan as BuildCandidatePlans
(routing.go:27) participant Fuzzy as FuzzyMatchModel
(discovery.go:66) participant Score as scoreCandidate
(routing.go:477) participant Sel as SelectBestCandidate
(routing.go:556) participant Run as Runner.Run
(runner.go:141) participant RunAgent as Runner.RunAgent
(agent_runner.go:65) participant Resolve as resolveEmbeddedAgentProvider
(agent_runner.go:464) participant AgentLib as agentlib.Run
(agent_runner.go:320) participant HTTP as Provider HTTP endpoint User->>CLI: ddx work --local --provider vidar-omlx --model-ref qwen/qwen3.6 CLI->>Norm: RouteFlags{...} Note over Norm: --provider consumed only
if it names a registered harness
(GAP #1) Norm-->>CLI: RouteRequest{ModelPin, Profile} CLI->>Disc: DiscoverProviderModels(workDir, timeout) Note over Disc: 30s process-wide cache Disc-->>CLI: DiscoveryResult{Providers[]} CLI->>Plan: BuildCandidatePlans(req, states) loop each harness Plan->>Plan: evaluateCandidate Plan->>Plan: rejectIfNotViable end Plan->>Fuzzy: FuzzyMatchModel (when uncataloged) Fuzzy-->>Plan: resolved model ID Plan->>Score: scoreCandidate per plan Plan-->>Sel: []CandidatePlan (ranked) Sel-->>CLI: best plan CLI->>Run: Runner.Run(RunOptions) Run->>RunAgent: if harnessName == "agent" RunAgent->>Resolve: resolveEmbeddedAgentProvider(wd, model, provider, modelRef) Resolve->>Disc: second-pass lookup (cached) Resolve-->>RunAgent: {Provider, BaseURL, APIKey, Model} RunAgent->>AgentLib: agentlib.Run(ctx, Request{...}) AgentLib->>HTTP: POST /v1/chat/completions HTTP-->>AgentLib: stream | unexpected EOF AgentLib-->>RunAgent: Result RunAgent-->>User: Result (stdout + session log) ``` ## Layer 1 — CLI flag parse `ddx work` is a thin alias for `ddx agent execute-loop`. All flags are cloned from the inner command. - `cli/cmd/work.go:10` — `newWorkCommand` constructs the alias. - `cli/cmd/work.go:40` — flags are cloned verbatim from `newAgentExecuteLoopCommand`. - `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1319` — `--harness` flag declaration. - `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1320` — `--model` flag declaration. - `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1321` — `--provider` flag declaration. - `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1322` — `--model-ref` flag declaration. - `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1327` — `--local` flag declaration. - `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1338` — `runAgentExecuteLoop` entry point reads flags. - `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1342` through `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1357` — flag values are plucked into local variables (`harness`, `model`, `provider`, `modelRef`, `effort`, `local`). - `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1374` — `--local` branch takes the inline path; without it the loop submits to the running DDx server. - `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1384` — `ValidateForExecuteLoop(harness, model, provider, modelRef)` runs before any bead is claimed. The flag values eventually reach `ExecuteBead`, which in turn builds `RunOptions`: - `cli/internal/agent/types.go:62` — `RunOptions` struct definition. - `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:498` — `runner.Run(RunOptions{...})` constructs the options, including `Provider: opts.Provider` on line 504. **Observation:** `RouteFlags` (`cli/internal/agent/types.go:51`) has a `Provider` field; so does `RunOptions` (`cli/internal/agent/types.go:74`). They are the raw CLI inputs. The gap is not here — it is at the normalization boundary (Layer 2). ## Layer 2 — Normalize flags into `RouteRequest` The normalization boundary converts raw CLI flags into the minimal routing ask that the routing engine evaluates. - `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:289` — `NormalizeRouteRequest(flags RouteFlags, cfg Config, catalog *Catalog) RouteRequest`. - `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:294` — `RouteRequest{Effort, Permissions}` initial struct. Note the absence of `Provider`. - `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:303` — `flags.Harness != ""` branch assigns `HarnessOverride` directly. - `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:305` — `flags.Provider != ""` branch: the provider flag is **only** consulted as a harness-alias lookup. If `--provider vidar-omlx` does not match any entry in `builtinHarnesses`, the value is silently discarded. - `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:318` — `flags.Model != ""` branch routes the model through `catalog.NormalizeModelRef` to produce either `ModelRef` (known in catalog) or `ModelPin` (exact, bypasses catalog policy). - `cli/internal/agent/types.go:337` — `RouteRequest` struct definition. Fields: `Profile`, `ModelRef`, `ModelPin`, `Effort`, `Permissions`, `HarnessOverride`. **No `Provider` field — see Gap #1.** **Concrete outcome for our invocation:** `--provider vidar-omlx` does not name a registered harness, so the provider flag is dropped at `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:305`. `--model-ref qwen/qwen3.6` flows through `NormalizeModelRef`; since it is not in the shared catalog, it becomes a `ModelPin`. The resulting `RouteRequest` carries only `{ModelPin: "qwen/qwen3.6"}`. ## Layer 3 — Discovery probe - `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:117` — `init()` sets the process-wide cache TTL to `30 * time.Second`. - `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:124` — `DiscoverProviderModels(workDir, timeout)` entry. - `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:125` through `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:130` — cache hit returns the cached result if still fresh. - `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:141` through `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:155` — probes every configured provider via `providerstatus.Probe`; silently skips unreachable ones (partial failure is tolerated). - `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:162` — cache write under mutex. - `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:170` — `InvalidateDiscoveryCache` is the only way to force a re-probe in tests. Discovery is invoked on the routing hot-path only when a `ModelPin` is not in the catalog: - `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:576` — in `ProbeAndBuildCandidatePlans`, the discovery probe fires when `req.ModelPin != "" && req.ModelRef == "" && r.Discovery == nil`. Discovery is invoked a second time on the dispatch path: - `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:521` — in `resolveNativeAgentProvider`, `r.Discovery.ProvidersForModel(model)` looks up which providers advertised the requested model. ## Layer 4 — Candidate planning - `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:27` — `BuildCandidatePlans(req, stateOverride)` entry. - `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:33` through `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:46` — iterates every registered harness. `TestOnly` harnesses (script, virtual) are skipped unless `req.HarnessOverride` names them explicitly — this is the guard that prevents `script` from leaking into standard-tier fallback chains (see ddx-869848ec in the comment on line 40). - `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:47` — calls `evaluateCandidate` per harness. - `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:106` — `evaluateCandidate(name, harness, req, stateOverride)`. - `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:396` — `rejectIfNotViable(plan, req)` runs the viability gate. descending score; tiebreak prefers local-cost candidates, then alphabetical harness name. - `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:556` — `SelectBestCandidate(plans)` returns the first viable plan, or `"no viable harness candidate: all harnesses rejected"`. - `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:567` — `ProbeAndBuildCandidatePlans` is the full-probe wrapper used by actual dispatch (it calls live `ProbeHarnessState` per harness before building plans). ## Layer 8 — Dispatch `Runner.Run` is the dispatch fork: - `cli/internal/agent/runner.go:141` — `Run(opts RunOptions) (*Result, error)` entry. - `cli/internal/agent/runner.go:148` — `resolveHarness(opts)` picks the harness. - `cli/internal/agent/runner.go:376` — `resolveHarness(opts)` definition. - `cli/internal/agent/runner.go:382` — **Gap #2 origin**: if `opts.Harness == ""` and `opts.Provider != ""`, the `--provider` value is looked up as a harness alias. Branches: - `openai-compat` or `openai` (line 691): `oai.New(oai.Config{BaseURL, APIKey, Model})`. - `anthropic` (line 697): `anthropic.New(anthropic.Config{APIKey, Model})`. - `virtual` (line 702): `virtual.New(virtual.Config{DictDir})`. For `vidar-omlx`, the native-config resolution at `resolveNativeAgentProvider` loads the provider block from `~/.config/agent/config.yaml` or `.agent/config.yaml`. The HTTP layer is OpenAI-compatible; the request body is the standard `/v1/chat/completions` payload built inside the agent library, with tool schemas appended. Embedded agent dispatch: - `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:65` — `RunAgent(opts)`. - `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:94` — `resolveEmbeddedAgentProvider(wd, model, opts.Provider, opts.ModelRef)`. - `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:464` — `resolveEmbeddedAgentProvider` entry. - `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:493` — `resolveNativeAgentProvider(workDir, model, explicitProvider, explicitModelRef)`. - `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:519` — **Gap #2 again**: `explicitProvider != ""` overrides the default provider with the raw `opts.Provider` string. This is where `--provider vidar-omlx` finally re-enters the flow, but by now the routing engine has already ranked candidates without knowing about it. - `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:521` through `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:548` — discovery-based provider resolution: if the model isn't in the catalog and discovery has results, route to the first provider that advertises the model (exact or fuzzy). - `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:555` — `cfg.BuildProviderWithOverrides(providerName, overrides)` materializes the provider object from the native agent config. ## Layer 9 — Wire-level HTTP request The embedded agent library builds the request body and dispatches to the provider endpoint. DDx hands off at `agentlib.Run`. - `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:127` — system prompt construction via `prompt.NewFromPreset(agentCfg.Preset).WithTools(tools).WithContextFiles(...).WithWorkDir(wd).Build()`. - `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:153` — `agentlib.Request` assembly: `Prompt`, `SystemPrompt`, `Provider`, `Tools`, `MaxIterations`, `WorkDir`, `Metadata`, `Compactor`. - `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:320` — `agentResult, err := agentlib.Run(ctx, req)` — handoff into the `github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent` library. DDx does not own the HTTP transport from here on. - `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:688` — `buildAgentProvider(cfg)` creates the provider used by the request. Branches: - `openai-compat` or `openai` (line 691): `oai.New(oai.Config{BaseURL, APIKey, Model})`. - `anthropic` (line 697): `anthropic.New(anthropic.Config{APIKey, Model})`. - `virtual` (line 702): `virtual.New(virtual.Config{DictDir})`. For `vidar-omlx`, the native-config resolution at `resolveNativeAgentProvider` loads the provider block from `~/.config/agent/config.yaml` or `.agent/config.yaml`. The HTTP layer is OpenAI-compatible; the request body is the standard `/v1/chat/completions` payload built inside the agent library, with tool schemas appended. The agent library exposes its wire-level dump via the `AGENT_DEBUG_WIRE` environment variable (introduced in ddx-agent v0.3.12 — see ddx-a0f7a4bb). ## Layer 10 — Response parsing - `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:320` — same line; `agentlib.Run` returns `(AgentResult, error)` and DDx inspects the result starting at line 323. - `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:336` through `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:349` — maps `agentResult.Tokens`, `agentResult.ToolCalls`, `agentResult.SelectedProvider`, etc. into DDx `Result`. - `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:355` — wall-clock deadline → error. - `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:358` — idle timeout → error. - `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:361` — compaction-stuck circuit breaker → error. - `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:364` — stall detector (30 consecutive read-only tool calls) → error. - `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:367` — generic agent error, including the `ErrProviderRequestTimeout` wrapper (line 369). - `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:380` — agent non-success status (e.g. `StatusCancelled`). The `unexpected end of JSON input` error reported in ddx-6a5dfe35 surfaces from inside the agent library's streaming decoder; DDx sees it via the generic error branch at line 367 and forwards the message unchanged. The agent library owns the decode (DDx does not duplicate JSON parsing at this layer). ## Cross-References - [SD-015 Agent Routing and Catalog Resolution](SD-015-agent-routing-and-catalog-resolution.md) — intent/specification. - [SD-023 Agent Routing Visibility](SD-023-agent-routing-visibility.md) — routing evidence and `ddx agent doctor` projections. - [FEAT-006 Agent Service](../../01-frame/features/FEAT-006-agent-service.md) — the enclosing feature. ## Maintenance — CI citation check This document's value depends on its citations matching reality. To add a new citation: cite as `path/to/file.go:NNN` in the body of this document. The script parses the backtick-quoted `path:line` patterns and runs `awk 'NR==NNN'` on the file. If the line does not exist, the hook fails. -f "$path" ]; then echo "citations: MISSING FILE: $cite" >&2 failures=$((failures + 1)) continue fi # Line count check; awk 'END{print NR}' counts lines portably. nlines=$(awk 'END{print NR}' "$path") if [ "$line" -gt "$nlines" ]; then echo "citations: LINE OUT OF RANGE: $cite (file has $nlines lines)" >&2 failures=$((failures + 1)) continue fi # Sanity check: the line must not be blank, since every real citation # points at a struct, func, or statement. content=$(awk -v n="$line" 'NR==n' "$path") if [ -z "$(echo "$content" | tr -d '[:space:]')" ]; then echo "citations: BLANK LINE: $cite" >&2 failures=$((failures + 1)) continue fi done <<< "$citations" if [ "$failures" -gt 0 ]; then echo "citations: $failures/$total failed for $DOC_PATH" >&2 exit 1 fi echo "citations: $total/$total ok for $DOC_PATH"","is_error":false}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"d932d409-7007-4d29-af52-a78f704e35c0","uuid":"465c02eb-3d0f-4e0e-9149-057b595f6ab5","timestamp":"2026-04-18T06:48:07.386Z","tool_use_result":{"stdout":"#!/usr/bin/env bash # Verifies every `path:line` citation in SD-015-resolution-path-trace.md # points to a file that exists and a line that exists. description: Server execute-loop path in cli/internal/server/workers.go (around line 388-395) builds a LandRequest via BuildLandRequestFromResult and submits it directly through coordinator.Submit, bypassing LandBeadResult and gate evaluation entirely. Land() is intentionally a pure ref-advance contract and should not learn about gates. Choose one of two designs: (a) extend the coordinator pattern so Submit orchestrates gate-eval -> land-or-preserve, or (b) run gate evaluation upstream in workers.go before submission and convert the request to preserve-only when a required gate fails. Manifest path is available from the ExecuteBead result; reuse ExtractGoverningIDsFromManifest.

acceptance:
1. cli/internal/agent/model_catalog_yaml.go (or equivalent) declares an 'omlx' surface with at least the six canonical refs listed above mapping to their server-side IDs on vidar-omlx.

2. Catalog lookup is case-insensitive for the omlx surface (matches per v0.3.11 LookupModelLimits requirement).

3. Unit test in cli/internal/agent/ asserts that for at least 'qwen/qwen3.6' and 'qwen/qwen3.5', catalog.ContextWindowForModel() and the surface lookup return the omlx concrete ID (not the 'embedded-openai' lmstudio-shaped ID).

4. Canonical refs with no omlx target are rejected at route-plan time with a clear diagnostic ('ref X has no omlx target; vidar-omlx offers: [list]'), not a downstream 404 from the server.

5. With the catalog change, 'ddx work --once --local --provider vidar-omlx --model-ref qwen/qwen3.6' resolves to Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-4bit at the routing layer (the downstream dispatch may still hit the parse bug — fixing that is ddx-6a5dfe35's concern).
description:
## Superseded — wrong diagnosis

Filed under the belief that DDx needs hand-authored omlx surface entries in the model catalog to map qwen/qwen3.6 -> Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-4bit on vidar-omlx.

**This is not the right fix.** An audit of the resolution path (see parent epic ddx-2d974641) showed that the real defect is in fuzzy matching + the RouteRequest plumbing: the fuzzy matcher exists (discovery.go:FuzzyMatchModel) and provider discovery exists (DiscoverProviderModels probes /v1/models on each provider), but:

1. The matcher uses STRICT case-sensitive prefix — qwen/qwen3.6 can't match Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-4bit even semantically.
2. The matcher runs over a provider-union (d.AllModels()) that loses provider identity.
3. --provider is silently discarded at NormalizeRouteRequest (types.go:330 RouteRequest has no Provider field).
4. Catalog-resolved refs (ModelRef path) don't go through the fuzzy matcher at all.

Once those are fixed, no catalog entries are needed — canonical refs fuzzy-match against discovered omlx model IDs at runtime. See sibling beads filed under ddx-2d974641.

Closing as invalid: catalog-first approach would require hand-authoring entries for every new MLX model name format that appears, which defeats the purpose of discovery.
title: catalog: map shipped canonical model refs to omlx-flavor server IDs

acceptance: (1) Integration tests pass. (2) MANUAL: GraphiQL queries return real data. Cross-reference: worker results match `ddx server workers list`; session results match agent-logs/sessions.jsonl tail.

events.body:
Opus review of d979f4a.

REAL IMPLEMENTATION: resolver_agent.go implements 13 Query resolvers. Real sources wired: workers via state.GetWorkersGraphQL reading .ddx/workers/<id>/status.json (functionally equivalent to WorkerManager access, avoids lock), agentSessions via agent.ReadAllJSONL across registered projects, executions via ddxexec.NewStore per project.

SHAPE (flag only, not blocking — captured in refactor bead ddx-7e2f2cb9):
- slice-index cursors (extended pattern)
- 13 new if r.State == nil guards
- 13 new StateProvider methods (extension, not split)
- Uses *ddxgraphql.Worker directly (different shape from Projects/Beads Snapshot pattern)

ACCEPTANCE GAP: bead required "integration tests against real workers via server.WorkerManager / agent sessions store / execution runs store" — ZERO TestGraphQL* functions added in this commit. Underlying stores have their own tests (workers_test.go, server_test.go TestAgentSessions*, TestExec*), so no functional regression. Resolver-to-store path is unverified. This is systematic drift across Stage 2.5/2.6/2.7 and should be backfilled in a single follow-up bead.

BUILD+TEST: go build clean, go test ./internal/server/... -count=1 → ok 36.011s.

VERDICT: ACCEPT. Do not reopen. Test backfill bead will cover this.

Test backfill bead filed.

acceptance: (1) go.mod pins github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent v0.7.0. (2) go -C cli build ./... and go -C cli vet ./... pass. (3) ddx agent run --harness lmstudio --text ping exits 0 against at least one connected lmstudio-type provider. (4) ddx work --once picks a ready bead, routes via profiles, completes without the earlier 'no viable harness' / 'unknown provider' errors.
description:
## Sequencing

Run first within wave:1. All other wave:1 beads (004eccbc fixture capture, 672cf2bf work-loop UX, b9747ee3 registry audit) rebase on top of the v0.7.0 module bump if it breaks the build. Landing this first also lets 004eccbc capture fixtures against current-era reviewer output instead of pinning to v0.6.0-era and filing a follow-up recapture.

---

ddx-agent v0.7.0 is shipping (agent CHANGELOG 2026-04-20). Two consumer-relevant deltas since v0.6.0:

1. **Fixed**: HTTP provider-backed native harnesses (lmstudio, omlx, openrouter, ollama, openai) now dispatch through the concrete provider identity instead of by configured provider name. Unblocks our earlier failure: `agent: native config provider "lmstudio": config: unknown provider "lmstudio"`. (agent-117a0868)

2. **Changed**: `RouteRequest.ProviderPreference` removed from the public surface; profiles (`cheap`, `standard`, `smart`, user-defined) are now the public routing knob. `surface_policy` in the catalog can carry placement order, cost ceilings, failure policy, and reasoning defaults. (agent-285a05d2)

We never wrote any `ProviderPreference` code on the DDx side, so (2) is purely subtractive for us — nothing to remove.

## Work

1. `go -C cli get github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent@v0.7.0 && go -C cli mod tidy`.
2. Verify build: `go -C cli build ./...` — any compile breaks indicate hidden v0.7.0 API shape drift beyond what the changelog flags.
3. Verify no stray `ProviderPreference` references (expect none).
4. Confirm native harness dispatch: `ddx agent run --harness lmstudio --text ping` now succeeds against a connected lmstudio-type provider (bragi in our config).
5. Drain end-to-end: `ddx work` walks ready beads to completion using profiles for routing. Cheap tier should reach local lmstudio; standard/smart can escalate to openrouter.
6. If the catalog's `surface_policy` shape changed, sync `~/.config/agent/models.yaml` to the new schema and document any user-facing config migration in CHANGELOG-of-this-repo.

## Supersedes

- `ddx-4535f466` Track B (ResolveRoute audit to wire `ProviderPreference`) collapses to zero; the architectural argument landed upstream. Keep ddx-4535f466 open for Track A (test-fixture updates) only — those are orthogonal to the v0.7.0 bump.

## Blocks

- `ddx-bbb65768` (profile/automation: local-first default) — depends on v0.7.0's profile-owned placement.
- `ddx-4b59b816` (cost-tiered execution epic) — cheap/standard/smart routing is a precondition.

## Out of scope

- Reviewer malfunctions (ddx-f7ae036f, ddx-f8a11202, ddx-738edf47) — orthogonal to agent version.
- `ddx-672cf2bf` (work-loop silent-exit on epic-only queue) — DDx-local, no agent dependency.
title: ddx-agent v0.7.0 consumer integration: pull, verify, drain the queue

acceptance: RealLandingGitOps.SyncWorkTreeToHead takes a fromRev parameter. After read-tree HEAD + update-ref, the main worktree reflects the new HEAD for exactly the files changed by the landing commit (no phantom D or M entries), while unrelated local modifications (agent-logs, beads.jsonl heartbeat) are preserved. Both Land() fast-path and merge-path call sites pass the correct fromRev. FetchOriginAncestryCheck same. Interface + test stubs updated. go build ./... passes. go test ./internal/agent/... -count=1 passes. Manual verification: after the next worker land, running `git status --short` in the main worktree shows zero phantom entries related to the landing commit.
description:
RealLandingGitOps.SyncIndexToHead in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go only runs `git read-tree HEAD` after update-ref, which syncs the INDEX to HEAD but does NOT update files on disk in the main worktree. Every worker-landed commit that adds or modifies a tracked file leaves the main worktree showing phantom "D" or "M" entries in git status, even though HEAD is correct.

Impact during the Svelte+GraphQL migration epic: opus reviewers have been misled multiple times (reading stale working-tree copies instead of HEAD blobs), and I have manually run `git checkout -- <path>` after nearly every worker close (~6+ times this session). Subsequent agents that read files from the worktree see old content.

Scope of fix:
1. SyncIndexToHead -> SyncWorkTreeToHead. Take a fromRev parameter.
2. After read-tree HEAD, compute `git diff --name-only <fromRev> HEAD` to find the exact files changed by the landing commit
3. Run `git checkout-index -f -- <files>` to restore only those files (preserves unrelated local modifications like agent-logs and beads.jsonl heartbeats)
4. Apply to both call sites in Land() (fast-forward path and merge path) and the FetchOriginAncestryCheck update-ref block
5. Update LandingGitOps interface + test stubs

Alternative considered: git checkout-index -a -f (restore ALL files from index) — rejected because it would clobber legitimate local modifications to files unrelated to the landing commit (agent-logs, beads.jsonl heartbeat writes).

This is a P0 blocker for running the migration epic without constant human babysitting. Fix directly via deterministic edit, not via worker dispatch, because the surface is small, the fix is mechanical, and the diagnostic cost of a worker misunderstanding the fix is higher than the implementation cost of just applying it.

events.body:
Commit: 94f0a91

Renamed LandingGitOps.SyncIndexToHead -> SyncWorkTreeToHead with a new fromRev parameter. Implementation:
1. read-tree HEAD (syncs index — preserved)
2. git diff --name-only fromRev HEAD (enumerates the minimum set of files changed by the update-ref)
3. Partition: index-present files go through git checkout-index -f --; deleted-in-HEAD files go through os.Remove

This preserves unrelated local modifications (agent-logs, beads.jsonl heartbeat, operator scratch) by passing the exact file list rather than using checkout-index -a -f.

Call sites updated:
- Land() fast-forward path -> fromRev=currentTip
- Land() merge path -> fromRev=currentTip
- FetchOriginAncestryCheck origin-ahead branch -> fromRev=localSHA

Test coverage:
- TestLand_HappyPath_FastForward now asserts that feature.txt is materialized on disk AND git status --porcelain is clean for that path after Land() returns. This is the regression assertion for the bug.
- Existing Land unit tests + integration tests + coordinator tests all pass.

Build + vet + test clean.
events.summary: Fixed in commit 94f0a91: SyncIndexToHead renamed to SyncWorkTreeToHead with fromRev parameter, materializes only files changed by the landing commit, preserves unrelated local modifications

title: bug: Land() leaves main worktree stale — update-ref + read-tree doesn't materialize files

acceptance: FEAT-002, FEAT-013, architecture.md, and TP-002 describe one `ddx server` process managing multiple project roots on one machine with explicit project registry, request scoping, per-project isolation, and migration semantics from the current single-project host
description:
<context-digest>
Review area: ddx server topology. Evidence covers FEAT-002, FEAT-013, architecture.md, TP-002, and the current assumption that one `ddx server` serves exactly one repo.
</context-digest>
Design the first-step multi-project `ddx server` module for a single machine.

## Goals
1. Replace the single-project server assumption with a project registry / project-root collection managed by one server process on one machine
2. Define project-scoped API, MCP, and UI routing semantics so clients can enumerate projects and then operate within one selected project context
3. Define per-project config, cache, and adapter boundaries so one bad project cannot poison other served projects
4. Define how project-scoped worktrees and worker pools are managed under one server process without assuming only one active repository
5. Define migration from today's single-project `ddx server` invocation and data adapters without breaking current local use

## Required outputs
- Project registry/discovery contract
- Request routing and project selection model
- Per-project isolation and failure-mode analysis
- Impact on FEAT-008 UI and TP-002 end-to-end coverage
title: Design one-machine multi-project ddx server topology

events.body:
Stage 2.2 (node/projects resolvers, commit 7e4ce6a) established the resolver pattern cleanly but left five decisions open that affect every subsequent resolver bead. Lock these in at Stage 2.3 (beads + beadsByProject) before the pattern propagates to 2.4-2.7.

1. StateProvider interface split
   Current: single StateProvider in graphql/resolver_node.go with 3 methods (GetNodeSnapshot/GetProjectSnapshots/GetProjectSnapshotByID). Stage 2.3 will add GetBeads/GetBeadByID/GetBeadsByProject — the interface will balloon to 50+ methods across all stages. Recommendation: split into per-resolver-group sub-interfaces (NodeState, BeadState, DocumentState, WorkerState, etc.) each defined next to its resolver file. The top-level Resolver.State field embeds a composite interface that combines them. File: introduce state_provider.go with the composite interface and move the existing StateProvider methods into a new NodeState interface.

2. Stable-key cursors (Relay connections)
   Current: resolver_node.go uses encodeCursor/decodeCursor that emit base64("cursor:<slice-index>"). Slice-index cursors break under insert/delete between pages — fine for projects (rarely mutate) but broken for beads (mutated constantly). Stage 2.3 MUST use a stable-key cursor: base64 of (sort-key, id) or similar. Do not copy-paste the slice-index helpers from resolver_node.go; introduce stable-key helpers in a shared graphql/cursors.go and use them for beads/documents/commits/sessions.

3. Snapshot struct duplication vs direct domain types
   Current: graphql/resolver_node.go defines ProjectSnapshot that mirrors server.ProjectEntry field-for-field. Fine for 3 fields. Will become painful if every state type needs a shadow struct (Bead, Document, Worker, Session, ExecutionRun, Persona, Commit, ...). Decide now: either accept the snapshot pattern and live with duplication, OR let the graphql package depend on narrowly-factored state types directly. Pick ONE before Stage 2.3 so we do not end up with both patterns.

4. r.State == nil nil-check
   Current: resolver_node.go has if r.State == nil { return ...} guards in every method. Will copy-paste into 50 resolvers. Refactor: Resolver constructor rejects nil state, drop the runtime check. Trivial fix (one constructor), huge win in readability across 50 call sites.

5. node(id) prefix dispatch
   Current: node(id) switches on "node-"/"proj-" prefixes with an if-else chain. Stage 2.3 adds bead- prefix. Stage 2.4-2.7 add doc-, worker-, session-, exec-, persona- prefixes. The chain grows to 9 branches. Better: prefix-to-loader map (or a registry that each resolver file registers into from its init()). Lock this at 2.3 before the chain grows.

None of these are defects in 2.2. They are shape decisions that SHOULD be made at 2.3 to avoid technical debt accumulation across 2.4-2.7.
events.source: Stage 2.2 opus review shape concerns — propagated to 2.3
events.summary: Five load-bearing decisions from Stage 2.2 review that Stage 2.3 should lock in before the pattern propagates to 2.4-2.7

events.body:
stalled: no write activity after 30 consecutive read-only tool calls
result_rev=a009b4824d91969bd13e53e9afc9f0364e48268c
base_rev=a009b4824d91969bd13e53e9afc9f0364e48268c
retry_after=2026-04-15T04:50:24Z

This is a distinct failure mode from the long-context hang seen on Stage 2.2. Here the model is actively producing tool calls (reading files) but never commits to a write/edit, so the stall detector after 30 reads cancels the run. Symptom of context-window exhaustion: the model is re-reading the same files or exploring too broadly without ever reaching a decision. Not a 500. Not a hang. Not a loop abort.

Escalated to claude sonnet per user directive. Local model is not viable for code-heavy beads with the current prompt shape (plan doc + 1908-line schema + bead context). Future cost-tiered routing epic should capture: prompt-context pre-filtering for local models, AND a check for "N consecutive reads without a write" that kicks in earlier than the 30-read cap.
events.summary: qwen/qwen3-coder-next stalled: 30 consecutive read-only tool calls with no write activity — stall detector triggered

events.body:
Opus review of merge commit 2f46543 / rewrite 8fc5534.

SHAPE COMPLIANCE vs Stage 2.3 note (5 concerns, the note was on THIS bead):
1. StateProvider split — IGNORED. state_graphql.go:66 EXTENDED the monolithic interface with GetBeadSnapshots.
2. Stable-key cursors — CONTRADICTED (strict fail). resolver_beads.go:53 calls encodeCursor(i) slice-index helper. The bead that had the warning attached implemented precisely what the warning said not to do.
3. Snapshot struct vs domain types — IGNORED. Added BeadSnapshot + BeadDependencySnapshot full-copy in resolver_node.go:11-59 and state_graphql.go:61-87.
4. Nil-checks — IGNORED. resolver_beads.go:11-13 and :32-34 both start with if r.State == nil early-return.
5. node(id) prefix registry — IGNORED, AND node(id) does NOT handle bead- prefix. Functional gap: node(id: "bead-xxx") returns nil,nil despite beads being first-class now.

IMPLEMENTATION: correct. beads resolver handles first/after/last/before/status/label/projectID. beadsByProject delegates. hasNextPage logic correct (truncatedByFirst flag — genuine improvement over Projects resolver which lacks it). TestGraphQLBeadsPagination/StatusFilter/ByProject all PASS 0.033s.

ADDITIONAL GAP OPUS FLAGGED beyond the refactor bead (ddx-7e2f2cb9):
- Pagination test uses only 3 beads despite acceptance criterion requiring 15
- node(id) missing bead- prefix branch (functional not just shape)

Both added to refactor bead scope below.

description:
Per codex review: DDx routing_signal_tmux.go (with ReadClaudeQuotaViaTmux, ReadCodexQuotaViaTmux, ANSI strip, output parsers) is duplicated by agent internal/harnesses/{claude,codex}/quota_tmux.go (commit 8099293). DDx should not keep parallel tmux scrape parsers long-term.

Two paths:
A. Agent owns tmux scrape; DDx triggers refresh via service.HealthCheck(harness target). The agent does the tmux invocation internally; DDx never shells out tmux. Delete DDx routing_signal_tmux.go.
B. Remove the DDx-side doctor refresh path entirely. Quota state populates lazily via the agents normal probe cadence; doctor --routing reads from cache only.

Recommendation: A. service.HealthCheck(harness) already triggers a fresh probe internally (per the implementation report from agent-3afb76bb). The tmux refresh becomes implicit when DDx asks for HealthCheck on claude/codex. No new contract surface needed.

After this decision: routing_signal_tmux.go can delete from DDx (folded into the file-delete cascade); doctor --routing migration uses service.HealthCheck instead of explicit RefreshXQuotaViaTmuxForWorkDir.

description:
<context-digest>
Review area: embedded ddx-agent runtime observability and live worker progress. Evidence covers FEAT-006 execution/evidence ownership, FEAT-002 server activity surfaces, FEAT-008 web UI expectations, execute-loop worker supervision, and the need for DDx-owned progress telemetry beyond coarse session logs when DDx runs the embedded harness.
</context-digest>
Define the progress-event contract for managed embedded-agent runs so DDx can surface meaningful live execution status in the CLI and server/UI.

## Goals
- Define structured phase/heartbeat output for embedded-agent runs (queueing, launch, running, post-checks, landing, preserved, done)
- Define the minimal per-event fields needed for operator visibility and later reporting: worker/project/bead/run identity, phase, elapsed/heartbeat timing, harness/provider/model/profile, and token/cost deltas when available
- Clarify which live progress artifacts remain runtime-only and which summaries/indexes are exposed through DDx-owned APIs and UI surfaces
- Keep provider-native transcript ownership external; DDx progress output should be normalized runtime telemetry, not transcript duplication

## Required spec work
- Update FEAT-006, FEAT-002, and FEAT-008 so embedded-agent progress visibility is an explicit contract rather than an implementation detail
- Clarify how progress events relate to tracked execution artifacts, session logs, and server-managed worker state

## Required implementation planning
- Define the server/API/UI read model for live worker progress and recent phase history
- Define how CLI and UI consume the same structured progress feed without introducing hidden workflow policy

Replace the 7 sibling ddx-* skills with a single `ddx` skill using progressive disclosure (SKILL.md + reference/ subfiles) per the agentskills.io open standard. Portable across Claude Code, OpenAI Codex, Gemini CLI, and other skills-compatible harnesses.

Plan file: /home/erik/.claude/plans/let-s-review-the-skills-synchronous-kite.md (reference only — do not require reading it; beads inline the context).

Governing spec: docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-011-skills.md (rewritten to reflect this design).

Motivation: Today ddx init ships 2 of 7 embedded skills, skill trees have drifted between /skills/ and cli/internal/skills/, HELIX opinions leak into ddx-bead, and users must guess between /ddx-run vs /ddx-work vs /ddx-bead. The single-skill design routes every natural-language intent ("do work", "drain the queue", "review this") into the same skill, then into the right reference file.

This epic contains 10 child beads covering: (a) create SKILL.md + 5 reference/*.md files; (b) wire embed.go + Makefile copy-skills target; (c) update ddx init for single skill + stale-dir cleanup + AGENTS.md merge markers; (d) update ddx update refresh path; (e) ship eval suite (routing.jsonl + eval-skill.sh) for CI enforcement of cross-harness routing behavior.
