acceptance: (1) A regression test at cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_evidence_test.go (or execute_bead_land_test.go) runs a simulated successful execute-bead attempt against a real temp git repo and asserts: (a) after the run returns, the working tree is clean (git status --porcelain is empty), and (b) at least one file under .ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/ is present in git log --all --oneline for the attempt's result or successor commit. Test must fail on current HEAD (98e379b) and pass post-fix. (2) The same test uses bead tracker evidence to confirm the bead's closing_commit_sha still resolves after the merge (git cat-file -e). (3) cli/internal/server/workers_test.go TestWorkerManagerStartAndShow (or a sibling test) exercises the server-worker path and asserts the same clean-worktree guarantee. (4) Full test suite (cli: go test ./cmd ./internal/agent ./internal/server) passes. (5) Manual dogfooding: ddx agent execute-loop --once against a ready bead leaves git status --porcelain empty, and HEAD or HEAD^ contains at least one file under .ddx/executions/<attempt>/. (6) No changes to AGENTS.md, FEAT-006, or skills/ddx-bead/SKILL.md — the policy is already correct; this is implementation only.
description:
## Scope

execute-bead's orchestrator currently leaves the per-attempt evidence files it
materializes (`.ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/manifest.json`, `prompt.md`,
`result.json`, `checks.json`, `usage.json`) untracked after the agent run
returns. Successful bead commits reach main via the "Merge bead" path, but the
evidence sits in the working tree unstaged and orphaned. This bead closes that
gap: after the agent finishes, DDx (not the agent) stages the evidence and
returns the working tree clean.

Concrete evidence of the gap: commit 98e379b on main is a one-shot backfill
that imports 254 historical `.ddx/executions/<ts>-<hash>/` directories
(2026-04-11 → 2026-04-15) that were never staged by the execute-bead pipeline.
That backfill must not need to happen again.

## Files to Touch

- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go` — the land/merge flow. After the
  agent's commit is produced (or after `Merge()` / bare-ref update), and
  before returning `ExecuteBeadReport`, stage the per-attempt evidence dir and
  fold it into the merge. Two shapes are acceptable:
  (a) amend the evidence into the bead's own commit (agent's commit + DDx's
      evidence files, single commit), OR
  (b) emit a trailing `chore: add execution evidence [<attempt-id>]` commit
      on the working branch as a sibling to the bead's commit, in the same
      merge transaction.
  Choose (b) if amending the agent's commit would rewrite its SHA — the agent
  commit's SHA is referenced by `session_id` / `closing_commit_sha` and must
  not move. Choose (a) only if the evidence can be staged BEFORE the agent
  commits (e.g. the agent is told via `git add` hooks to pick up a DDx-seeded
  index). Pragmatic recommendation: (b).
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go` — the per-run orchestrator entry. After
  the executor returns, verify the working tree is clean; if it is not,
  diagnose untracked files introduced by this run, stage them into the
  trailing evidence commit (if they are under `.ddx/executions/<attempt>/`),
  and fail the run with a clear error otherwise ("orchestrator left worktree
  dirty: <path>").
- `cli/internal/server/workers.go` — the server-worker path (`runWorker`,
  `buildAgentRunner`). Same contract applies: after `ExecuteBead` returns and
  `LandBeadResult` is called, the worker must verify the worktree is clean
  before declaring the attempt complete. If the land flow is still dropping
  evidence, surface that as a test failure, not a silent leak.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land_test.go` OR a new
  `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_evidence_test.go` — regression tests.

## Files NOT to Touch

- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go` lines 875-960 — the per-bead
  instructions text (`executeBeadInstructionsClaudeText` /
  `executeBeadInstructionsAgentText`). Those are agent-facing and must keep
  telling the agent to `git add <specific-paths>` only. The fix belongs in
  the orchestrator, not the prompt: DDx wrote the evidence, DDx commits it.
- `AGENTS.md`, `docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-006-agent-service.md`,
  `skills/ddx-bead/SKILL.md` — the policy is already correct as of commit
  3a2a0c5. This bead implements the policy; it does not amend it.

## Cross-Bead Context

- Parent epic `ddx-32b3008e` ("Evolve execute-bead into a tracked
  execution-evidence workflow", P0, spec-id FEAT-006) is the meta for this
  work. This bead is the first execution-layer task under that epic.
- The trail-preservation policy is codified in commit 3a2a0c5: AGENTS.md
  "Merge Policy" section, FEAT-006 "Execution Trail Preservation in
  Downstream Merges" section (with 3 acceptance criteria), and
  skills/ddx-bead/SKILL.md "Branch Merge Policy" section.
- The land flow today uses a mix of `git merge --no-ff` and
  `git update-ref` (for bare-repo worktrees) — see
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:166-544 for the three code paths
  that touch the merge. Any of them may be the right insertion point; pick
  the one that runs LAST after the agent commit is finalized.
- The bug-fix commit 2792dfc ("anchor SessionLogDir to project root") gives
  the orchestrator a reliable absolute path for session logs. This bead is
  the analogous fix for execution evidence.

## Spec Excerpt (FEAT-006 Execution Trail Preservation, normative)

> **Execution Trail Preservation in Downstream Merges** [...]
> Bead records store `closing_commit_sha` as a pointer back into git
> history; routing, cost, latency, retry-count, and tier-escalation reports
> read these commits directly. The trail realizes the `output = bead(input)`
> contract the system is built on: every bead attempt is recoverable from
> git history alone.
>
> **Acceptance Criteria (execution trail preservation):**
> - Given a branch containing at least one `chore: update tracker
>   (execute-bead` commit, when the branch is merged to its target, then
>   every commit SHA on the feature branch remains reachable from the target
>   branch HEAD after the merge.
> - Given a bead has `closing_commit_sha` set, when the branch containing
>   that commit is merged to main, then `git cat-file -e
>   <closing_commit_sha>` still succeeds on main after the merge.

This bead adds a fourth, implementation-layer criterion: that the
per-attempt evidence dir is *present in the trail at all*, not merely
preserved across merges.

## Verification Commands

- `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent -run TestExecuteBeadLandsEvidence`
  passes (new regression test, see AC below).
- `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent ./internal/server ./cmd` passes
  (full suite; no regressions).
- Manual dogfooding: in a throwaway clone, run
  `ddx agent execute-loop --once` against a ready bead. After it returns,
  `git status --porcelain` MUST print nothing. `git log -1 --stat` MUST show
  at least one file under `.ddx/executions/<attempt>/` in the HEAD commit
  (or its immediate predecessor in the same merge).
title: orchestrator: land execution evidence and leave worktree clean after agent run

acceptance:
(1) Reviewer verdict extraction consumes NORMALIZED reviewer text (the harness's final cleaned response), not raw stream frames. If the agent module does not yet expose a normalized final-response API, this bead is blocked on the agent-side stream normalizer bead (sibling in ~/Projects/agent).
(2) Fixture reviewer-stream-approve-misextracted.jsonl (from ddx-004eccbc) drives a regression test: the pre-fix behavior records BLOCK; the fix records APPROVE.
(3) ParseReviewVerdict no longer defaults to BLOCK on an unrecognized verdict line. Unrecognized output returns a parse error; the caller handles that as a review-error (feeds ddx-738edf47's retryable-failure path), not a silent BLOCK.
(4) Unit tests cover: clean APPROVE, clean BLOCK, REQUEST_CHANGES, unparseable, empty.
description:
## Wave 2 framing: risk-reduction, not correctness

This bead (scope:partial) stops silent-BLOCK mis-records — it does NOT stop mis-extraction itself. After wave:2 lands, the reviewer will still misfire on codex/claude raw stream frames because clause (1) (consume normalized final text) is deferred to ddx-7bc0c8d5. What changes: misfires surface as loud review-error reopens instead of silent BLOCKs, feeding ddx-738edf47's retryable-failure path. Expect a "review-error reopens per closed bead" metric to look rough in this window. The real extraction fix lands when agent-32e8ff5e ships upstream and ddx-7bc0c8d5 migrates.

---

Reporter: nexiq+axon 2026-04-18/19/20. Symptom 1 of review-malfunction report.

The post-merge reviewer in `ddx agent execute-loop` records `{"verdict":"BLOCK"}` in `.ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/result.json` even when the reviewer LLM produced a well-formed `### Verdict: APPROVE` (or REQUEST_CHANGES) with full AC grade table and cited evidence. Observed ~20 times in a row on nexiq codex+claude loop 2026-04-19 against beads nexiq-b5661a77, nexiq-50485cd3, nexiq-be1bc61d, nexiq-8999acab, hx-8a26c6df, hx-4901d83a, hx-e33210f6, nexiq-35d64a6b, and more — every bead in the run.

Code site: `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go:76` — `reVerdictLine` regex `^#{1,4}\s+Verdict:\s*(APPROVE|REQUEST_CHANGES|BLOCK)\s*$`. When the reviewer output contains the raw verdict on a heading line but is wrapped inside a codex streaming JSON event body (e.g. `{"type":"item.completed","item":{..."text":"### Verdict: APPROVE..."}}` with escaped newlines), the regex does not match across the wrapped payload and `ParseReviewVerdict` falls through to the `VerdictBlock` default at line 86 ("safe default: requires human attention"). The bead then gets a `review_malfunction` event with detail "post-merge review: malformed BLOCK verdict (missing rationale)".

Actual reviewer output is preserved in `.ddx/agent-logs/sessions.jsonl` under the reviewer session id and can be recovered post-facto; the parser just fails to see it.

Hypothesis from report: parser anchors on `### Verdict:` heading but a codex harness stream serializes text with `\n` escaping or wraps the reviewer body inside thread/turn JSON events so the multiline anchor never hits. Root fix options: (a) extract reviewer text from the raw stream before parsing (strip codex thread/turn JSON to the inner `item_0.text` payload); (b) switch the reviewer prompt to require machine-readable JSON `{verdict, ac_grades, summary}` and parse that; (c) broaden the regex to match non-anchored `### Verdict: X` headings even inside JSON-escaped text.

## Upstream dependency

Blocked on agent-32e8ff5e (in ~/Projects/agent): the fix moves reviewer-text extraction behind the ddx-agent service boundary so DDx no longer parses raw harness stream frames. Until that lands, f7ae036f's clause (1) on "normalized reviewer text" cannot be satisfied and the regression test from ddx-004eccbc remains red. The parse-error / no-default-BLOCK changes (clauses 3-4) can land independently and are worth doing now.

## Scope split (wave 2)

Clauses (3) and (4) — no default-BLOCK, parse errors surface as review-error, fixture regression test — land in wave 2 with only ddx-004eccbc as a prereq.

Clause (1) — consume normalized final text from the agent service instead of raw stream frames — is deferred to ddx-7bc0c8d5 because it depends on agent-32e8ff5e shipping upstream. This bead is labeled scope:partial to signal that wave 2 only closes (3)+(4); a follow-up pass on (1) happens inside ddx-7bc0c8d5's migration.
title: exec: post-merge reviewer mis-extracts verdict, emits spurious BLOCK on APPROVE

acceptance:
1. Per-field hard cap of 65,535 bytes on bead.events[].body and adjacent writer paths, documented in cli/internal/bead package comment. bd-compatibility rationale in the comment.
2. Reviewer raw streams never enter bead.events[].body. Reviewer output path writes the full stream to .ddx/executions/<id>/reviewer-stream.log; event body is ≤512 bytes (verdict + first-line rationale + path reference). Unit test: a 2MB reviewer stream produces a <=512-byte event body referencing the full payload on disk.
3. Other writers that append to bead.events[].body enforce the per-field cap; overflow persists as an artifact under .ddx/executions/<id>/ and the event body carries a path reference plus summary.
4. Reader: bead/session scanner buffers raised from the current 1MB to at least 16MB (covers observed extremes: 1.46MB bead row, ~7MB session rows). No new global "shared buffer constant" abstraction — raise each call site's existing Buffer() arg (cli/internal/bead/store.go:172, processmetrics.go:723, session_log_tailer.go:91). claude_stream.go:101 is already 16MB.
5. Regression test: a synthetic beads.jsonl line of 2MB reads cleanly; a line exceeding the buffer surfaces an error naming the bead id and does not crash.
6. bd round-trip smoke: a DDx-authored bead with one 65,535-byte description field exports to JSONL and is accepted by `bd import`. Requires bd on PATH; otherwise t.Skip() with a clear message.
7. Migration / recovery: corrupted rows encountered during read fall back to best-effort parse (truncated body + warning); do NOT mutate existing tracker files in-place. A separate `ddx bead repair` bead can be filed if real corruption is found in the wild.
8. cd cli && go test ./... green.
description:
Reporter: nexiq+axon 2026-04-18/19/20. Symptom 2 of review-malfunction report.

A reviewer emitted a ~1.46MB event body (streamed reviewer output dumped verbatim into bead.events[].body). Because cli/internal/bead/store.go:172 sets bufio.Scanner.Buffer to 1MB, subsequent reads of beads.jsonl fail with "bufio.Scanner: token too long." Tracker effectively bricked until the row is hand-trimmed.

## Compatibility constraint (validated 2026-04-20)

Empirical bd test (/tmp/bd-size-test via homebrew bd 1.0.2):
- Per-field insert cap: 65,535 bytes. Exactly Dolt TEXT column limit. 65535 inserts; 65536 errors "string too long."
- Per-line export: easily exceeds 1MB when a bead accumulates multiple long comments/events. Observed: one bead with 5 × 65KB comments → 326KB line. Our 1.46MB corruption is ~23 such events, plausible for a long-running bead.
- bd export lines ARE currently unreadable by DDx when they exceed our 1MB scanner cap — even when no single field is oversized.

## Design contract

- Write-side (DDx): enforce per-field hard cap of 65,535 bytes so DDx-authored beads round-trip through `bd import`. Soft cap ~10KB with head+tail+marker truncation for typical event bodies. Overflow persists as an artifact file under .ddx/executions/<id>/, event body stores path + one-line summary.
- Read-side (DDx): accept arbitrarily long lines from bd-compatible tools. Scanner buffer sized generously (suggest 64MB) so normal accumulated beads never trigger token-too-long.
- Read-side tolerance: if a line does exceed the buffer, surface a clear error with bead id and pointer to the artifact, do not crash.

## Known scanner sites

All currently undersized relative to bd export reality:
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:172 (1MB)
- cli/internal/processmetrics/processmetrics.go:723 (1MB)
- cli/internal/agent/claude_stream.go:101 (16MB — probably OK)
- cli/internal/agent/session_log_tailer.go:91 (4MB)

## Artifact reference

Fixtures for this bug (2MB synthetic reviewer stream, 1.46MB-style beads.jsonl row) land via ddx-004eccbc.
title: exec: reviewer stream bloats bead event body, corrupts beads.jsonl via bufio.Scanner limit

acceptance: skills/ddx/evals/routing.jsonl exists with at least 15 rows, each valid JSON, each with phrase + expected_reference + expected_cli keys. scripts/eval-skill.sh exists and is executable. make eval-skill runs in under 10 minutes against both --harness claude and --harness codex and exits 0 when the SKILL.md routing matches the fixtures. make eval-skill exits non-zero if any row fails. scripts/eval-skill.sh --validate passes against the current SKILL.md (frontmatter has only name + description; no Claude-only fields; body under 500 lines; all referenced reference/*.md files exist). CI runs make eval-skill on PRs that modify skills/ddx/**.
description:
Anthropic's skill-authoring guidance treats evaluations as load-bearing, not optional. Ship a cross-harness eval suite for the ddx skill so CI can enforce routing behavior on every PR.

Components required:
1. skills/ddx/evals/routing.jsonl — JSONL file with at least 15 rows, each a user phrase + expected reference file + expected CLI invocation. Cover every intent-router entry in SKILL.md plus edge phrasings. Example rows:
     {"phrase":"what is on the queue", "expected_reference":"reference/status.md", "expected_cli":"ddx bead ready"}
     {"phrase":"drain the queue", "expected_reference":"reference/work.md", "expected_cli":"ddx work"}
     {"phrase":"run the next bead", "expected_reference":"reference/work.md", "expected_cli":"ddx work"}
     {"phrase":"got any work for me", "expected_reference":"reference/work.md", "expected_cli":"ddx bead ready"}
     {"phrase":"review this", "expected_reference":"reference/review.md", "expected_cli":"ddx bead review"}
     ... 10+ more covering beads, agents, status, doctor.
2. scripts/eval-skill.sh — driver that for each row:
     a. Runs 'ddx agent run --harness <h> --text <phrase>' against --harness claude and --harness codex.
     b. Parses the harness output and checks whether it mentions the expected_reference filename or invokes the expected_cli command (regex or grep-based check is fine for v1; tighten later).
     c. Reports pass/fail per row per harness. Exits non-zero on any fail.
   Also supports a --validate flag that does agentskills.io spec conformance on skills/ddx/SKILL.md (no Claude-only frontmatter fields, body < 500 lines, all referenced reference/*.md files exist, name and description present).
3. Makefile target 'eval-skill' (in cli/Makefile or root Makefile) that runs scripts/eval-skill.sh. Add 'make eval-skill' to CI on PRs that touch skills/ddx/.
4. CI config (.github/workflows/*.yml or equivalent) — add a step that runs make eval-skill when skills/ddx/** changes.

In-scope files:
- skills/ddx/evals/routing.jsonl (new)
- scripts/eval-skill.sh (new)
- Makefile (new or edited target)
- .github/workflows/ci.yml or equivalent (add step)

Out-of-scope:
- The agent eval framework itself — v1 is a simple bash driver; a more sophisticated harness can come later.

acceptance:
1. ddx work --model qwen3.6 discovers qwen3.6-35b-a3b via prefix match and routes there.
2. When a provider has both qwen3.6 and qwen3.6-35b-a3b, exact match wins (selects qwen3.6).
3. Shortest suffix wins: qwen3.6-27b (suffix len 4) beats qwen3.6-35b-a3b (suffix len 8).
4. On equal suffix length, catalog entry preferred over discovered-only.
5. Fuzzy match prints: model "qwen3.6" resolved to "qwen3.6-27b" (fuzzy match).
6. Deprecated model prints warning but proceeds.

Unit tests:
T1. Prefix match: qwen3.6 → qwen3.6-35b-a3b when only prefix available.
T2. Exact beats prefix: qwen3.6 → qwen3.6 when both exist.
T3. Shortest suffix tiebreak: qwen3.6-27b wins over qwen3.6-35b-a3b.
T4. Catalog preferred over discovered on equal suffix length.
T5. Tie warning emitted when multiple candidates survive all tiebreakers.

cd cli && go test ./... && go build .
description:
Add fuzzy prefix matching to model resolution so --model qwen3.6 resolves
to qwen3.6-35b-a3b when a provider advertises it.

Depends on ddx-71fe194f (discovery integration) being landed first — this
bead extends the unified model pool with prefix matching.

Implementation:
- In the model resolution step, after checking for exact match, check for
  prefix matches across the unified pool (catalog + discovered).
- Tiebreak: shortest suffix after prefix. On equal suffix length, prefer
  catalog entries over discovered-only, then alphabetical.
- When fuzzy match is used, print: model "X" resolved to "Y" (fuzzy match).
- When resolved model has deprecation metadata, print warning.
- When multiple models tie after all tiebreakers, warn and pick first.

Review area: tracked-vs-ignored execution state. Evidence covers the existing ddx init gitignore gap, the new requirement for tracked execution evidence, and the need to keep only local scratch, locks, transient worktrees, and provider-local runtime state ignored.

acceptance:
**User story:** After the upstream point release lands and DDx pulls it, `ddx agent run` with no flags just works on my machine. `--profile local`, `--harness claude`, `--model opus-4.7` behave per the upstream contract. The drain-queue failure mode (empty spec → gemini+minimax → 19-burn) is impossible.

**Acceptance criteria:**

1. **Upstream prerequisite tagged and pulled.** Go.mod references a ddx-agent version whose CHANGELOG documents: first-class `default` and `local` profiles; supported-models allow-list in routing inputs + gating; `ErrHarnessModelIncompatible`; public decision-trace (`RouteDecision.Candidates` + typed error); cost dimension on `ProviderEntry` with unknown-cost fallback. All upstream beads in `~/Projects/agent/.ddx/` labeled `routing-plan` are closed.

2. **No DDx-side synthesis on default path.**
   - `ddx agent run` with zero flags issues exactly one `ResolveRoute(Profile: "default")` call, no Model, no Harness, no Provider.
   - `ddx work` default: same shape.
   - `ddx agent execute-bead` with no overrides: same shape.
   - Integration test: same config that caused the drain-queue failure (real `~/.local/share/ddx/.ddx/config.yaml`) now either succeeds or returns a single typed error — never 19-burn.
   - Unit test: `workerDispatchAdapter.DispatchWorker({})` → spec with `Profile: "default"`, no other fields. No `ResolveTierModelRef`, no `ResolveProfileLadder` in the call graph on the default path.

3. **Preflight before claim.**
   - Call sequence at `execute_bead_loop.go:352`: next-candidate → `ResolveRoute` → (preflight gate, new) → `Claim` → invoke. Route error OR preflight rejection exits the loop with a `worker-level` failure — no bead claimed.
   - Test: seed a pathological route (harness with allow-list that excludes the decision's model — fabricated via upstream test seam). Assert `beadStore.Claim` is never called; worker record shows `status: execution_failed` with detail naming the rejected pair; no bead has a `kind:tier-attempt` event from this attempt.

4. **Config deprecation is deliberate, not warning-only.**
   - Loading a config with `agent.routing.default_harness` set produces a **hard error** at startup with a migration pointer. Field is deleted from the config schema.
   - Loading a config with `agent.routing.profile_ladders` or `agent.routing.model_overrides` set produces a one-time warning per process AND causes the default path to ignore them. Opt-in with `--escalate` or `--override-model` explicit flags.
   - CHANGELOG entry + migration guide in DDx docs.
   - Migration test: fixture config containing all three deprecated fields, run `ddx agent run` with no flags, assert warnings printed and default path honored (not the deprecated config).

5. **Override behavior matches G3 exactly.**
   - `--harness claude` → `ResolveRoute(Profile: "default", Harness: "claude")`. If claude fails upstream's gates (binary missing, quota exhausted), surfaces the typed error; no silent substitution. E2E test mocks each gate failure.
   - `--model opus-4.7` → `ResolveRoute(Profile: "default", Model: "opus-4.7")`. Lowest-cost available quota-OK provider wins. E2E test seeds 3 providers at different cost with shared model; asserts lowest-cost pick.
   - `--harness claude --model opus-4.7` → typed `ErrHarnessModelIncompatible` when combo is invalid; clean dispatch otherwise.
   - `--profile local --harness claude` → upstream typed error ("claude is non-local, profile=local is local-only"); DDx surfaces it unmodified. E2E test.

6. **Playwright — drain-queue goldens.**
   - Fixture matches user's real config (endpoints: lmstudio + omlx + lmstudio + lmstudio, no gemini binary test or with gemini installed but not in allow-list for the chosen model).
   - Click Drain on project home → within 5s: worker row appears with harness = `agent` (or another live-endpoint-backed harness). Never gemini + non-gemini model. Never 19 failures.
   - After drain completes: worker status `success` (or typed error exit with reason recorded on the bead). Attempts count matches actual work done.

7. **No compensating DDx logic retained.**
   - Search DDx for code that re-implements any of: allow-list checks, exact-pin filtering, profile-to-preference mapping, provider cost scoring. Must be absent. If found, PR fails review.
   - `ResolveProfileLadder`, `ResolveTierModelRef`, `workersByHarness` escalation helpers are deleted from the default path. They remain reachable via `--escalate` only. Dead-code detector run in CI catches orphans.

8. **D6 / D7 are explicitly out of scope and filed as follow-ups.**
   - `ddx-05b4cc9d` (workers filter) remains its own bead and may land before or after this one.
   - A new bead is filed post-release for the route-status / providers-UI decision-trace consumer work, pointing at A4 upstream as satisfied.

9. **Documentation.**
   - DDx's CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md links to upstream profile catalog + override precedence + best-provider contract docs. No re-documentation on the DDx side.
   - DDx CHANGELOG entry covers the go.mod bump, D4 breaking changes, and new CLI surface semantics.

10. **Regression goldens for the drain-queue bug.**
    - Integration test exists that reproduces the original failure mode (empty spec + user's config + gemini binary installed + minimax model in overrides). Before this bead's work: test fails with 19-burn. After: test succeeds or exits with one clean error. Test stays in the suite as a regression guard.
description:
## Status after codex review (2026-04-22)

Original plan was consolidated in this bead but **reduced in scope** after codex's review surfaced that most of the compatibility and data-model work belongs upstream in ddx-agent. This bead is now the **DDx-side tracker only**. Upstream work is queued as separate beads in `~/Projects/agent/.ddx/` (prefix `agent-*`) and will land as a single tagged point release; nothing in DDx lands until that release is pulled.

## Goals (unchanged — user stated 2026-04-22)

**G1. Zero-interaction defaults.** `ddx agent run` and `ddx agent execute-bead` just work with no flags.

**G2. Usable profiles.** `--profile standard`, `--profile local` force clear, documented rules.

**G3. Usable overrides.** `--harness claude` forces claude. `--model opus-4.7` forces opus-4.7. Within requirements: available, quota OK, lowest cost.

## Upstream dependencies (tracked in ~/Projects/agent/.ddx/)

This bead blocks on a single upstream point release that carries:

- Profile catalog with first-class `default`, `local`, `standard` (and any others DDx needs to expose) + documented provider-preference rules.
- Supported-models allow-list on routing-inputs `HarnessEntry`; gating rejects non-allowed models even when `ExactPinSupport: true`.
- Typed `ErrHarnessModelIncompatible` surfaced from `Execute` so explicit `--harness X --model Y` conflicts fail fast with actionable detail.
- Public `RouteDecision.Candidates` + typed error for "no viable candidate" with per-candidate rejection reasons — DDx's decision-trace consumers need this.
- Cost dimension on `ProviderEntry` (`CostUSDPer1kTokens`) + scoring tiebreak that prefers lower cost, with unknown-cost fallback, subscription-quota pricing, and near-quota demotion.
- Documentation for the profile catalog, override precedence, and "best provider" contract.

Once that release is tagged, DDx work below proceeds.

## DDx-side changes (reduced scope)

**D1. Dispatch sends a real spec.**

`workerDispatchAdapter.DispatchWorker` treats empty input as `{Profile: "default"}` explicitly. Dashboard Drain button calls the same path. No DDx-side default synthesis beyond "profile=default if unset."

**D2. Stop synthesizing a tier ladder by default.**

Default `ddx agent run` / `ddx agent execute-bead` / `ddx work` path calls `agentlib.ResolveRoute(Profile: spec.Profile)` with no Model string unless `spec.Model` was explicitly given. No per-tier iteration, no `ResolveTierModelRef` lookup, no preference-order fallback. The tier-ladder machinery becomes opt-in via `--escalate` only.

**D3. Preflight gate (defensive only, post-upstream).**

After `ResolveRoute` returns, DDx verifies the decision's `(Harness, Model)` is not in the typed-incompatibility set surfaced by upstream. The compatibility data comes from upstream — DDx does **not** duplicate the allow-list. Insertion point: `execute_bead_loop.go:352`, **before** `Claim`. Route/preflight failure exits the loop with a worker-level failure record; no bead is claimed, no burn cycle.

**D4. Deprecate DDx-side routing config.**

Deliberate breaking change in the same release that pulls the upstream point release:
- `agent.routing.default_harness` — field deleted; upstream's profile model makes it redundant.
- `agent.routing.profile_ladders` — kept but gated behind explicit `--escalate` only. Not read on the default path. Warning at config-load if set without `--escalate` ever being used in the life of the process.
- `agent.routing.model_overrides` — same treatment as `profile_ladders`: explicit-opt-in only, warning otherwise.
- `agent.harness` (top-level) — kept, clarified as a **preference for tie-breaks**, not a default override.

Migration note published in DDx changelog.

**D5. CLI surface parity with goals.**

- `ddx agent run` with zero flags → `ResolveRoute(Profile: "default")`. No other DDx inputs.
- `ddx agent run --profile local` → pass through unchanged to upstream.
- `ddx agent run --harness claude` → `ResolveRoute(Profile: "default", Harness: "claude")`. Upstream picks best provider within claude.
- `ddx agent run --model opus-4.7` → `ResolveRoute(Profile: "default", Model: "opus-4.7")`. Upstream picks lowest-cost available quota-OK provider.
- `ddx agent run --harness claude --model opus-4.7` → typed `ErrHarnessModelIncompatible` from upstream when incompatible; clean success otherwise.
- `ddx work` (drain) default → same as `ddx agent run` default.

## Explicitly dropped from this bead (per codex)

- **D6 — `ddx agent route-status` / providers UI decision-trace consumers.** Adjacent but not required. Files as a follow-up once A4 lands upstream. Hooks into `ddx-23978824` (providers UI).
- **D7 — `workersByProject` id/path filter.** Independent bug. Stays as `ddx-05b4cc9d`; not blocked by this bead and does not block this bead.

## Sequencing

1. **Upstream beads land and point release is tagged.** (Separate queue in `~/Projects/agent/.ddx/`.)
2. DDx `go.mod` bumps to that release.
3. D1, D2, D3, D5 land together in one DDx PR — no compensating paths retained.
4. D4 lands with D1-D3-D5 or in the immediate follow-up PR. Migration warning + changelog.
5. **Then** D6 / `ddx-23978824` / sessions-billing-mode (`ddx-6904a90b`) work can consume the new decision-trace surface.

## Cross-references

- Upstream beads: see `~/Projects/agent/.ddx/beads.jsonl` under label `routing-plan`.
- Direct codex review feedback logged in bead notes below.
- Supersedes original 3-step proposal from conversation history.
- Addresses `project_endpoint_routing_design.md` memory directive (2026-04-21): "kill named-provider profiles, move to endpoints + live discovery + fuzzy model match."
- Hard downstream dependents (wait for this bead):
  - `ddx-b6cf025c` — drain-worker count control (default profile must mean something first)
  - `ddx-23978824` — providers UI (needs decision trace from A4)
  - `ddx-6904a90b` — sessions billing-mode (benefits from richer session.routing metadata)
- Independent of this bead: `ddx-05b4cc9d` (workers filter).

## Codex review — key corrections folded in

1. A3 confirmed by code inspection: `HarnessEntry` has no model-allow-list field; `engine.go:456` `resolveModel` returns `req.Model` directly when no provider discovery; `gating.go:88` only checks `ExactPinSupport`. Allow-list must be added to routing inputs/capabilities, not just "fix ExactPin semantics."
2. A4 bigger than originally written: public `RouteDecision` has no `Candidates` field; service drops rejection data on error. Needs typed error + public surface.
3. Earlier claim that codex/claude have `Models: nil` was wrong — that's DDx's local `harness_config.go`, not upstream's `internal/harnesses/registry.go`. Upstream has codex=[gpt-5.4], claude=[sonnet, opus, claude-sonnet-4-6]. Allow-list work is universal upstream.
4. Upstream catalog has no `default` or `local` profile today (models.yaml:195). Must be added before DDx materializes them.
5. D3 restricted to defensive-only; all compatibility logic belongs upstream.
6. G2 precedence conflict (`--profile local --harness claude`): resolved in upstream docs as explicit-pin-wins but upstream returns typed error if the pin violates the profile's hard constraint (e.g., local-only with non-local harness).
7. G3 cost needs explicit data model — no price field on ProviderEntry today.
8. D4 reframed as deliberate breaking change, not warning-only drift.
9. D6 + D7 dropped from epic scope.
10. Quick-test gates added: upstream exact-pin regression; service-layer typed error preserves reasons; `ResolveRoute(Profile: "default")` against endpoint-only config; DDx worker exits before any bead claim.
title: Adopt ddx-agent routing point release: zero-interaction defaults, usable profiles, sharp overrides (DDx-side consumer)

acceptance:
- [ ] ddx bead review command produces a well-formed review prompt
- [ ] Template documented (required sections, expected output contract)
- [ ] Reads governing-doc refs from bead fields
- [ ] Used by the post-review path from gap #1
description:
Manual review prompt construction is tedious and inconsistent. A command that reads a bead and produces a review-ready prompt (AC + commit diff + governing-doc excerpts + expected output format) would make post-merge review cheap enough to always run.

## Scope
- ddx bead review <bead-id> [--iter N] [--output <file>]
- Pulls bead AC from the bead record
- Pulls commit diff via git show (latest successful execute-bead result_rev by default, or --from-rev)
- Reads governing-doc slugs from bead 'spec-id' / 'refs' fields and excerpts the relevant sections
- Renders a prompt template asking the agent to grade each AC item and produce structured APPROVE / REQUEST_CHANGES / BLOCK
- Output is the prompt body (stdout or to a file); pipe into ddx agent run --prompt @-

## Context
Observed during axon ADR-018 loop: I wrote /tmp/review-A1.md by hand twice (iter 1 and iter 2). Both prompts had the same structure. A templated command would eliminate that manual step and make reviews reproducible.

acceptance: (1) Root cause documented in code comment next to SynthesizeCommit. (2) SynthesizeCommit completes with exit 0 when only change is in a gitignored directory. (3) Regression test: a project with non-empty .ddx/agent-logs and no real changes yields SynthesizeCommit → (false, nil) (no commit, no error). (4) Regression test: a project with one real modification + non-empty .ddx/agent-logs yields (true, nil) with only the real change committed. (5) ddx work --once drains ddx-672cf2bf or another ready bead end-to-end without hitting staging-changes exit 1.
description:
Blocks `ddx work` whenever the gitignored runtime directories `.ddx/agent-logs/` and `.ddx/workers/` have any content. Every real-world project hits this after the first agent run.

## Symptom

Every tier attempt in the execute-loop aborts:

```
pre-execute-bead checkpoint: staging changes: exit status 1
```

## Root cause

cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:363 `RealGitOps.SynthesizeCommit` runs:

```
git -C <dir> add -A -- . ':(exclude).ddx/agent-logs' ':(exclude).ddx/workers' ':(exclude).ddx/executions/*/embedded' ':(exclude).ddx/executions/*/no_changes_rationale.txt' ':(exclude).claude/skills' ':(exclude).agents/skills'
```

When `.ddx/agent-logs/` and `.ddx/workers/` exist as untracked directories (normal after the first agent run), and both are in .gitignore (lines 421-422), git add emits:

```
The following paths are ignored by one of your .gitignore files:
.ddx/agent-logs
.ddx/workers
hint: Use -f if you really want to add them.
```

AND exits with status 1. The `:(exclude)` magic pathspec is not sufficient to silence this exit-1 in git's add behavior with `-C` form — even `-c advice.addIgnoredFile=false` does not suppress it.

## Fix candidates (not pre-decided)

1. Check `git status --porcelain` first; only run `git add` if there are tracked changes to stage.
2. Enumerate dirty files in Go (via `git status --porcelain`), filter out excluded paths, pass the survivors explicitly to `git add --`.
3. Distinguish stderr "benign ignored-files warning" from real errors; accept exit 1 when that's the only message.
4. Move `.ddx/agent-logs/` and `.ddx/workers/` out of the repo tree so they're outside the pathspec entirely.

Prefer (1) or (2) — they're honest about the intent "stage only real modifications." Option (3) is fragile against git message changes; (4) touches a lot of downstream tooling.

## Evidence

Worker: worker-20260420T145025-61dc. Bead ddx-672cf2bf. All three tier attempts (cheap/standard/smart) failed identically. Confirmed reproducible via manual run of the exact add command in `/Users/erik/Projects/ddx`.

## Relationship to other beads

Independent of ddx-672cf2bf (silent-exit on epic queues) — this is loud-exit on staging. Independent of v0.7.0. Blocks the wave:1 smoke test in ddx-e7254128.
notes: Fixed in commit edbf8a17. Dropped :(exclude) pathspecs for already-gitignored paths (.ddx/agent-logs, .ddx/workers). Regression test TestSynthesizeCommit_GitignoredDirsDoNotFail covers AC (3) and (4). AC (5) verified: worker-20260420T150211-60cc got past the pre-execute-bead checkpoint and dispatched the agent on ddx-004eccbc without the prior 'staging changes: exit status 1' error.
title: exec: pre-execute-bead checkpoint fails when .ddx/agent-logs or .ddx/workers are untracked

description:
Migrate cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead_test.go TestExecuteBeadDirtyWorktreeWithoutCommits() from fakeExecuteBeadGit + fakeAgentRunner to real git + script harness.

Pattern:
- Use cli/internal/agent/integration_helper_test.go newScriptHarnessRepo(t, beadCount) to build an isolated repo with real git.
- Use runGitInteg (from cli/internal/git/git_basic_test.go) for any supplemental git invocations, so GIT_* env is scrubbed.
- Replace fakeExecuteBeadGit with the RealLandingGitOps flow used in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land_test.go.
- Replace fakeAgentRunner with the script harness (cli/internal/agent/script.go) driven by a per-attempt directive file. When the test needs to simulate 'agent emits no commits' or 'agent emits a dirty worktree', encode that as script directives rather than a canned Result struct.
- Preserve every assertion — the point is that the test now exercises real git + real merge/preserve semantics, not that the surface shrinks.

Scope limit: ONLY this test. Do not migrate any other test in the same file; touching another test expands blast radius outside the bead.

Verification: cd cli && go test ./cmd -run '^TestExecuteBeadDirtyWorktreeWithoutCommits$' -count=1 passes. No struct ending in -GitOps or -Runner that fakes the thing under test is referenced from this test after the migration.

Governing concern: concerns.md §testing 'no mocks, period' + 'never mock the thing you are testing'. Parent: ddx-d9df348d.

acceptance: FEAT-002, FEAT-008, FEAT-013, FEAT-014, SD-019, and TP-002 all describe the same host+user ddx-server model, including runtime/index storage, worker boundaries, provider dashboarding, replay-backed fixtures, and mandatory Playwright coverage for new UI surfaces
description:
<context-digest>
Review area: host+user ddx-server planning stack after the server model evolved beyond the earlier multi-project topology draft. Evidence covers FEAT-002, FEAT-008, FEAT-013, FEAT-014, SD-019, TP-002, the fresh-eyes server review, and the updated requirements for a per-user host daemon, localhost HTTP UI, SQLite-backed runtime/index state, embedded-agent progress visibility, provider dashboarding, replay-backed fixtures, and Playwright coverage for every new UI surface.
</context-digest>
Update the governing server planning docs so they describe the current intended host+user ddx-server model before follow-on server design and implementation beads continue.

## Goals
- Reconcile FEAT-002, FEAT-008, FEAT-013, FEAT-014, SD-019, and TP-002 around a single host+user ddx-server instance with explicit project registry, worker management boundaries, and queue semantics
- Define the split between git-backed project truth, tracked execution evidence, and host-local runtime/index state backed by an embedded database
- Define the current transport/security model: localhost unauthenticated HTTP for UI/API now, explicit future transport/auth evolution later
- Add the embedded-agent progress, provider dashboard, replay-backed fixture, and Playwright coverage requirements to the governed server plan
- Make the scheduling boundary explicit: server may allocate workers across projects, but bead ordering remains owned by each project queue

## Boundaries
- Keep Axon and multi-host service-backed topology as later-stage work
- Do not reopen the already-shipped single-project execute-bead loop contract except where the new server plan must reference it

acceptance: A regression test covers a mixed log directory containing both routing-outcomes.jsonl and legacy sessions.jsonl rows, and ddx agent usage includes both data sources instead of dropping legacy sessions when routing outcomes are present.
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: cli/cmd/agent_usage.go:44
Category: integration
Severity: medium
Description: Commit 83700bf switches ddx agent usage to prefer routing-outcomes.jsonl, but the command only falls back to sessions.jsonl when there are zero routing-derived rows at all. In a mixed log directory, as soon as one new routing outcome exists, all older legacy session rows are ignored. That breaks the recorded requirement to preserve backward compatibility for existing activity/session rows and will under-report historical usage until every old session has been replayed into the new store.
Suggested fix: Merge routing-metrics rows with legacy sessions.jsonl rows during the compatibility window, or keep using legacy rows for historical coverage until a complete migration marker exists; add regression coverage for mixed old/new log directories.
title: integration: preserve legacy session history in agent usage

acceptance: A review-labeled bead closed with an explicit `--commit` or with real staged implementation/docs changes records the exact closing_commit_sha unless the close is metadata-only, and regression coverage proves metadata-only review closes still omit the field while non-metadata review closes preserve it.
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: cli/cmd/bead.go:175
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: Commit 91f17a05b054b92d6d2b883bb4563e18fc2ce4d8 treats every bead carrying review labels as metadata-only by setting `recordClosingProvenance := !isMetadataOnlyReviewBead(beadBeforeClose)` and defining `isMetadataOnlyReviewBead` purely from labels. That is broader than the fix described in the commit message and FEAT-019: only metadata-only review closes should omit `closing_commit_sha`. Review beads can legitimately govern real docs or code changes, and this patch now drops their provenance even when the caller provides `--commit` or the close lands alongside real staged work. In a throwaway git repo with the current CLI binary, closing a normal bead with `--commit <short-head>` recorded `closing_commit_sha`, while closing an otherwise identical `action:review` bead with the same explicit commit omitted the field entirely. That makes replay and audit for non-metadata review passes fall back to sessionless/manual mode despite an exact implementation boundary being available.
Suggested fix: Narrow the suppression check to actual metadata-only review closes instead of all review-labeled beads, and add regression coverage for both explicit-commit and mixed-close review flows so non-metadata review beads still retain exact closing provenance.
title: correctness: preserve closing provenance for non-metadata review beads

acceptance: FEAT-002 explicitly defines the canonical project-scoped route family for document, bead, doc-graph, agent-session, execution, and mutation surfaces, plus the matching project-selection rule for project-aware MCP tools; and any remaining unscoped /api/... or /mcp/... forms are documented only as singleton compatibility aliases rather than the primary contract.
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-002-server.md:65
Category: correctness
Severity: high
Description: The commit adds the multi-project registry and says unscoped `/api/...` and `/mcp/...` forms are only compatibility aliases, but the numbered FEAT-002 requirements for documents, beads, doc graph, agent activity, executions, and bead mutations still specify only the legacy unscoped endpoints and tools. That leaves the governing feature contract internally inconsistent: an implementation can satisfy the numbered requirements without ever exposing the canonical `/api/projects/:project/...` resource families or project-scoped MCP semantics promised by SD-019 and by bead ddx-efd858c4 acceptance.
Suggested fix: Thread project-scoped routing through the FEAT-002 functional requirements for every affected surface, or add an explicit default rule that all server resource routes and project-aware MCP tools are scoped by `/api/projects/:project/...` and an equivalent project selector unless they are registry-wide. Keep legacy unscoped forms documented only as singleton compatibility aliases.
title: correctness: make FEAT-002 resource routes explicitly project-scoped

acceptance: The frontend CI job rebuilds cli/internal/server/frontend/dist before compiling cli/build/ddx, and a change limited to frontend src cannot pass Playwright by reusing stale committed dist assets
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: .github/workflows/ci.yml:163
Category: integration
Severity: medium
Description: The new frontend-e2e job compiles the Go server with `make build` before any frontend bundle build step, and the server embeds checked-in files from `cli/internal/server/frontend/dist` via `//go:embed all:frontend/dist`. FEAT-008 defines the build pipeline as `bun run build` producing `dist/` before `go build`, so this job can pass Playwright against a stale committed bundle instead of the current `src/` when frontend code changes without regenerating dist. That creates a false-negative CI path for the very regressions this job is supposed to catch.
Suggested fix: Install frontend dependencies with the declared frontend toolchain, run the frontend build to refresh `cli/internal/server/frontend/dist`, then compile the Go binary so Playwright exercises the current embedded UI assets.
title: integration: rebuild frontend dist before Playwright CI run

acceptance: API-001 and FEAT-006 agree on a single owner for required post-run checks and land/preserve semantics; no implementation path requires running those steps twice around one execute-bead attempt
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: docs/helix/02-design/contracts/API-001-execute-bead-supervisor-contract.md:65
Category: correctness
Severity: high
Description: API-001 says the supervisor should run `ddx agent execute-bead` and then separately run required post-run checks, classify the outcome, and apply land/preserve semantics. FEAT-006 already defines `ddx agent execute-bead` itself as the canonical workflow that resolves execution documents, runs required executions, evaluates merge eligibility, and either lands or preserves the iteration. Implementing API-001 as written would either duplicate required executions and landing logic around `execute-bead` or force FEAT-006 to become stale without saying so, which makes the command/supervisor boundary ambiguous for the first shipped worker.
Suggested fix: Make one layer authoritative. Either define the supervisor as a thin wrapper around `ddx agent execute-bead` result states, or narrow `execute-bead` into a lower-level primitive and update FEAT-006/SD-012 accordingly so post-run checks and land/preserve ownership live in exactly one place.
title: correctness: avoid duplicating execute-bead post-run and landing ownership in API-001

acceptance: Given a bead whose first close and only correlated sessions are before the cutoff but whose updated_at is later, metrics summary --since excludes it from bead totals and cost/feature unknown counts; a regression test covers the late-update/no-facts case; cd cli && go test ./internal/processmetrics -count=1 passes
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: cli/internal/processmetrics/processmetrics.go:285
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The new summary window helper uses bead `updated_at` as the inclusion gate, so a bead whose close fact and only correlated sessions are both before the cutoff still appears in `ddx metrics summary --since ...` if it was edited later for unrelated reasons. `beadsVisibleInSummaryWindow` admits that bead, and the downstream summary logic counts it in `summary.Beads.Total/Closed`, `summary.Beads.UnknownCost`, `summary.Cost.Beads`, and `summary.Cost.Features` even though there are no in-window lifecycle or session facts. Repro: with one bead closed on 2026-01-01, one correlated session on 2026-01-01, and only `updated_at=2026-03-05`, `Summary(Query{Since: 2026-02-01, HasSince: true})` returns `beads.total=1 closed=1 unknown_cost=1 cost.beads=1` while sessions, cycle-time, and rework are all zero. SD-016 says `--since` means include only facts observed on or after the cutoff for the relevant fact type; a tracker metadata edit after the window is not a new lifecycle or cost fact.
Suggested fix: Define summary bead inclusion from the same fact timelines used by the underlying sections, rather than raw `updated_at`. A bead with no in-window close/reopen/session facts should not surface in the windowed summary or its cost/feature counts, and regression coverage should include the late-update/no-facts case.
title: correctness: summary since window must ignore late-updated pre-cutoff beads

acceptance: A focused agent config regression test proves unsupported preset names from .ddx/config.yaml and AGENT_PRESET do not silently flow into prompt.NewFromPreset; invalid values are either normalized intentionally or surfaced via an explicit warning/error path rather than relying on library fallback.
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:190
Category: integration
Severity: medium
Description: Commit 7b06cfca changes the built-in default preset to agent, but resolveAgentConfig still copies any .ddx/config.yaml preset and AGENT_PRESET value straight into cfg.Preset at lines 190-215 and later passes it to prompt.NewFromPreset. In github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent v0.1.0, prompt.GetPreset falls back to agent for unknown names, so stale values like forge are still silently accepted. That means the runtime continues to rely on unknown-name fallback for user-supplied config and env values, so hx-96df1675's acceptance criterion about eliminating active config/runtime fallback paths is still not met.
Suggested fix: Validate configured preset names against the embedded agent library's supported preset set when loading config and env, then either normalize intentionally supported aliases or surface a deterministic warning/error for unsupported values, with regression coverage for invalid configured presets.
title: integration: validate configured agent presets instead of silently falling back

acceptance: A regression test covers a harness with an earlier routing-outcomes.jsonl row and a later legacy-only sessions.jsonl row lacking any matching native_session_id, trace_id, or span_id, and ddx agent usage reports both sessions without double-counting mirrored rows; cd cli && go test ./cmd -run TestAgentUsage -count=1 passes.
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: cli/cmd/agent_usage.go:208
Category: integration
Severity: medium
Description: Commit 9f96a3e scopes the legacy-session cutoff by harness, but `aggregateUsageAggs` still drops every `sessions.jsonl` row at or after the first routing outcome timestamp for that harness whether or not that session actually has a mirrored routing outcome (`cutoffByHarness` at lines 208-210 and 256-279). That remains an undercount path because session logging and routing-outcome logging are separate best-effort writes: the runner always appends the session first and then ignores any `AppendOutcome` failure (`cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:153-163`, `cli/internal/agent/routing_metrics.go:214-256`). If one later routing-outcome append is skipped or fails, `ddx agent usage` silently drops the valid legacy session row for that same harness as soon as any earlier outcome exists.
Suggested fix: Gate legacy-row suppression on actual mirrored identity matches rather than a blanket per-harness timestamp cutoff, or persist an explicit per-row migration marker that proves the session was mirrored before dropping it; add regression coverage for a harness with an earlier routing outcome plus a later legacy-only session row that lacks matching native/trace/span identifiers.
title: integration: keep same-harness legacy sessions visible when routing outcomes are missing

acceptance: Given mixed pre-cutoff and post-cutoff bead/session facts, metrics summary with --since excludes pre-cutoff totals and summary counts/aggregates match the chosen window semantics consistently across bead, session, cost, cycle-time, and rework sections; regression tests cover the filtered summary case
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: cli/internal/processmetrics/processmetrics.go:265
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: `Summary` accepts a windowed query but still counts all beads and all sessions from `loadInputs()` before applying the cutoff, so `--since` produces internally inconsistent dashboards. In `Summary`, `summary.Beads.Total/Open/Closed` and `summary.Sessions.*` are accumulated from the full stores (`len(beads)` and the unfiltered `sessions` loop), while cycle-time and rework use the cutoff and cost partially windows only per-session math. Repro: from `cli/`, a temporary fixture with one January bead/session and one March bead/session and `since=2026-02-01` prints `beads_total=2 sessions_total=2 cost_beads=2 cycle_known=1`, so pre-cutoff facts still appear in the top-level summary even though only one bead is inside the requested window.
Suggested fix: Define summary window semantics from SD-016's windowing rules and apply them consistently across bead, session, cost, cycle-time, and rework aggregates so a `--since` summary only reports facts observed on or after the cutoff.
title: correctness: honor --since across metrics summary aggregates

acceptance: A config containing agent.agent_runner.preset: forge fails config validation before runtime resolution, and regression coverage proves the schema's allowed preset set stays aligned with prompt.PresetNames()
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: cli/internal/config/schema/config.schema.json:276
Category: integration
Severity: medium
Description: Commit 3b26aac39659d65ea161498720609c15b2607726 updates the embedded config schema's default, description, and examples for agent_runner.preset, and adds a regression test that checks only those metadata fields. But the schema entry at cli/internal/config/schema/config.schema.json:276-283 still declares preset as a plain string with no enum, while ConfigLoader treats this embedded schema as the user-facing validation boundary in cli/internal/config/loader.go:68-70. As a result, .ddx/config.yaml values like preset: forge still pass config validation and only fail later when resolveAgentConfig checks prompt.PresetNames(), so the schema/validator surface remains out of sync with the actual supported preset set even after this fix.
Suggested fix: Add schema-level allowed-value enforcement for agent_runner.preset, ideally from the same supported preset source used by runtime validation, and add a focused config-loader or validator regression test proving unsupported preset values are rejected during config validation rather than at agent execution time.
title: integration: enforce supported agent_runner presets in config schema

Description: Commit 4d247a6bb67e122181a1cc0f005e6edcd83afee4 closes review-finding bead hx-f2b93e92 by recording closing_commit_sha=1bc26181bae1e88cd6c29fc11f20c5904ed2ae8a. As recorded now, hx-f2b93e92 replays against unrelated bead-close code instead of representing a metadata-only repair finding.
Suggested fix: Clear hx-f2b93e92.closing_commit_sha and leave the bead closed without replay provenance, or otherwise record only the actual governed implementation boundary if a non-metadata repair commit exists and is documented.

acceptance: In a non-git working directory, a review bead that already has closing_commit_sha=1234567890abcdef1234567890abcdef12345678 keeps that exact value after 'ddx bead close <id>'; metadata-only review closes in git repos still clear only positively identified '.ddx/beads.jsonl'-only backfill SHAs; and bead close acceptance tests cover both the non-git preserve path and the tracker-only clear path.
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: cli/cmd/bead.go:150
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: Commit 93ac319 narrows metadata-only review-close cleanup by checking whether the existing `closing_commit_sha` points at a tracker-only backfill before deleting it, but `commitIsMetadataOnlyTrackerBackfill` treats any `git show` failure as metadata-only (`return true`). Reproduced against the checked-out source by building `/tmp/ddx-review` from `cli/`, running it in a temp directory with `DDX_BEAD_DIR` and no git repo, creating a review bead, setting `closing_commit_sha=1234567890abcdef1234567890abcdef12345678`, and then closing the bead. The bead closes successfully but the stored `closing_commit_sha` disappears. That regresses FEAT-019 replay/audit semantics for portable/manual review workflows: when git metadata is unavailable, a valid previously recorded implementation boundary is erased even though the command cannot prove it was a tracker-only backfill.
Suggested fix: Make `commitIsMetadataOnlyTrackerBackfill` fail open for provenance preservation when commit inspection is unavailable, or otherwise only clear an existing `closing_commit_sha` after positively proving the referenced commit is a `.ddx/beads.jsonl`-only backfill; add regression coverage for closing a review bead with a pre-existing full SHA in a non-git workspace.
title: correctness: preserve existing review provenance when git lookup is unavailable

acceptance: A session row with cost_usd=0 is reported with cost_state=known, contributes to known-cost counts instead of estimated-cost counts, the -1 sentinel still maps to unknown, and regression tests cover zero-cost + sentinel cases
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: cli/internal/processmetrics/processmetrics.go:1083
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: `sessionCostState` and `sessionDerivedCost` treat any non-positive `cost_usd` as not-known by checking `sess.CostUSD > 0`. SD-016 explicitly says known-zero values are valid and must remain `cost_state=known`; only the `-1` sentinel means unknown. Repro: from `cli/`, a temporary fixture with one correlated session using model `qwen/qwen3-coder-30b` and `cost_usd=0` prints `state=estimated cost=0` from `processmetrics.New(dir).Cost(...)` instead of `state=known cost=0`.
Suggested fix: Treat `cost_usd >= 0` as known, handle the `-1` sentinel explicitly, and add regression coverage for zero-cost local sessions in both cost rows and summary counters.
title: correctness: classify zero-cost sessions as known in process metrics
Review issue

acceptance: Given a local plugin manifest sets install.root.target under `~/`, when `ddx install --local` runs inside a project, then both the global installed root and `.ddx/plugins/<name>` exist and project-relative plugin resource paths resolve
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: cli/cmd/install.go:244
Category: integration
Severity: medium
Description: Registry installs explicitly create `.ddx/plugins/<name>` as a project-local symlink when the manifest root target is global (`~/...`) so project-relative plugin paths still resolve. The new manifest-driven local install path does not mirror that behavior: it creates the global symlink only. Repro: a local plugin whose manifest sets `install.root.target: ~/.ddx/plugins/sample-plugin` produces `global_plugin_exists=yes`, `project_plugin_exists=no`, and still installs skills. Any code or docs that resolve plugin resources through `.ddx/plugins/<name>/...` now break in local developer installs.
Suggested fix: When `installLocal` honors a global root target, also create the same project-local `.ddx/plugins/<name>` symlink that `InstallPackage` creates for registry installs.
title: integration: keep project-local plugin path for local installs with global roots

acceptance: SD-015 defines a stable model-level attribution key for routing metrics so RoutingOutcome, QuotaSnapshot, and any derived burn/performance summaries can be associated with the resolved candidate model or canonical target; FEAT-014 stays consistent with that key; and the docs no longer imply that latency/cost/reliability from different models on the same surface are mixed into one routing signal
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-015-agent-routing-and-catalog-resolution.md:175
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The new minimal-routing schema records `RoutingOutcome`, `QuotaSnapshot`, and `BurnSummary` only by `harness` and `surface`. Earlier in the same design, however, each `CandidatePlan` carries `CanonicalTarget`, `ConcreteModel`, `EstimatedCostUSD`, and `PerformanceMetrics` because routing decisions can differ materially by resolved model or exact pin. A single surface can host very different models with different latency, reliability, and cost characteristics. If DDx aggregates outcome and burn data only at the harness/surface level, runs for one model will contaminate routing signals for another, making model-specific scoring and exact-pin behavior incorrect.
Suggested fix: Extend the minimal routing-metrics schema with a model-identifying dimension such as `canonical_target` or `concrete_model` (or explicitly define an equivalent normalization key), and update FEAT-014/SD-015 so performance, cost, and burn inputs remain attributable to the resolved model rather than only the surface.
title: correctness: keep routing metrics model-specific

description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: .ddx/beads.jsonl:4
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: Commit 81b620f4cc94d9740f7353042893c1a6c47b76a5 updates the ddx-5448b39c row to add spec-id=FEAT-006, but the same committed row still carries closing_commit_sha=not-a-sha. FEAT-004 documents closing_commit_sha as repairable tracker provenance and FEAT-019 replay/audit consume it as a durable commit boundary; leaving a non-commit literal in the touched row makes the bead metadata internally inconsistent and can break any tooling that expects a resolvable git object.
Suggested fix: Repair ddx-5448b39c through ddx bead update so closing_commit_sha is either removed or replaced with the actual full commit SHA for the design landing, rather than leaving the malformed value not-a-sha in tracker storage.
title: correctness: repair malformed closing provenance on ddx-5448b39c

acceptance: With git.auto_commit=always, auto-committing a nested relative path succeeds without a git pathspec error, and a regression test covers a relative-path caller such as ddx doc stamp or AutoCommit itself
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: cli/internal/git/autocommit.go:65
Category: integration
Severity: medium
Description: AutoCommit now changes the git working directory to filepath.Dir(filePath) but still runs git add with the original filePath. That works for absolute paths like .ddx/beads.jsonl in bead close, but it breaks existing relative nested-path callers. For example, from /Users/erik/Projects/ddx, running `cd cli && git add -n cli/cmd/bead.go` fails with `fatal: pathspec 'cli/cmd/bead.go' did not match any files`, because the path is interpreted relative to the new cwd as cli/cli/cmd/bead.go. cli/cmd/doc.go passes relative document paths into AutoCommit, so doc stamping auto-commit will now fail for nested docs paths.\nSuggested fix: Normalize filePath before staging when AutoCommit changes directories. Either resolve filePath to an absolute path and convert it to a repo-root-relative path for `git add`, or keep the cwd at the repo root and only change it for repo detection/rev-parse.
title: integration: preserve relative-path auto-commit callers

description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: .ddx/beads.jsonl:5
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: Commit c97a722fe4621449d8c8f60524302d63b2406e6c closes ddx-5751d1a2 against the actual implementation commit a5b8d2f0971cc4b9f99cdde438824261485f1655, but it records only the 7-character prefix `a5b8d2f` in closing_commit_sha. FEAT-019 and the bead close acceptance tests treat closing_commit_sha as durable replay and audit provenance; agent replay resolves it with `git rev-parse <sha>^`, so the short prefix works only while it stays unique. As repository history grows, that prefix can become ambiguous or stop resolving, which would break later replay or audit runs for ddx-5751d1a2.
Suggested fix: Rewrite ddx-5751d1a2.closing_commit_sha to the full 40-character commit SHA `a5b8d2f0971cc4b9f99cdde438824261485f1655`, and keep manual close/provenance normalization aligned on exact object IDs rather than abbreviated prefixes.
title: correctness: store full closing_commit_sha for ddx-5751d1a2
Close provenance

acceptance: ddx bead show ddx-1210706d --json no longer reports closing_commit_sha=9653820049db7edebe0374431544b1b8a8dbae88, and the provenance field used by FEAT-019 replay is documented or tested to identify the original governed-work diff rather than a later metadata-only tracker commit
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: .ddx/beads.jsonl:1
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: Commit 34b87b9 repoints ddx-1210706d.closing_commit_sha to 9653820049db7edebe0374431544b1b8a8dbae88 because that SHA is a pure tracker-only commit. But FEAT-019 replay uses closing_commit_sha as the original commit boundary: it checks out the parent of that SHA and compares against that commit's diff. For ddx-1210706d, that means replay now uses e51936a as the base state but compares against 9653820's one-line metadata diff, so the replay/audit surface no longer represents the actual tracker-governance work that bead governed, including the concerns.md taxonomy change mentioned in the bead acceptance. The last-commit fix removes the mixed-commit problem but replaces it with a metadata-only provenance target that cannot reconstruct the original governed-work diff.
Suggested fix: Either clear ddx-1210706d.closing_commit_sha so replay falls back to sessionless/manual semantics for this backfilled bead, or introduce an explicitly documented and tested provenance model that distinguishes implementation diff provenance from later tracker-close metadata commits before storing a pure metadata SHA in closing_commit_sha.
title: correctness: stop ddx-1210706d replay provenance from pointing at metadata-only commit

acceptance: `cd cli && DDX_BEAD_DIR=$(mktemp -d)/.ddx go test ./cmd/... -run TestBeadCommandsUnset.*` passes, and `ddx bead update <id> --unset events` either fails or leaves `ddx bead evidence list <id> --json` unchanged for a bead with existing evidence
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: cli/cmd/bead.go:373
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The new generic `ddx bead update --unset` path deletes any key from `b.Extra`, but append-only execution evidence is also stored in `b.Extra["events"]`. In a temp bead store, `go run . bead evidence add <id> ...` followed by `go run . bead update <id> --unset events` makes `ddx bead evidence list <id> --json` return `[]`, so this patch gives normal metadata updates a data-loss path that can erase audit evidence and violate the append-only contract of `ddx bead evidence`.
Suggested fix: Restrict `--unset` to an allowlist of removable provenance fields such as `closing_commit_sha`, or explicitly reject protected keys like `events`/`session_id` and add a regression test proving evidence cannot be removed through `bead update`.
title: correctness: prevent bead update --unset from erasing evidence

acceptance: A supported `ddx bead` workflow exists to repair closing_commit_sha on closed beads, and provenance backfills like ddx-5751d1a2 no longer require direct .ddx/beads.jsonl row edits.
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: .ddx/beads.jsonl:5
Category: drift
Severity: medium
Description: Commit 21e90117937e19ea62579328f27fd608714ccc7f repairs ddx-5751d1a2 by directly rewriting the managed row in .ddx/beads.jsonl. AGENTS.md explicitly forbids manual edits to bead storage and requires agents to use `ddx bead` subcommands or stop and ask if the CLI cannot perform the operation. FEAT-004 and SD-004 also define tracker mutations around DDx-controlled snapshot writes and atomic rename semantics. Landing this provenance repair as a hand-edited JSONL row bypasses the supported mutation path and normalizes exactly the storage-edit workflow the repo policy is trying to prevent.
Suggested fix: Add or use a supported `ddx bead` command path to repair `closing_commit_sha` on closed beads, then reapply this provenance repair through that command flow instead of a manual .ddx/beads.jsonl edit.
title: drift: stop hand-editing DDx-managed bead storage for provenance repairs

acceptance: SD-016 states that cost_usd=-1 means unknown rather than known cost, direct cost aggregation excludes the sentinel value, and the cost_state / cost precedence rules align with FEAT-014 and FEAT-006 sentinel semantics
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-016-process-metrics.md:191
Category: correctness
Severity: high
Description: SD-016 defines `cost_state=known` whenever a session row reports `cost_usd` and says bead lifecycle cost should use `session.cost_usd` whenever it is present. But FEAT-014 and FEAT-006 explicitly define `cost_usd=-1` as the sentinel for unknown cost, not a real price. Implementing SD-016 as written would treat unknown-cost sessions as known values, producing negative dollar totals and skipping the estimation/unknown fallback for a core FEAT-016 metric.
Suggested fix: Update SD-016 so `cost_usd=-1` is mapped to `cost_state=unknown` and excluded from direct cost sums, with estimation or unknown fallback applied only to non-sentinel values.
title: correctness: treat cost_usd=-1 as unknown in SD-016

acceptance: ddx bead show hx-010a50cf --json omits closing_commit_sha, and closing a metadata-only review bead through the supported ddx CLI no longer stamps the prior HEAD or unrelated metadata commit as closing provenance when no new close commit lands
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: .ddx/beads.jsonl:27
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: During this last-commit review, the supported `ddx bead close hx-010a50cf` flow closed a metadata-only `action:review` bead and persisted `closing_commit_sha=d20a24fd8ff635481f19c934e8643472ed0a7cde` on the new tracker row. That SHA is the previously reviewed provenance-repair commit, not a close commit created by the review workflow, and FEAT-019 explicitly says tracker-only backfills should omit `closing_commit_sha` rather than point replay at an unrelated metadata or earlier implementation boundary. The repo source and tests still indicate review beads should suppress this field, so the durable tracker state produced by the current supported CLI path remains misleading.
Suggested fix: Make the supported bead-close path omit `closing_commit_sha` for metadata-only review closes when no landed close commit exists, add regression coverage for that no-new-commit path, and repair hx-010a50cf so its closing provenance is absent rather than pointing at d20a24f.
title: correctness: metadata-only review close still stamps prior HEAD provenance

acceptance: With git.auto_commit=always in a git repo and DDX_BEAD_DIR unset, running 'ddx bead close <id>' leaves 'git status --short' clean, and 'git show HEAD -- .ddx/beads.jsonl' includes the bead row with closing_commit_sha equal to 'git rev-parse HEAD' rather than requiring an extra uncommitted worktree update
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: cli/cmd/bead.go:518
Category: integration
Severity: medium
Description: The new close flow writes bead status first, auto-commits .ddx/beads.jsonl, and only then writes closing_commit_sha back to the store. In a clean temp git repo with git.auto_commit=always and DDX_BEAD_DIR unset, running 'ddx bead close <id>' leaves 'git status --short' dirty with ' M .ddx/beads.jsonl'. The landed HEAD commit 9494afc82408cac9a650d616fc3207197e1dfbd6 contains only the status=open->closed change, while the durable closing_commit_sha appears only in the uncommitted worktree diff. That means the command still fails to land close provenance atomically and leaves replay/audit metadata outside the recorded close commit.
Suggested fix: Restructure bead close so the recorded closing_commit_sha is present in the same landed commit that closes the bead, or perform and persist a second deterministic commit/update so git status is clean after close and HEAD contains the final provenance-bearing row.
title: integration: persist close provenance in the landed tracker commit

acceptance: Given installed.yaml contains both plugin and resource entries, when `ddx doctor --plugins` runs, then it emits plugin_validation findings only for plugin-like entries and no false `package.yaml` findings for resources
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: cli/cmd/doctor.go:452
Category: integration
Severity: high
Description: `checkInstalledPlugins` iterates every row in installed state without filtering by package type, even though `InstalledEntry` records resource installs as well as plugins. A minimal repro with an installed `resource` entry (`persona/example`) makes `ddx doctor --plugins` emit `/tmp/.../persona.md/package.yaml: open ...: not a directory` and `missing package.yaml` for a non-plugin file. This breaks the plugin audit for users who have installed single resources.
Suggested fix: Restrict `ddx doctor --plugins` to package types that actually represent plugin roots (at least `plugin`, and `workflow` if workflows are intentionally audited), and skip `resource` entries entirely.
title: integration: limit doctor --plugins to plugin installs

acceptance: TP-002 either describes TC-010 as planned work only, or the repository contains the project-scoped HTTP routes /api/projects and /api/projects/:project, the MCP tools ddx_list_projects and ddx_show_project, and corresponding server tests that verify TC-010.1 through TC-010.9
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: docs/helix/03-test/test-plans/TP-002-server-web-ui.md:157
Category: drift
Severity: medium
Description: TC-010 now says the project-scoped routing and MCP registry checks are implemented in `cli/internal/server/server_test.go`, but the same table keeps every TC-010 case in Planned status and the current server/test code still exposes only legacy `/api/...` routes plus MCP tools such as `ddx_list_beads` and `ddx_show_bead`. There are no `/api/projects` routes, no `ddx_list_projects` or `ddx_show_project` tools, and no matching tests. The updated sentence therefore misstates current coverage and makes the governing test plan disagree with the code it references.
Suggested fix: Either change TP-002 to describe TC-010 as planned future coverage, or implement the scoped `/api/projects...` surfaces, MCP registry tools, and matching tests before claiming those checks are already implemented.
title: drift: TP-002 overstates scoped-routing and MCP test coverage

acceptance: The frontend CI job uses Bun tooling and bun.lock for dependency installation and Playwright execution, and .github/workflows/ci.yml no longer depends on cli/internal/server/frontend/package-lock.json or npm commands for the embedded frontend path
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: .github/workflows/ci.yml:156
Category: drift
Severity: medium
Description: The new frontend-e2e job sets up Node, keys its cache from `cli/internal/server/frontend/package-lock.json`, and runs `npm ci`, `npx playwright install`, and `npm run test:e2e`. The active `typescript-bun` concern and TP-002 declare Bun as the frontend package manager and script runner, with `bun.lock` as the authoritative lockfile and `bunx`/`bun run` as the expected commands. Wiring this job to npm cements a second dependency-resolution path in CI that can drift from the declared Bun toolchain and from local/frontend build behavior.
Suggested fix: Switch the job to `oven-sh/setup-bun`, cache/install from `cli/internal/server/frontend/bun.lock`, and run the Playwright install/tests with `bunx` and `bun run` so CI matches the active concern contract.
title: drift: run embedded frontend CI with Bun practices

acceptance: architecture.md and SD-019 define the same fatal-vs-recoverable registry error policy, including explicit duplicate-id behavior, and TP-002 reflects the chosen behavior in planned or implemented coverage
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: docs/helix/02-design/architecture.md:223
Category: integration
Severity: medium
Description: The reviewed docs disagree about how invalid project registries behave. `architecture.md` says a project-registry mismatch should fail only the affected project entry and keep siblings available, explicitly naming duplicate ids as one such case. SD-019 instead says duplicate project ids are a startup/configuration error and that an invalid registry should fail fast before serving partial context. Those are incompatible operator and implementation contracts for one of the key multi-project isolation paths.
Suggested fix: Split registry errors into recoverable per-entry failures versus fatal registry-shape errors, then align architecture.md, SD-019, and any related tests around the chosen policy. Duplicate ids should have one unambiguous outcome.
title: integration: align duplicate-project-id failure policy
